Struck by Qadir cruise missile. Hull damage above waterline, remained navigable and proceeded to Al Jubail.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Hit by projectile

VESSEL_ATTACKS.vessel: USS Frank E. Peterson / USS Michael Murphy
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Claimed attacked by Iranian cruise missiles during mine-clearing operation; denied by US Navy.

No vessel attacks on Day 45. However, US blockade enforcement begins at 10am ET. CENTCOM forces boarding and inspecting vessels in international waters that transited under IRGC toll regime. 320+ vessels remain at anchor. Tension extremely elevated but no kinetic incidents in first hours of blockade.
Vessel struck by a sea-skimming drone during nighttime transit; no oil spill reported and crew evacuated to safe room.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.loc: 112 nm SE of Ras Al Hadd, Oman
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Vessel struck by two unidentified projectiles causing fire on board; situation stabilized.
Chinese-owned tanker initially turned back from US blockade, then successfully passed through.
U.S.-sanctioned methanol carrier intercepted by U.S. blockade and redirected.
Tanker heading to UAE successfully transited as it was not bound for Iran.
Empty cargo ship departed Bandar Imam Khomeini and exited the Gulf.
Vessel boarded and diverted to Iranian waters
Minor damage to hull from drone strike

VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Minor structural damage from UAV, fire extinguished

VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Vessel struck by IRGC aerial drone
Hezbollah claimed its fighters struck an Israeli military vessel off the coast of Lebanon.
Indian refiners are reportedly refusing to discharge its cargo due to the risk of secondary U.S. sanctions.
IRGC gunboats opened fire on the Indian-flagged tanker. It was one of two Indian vessels targeted, leading to a diplomatic protest.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Struck by projectile, damage to containers, vessel operational
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Hit by an unknown projectile; UKMTO reported damage to several containers, but no fires or environmental impact.
Vessel reported a 'splash' in close proximity to the ship; no damage reported.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Fire extinguished, vessel under tow to safe anchorage.
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Struck by unidentified loitering munition 50nm east of Fujairah.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.loc: Gulf of Oman, ~55 nm ESE of Sur
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: IRGC Navy fast-boats shadowed vessel for ~40 minutes before breaking off on CENTCOM helicopter arrival. Master reported no boarding attempt.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.loc: Strait of Hormuz, ~22 nm SSE of Larak
VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Boarding interdicted by USN escort; vessel cleared to transit south.
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: IRGC Navy fast-boat element approached southbound VLCC at speed; aborted on USN P-8 vector and CG escort interposition. Master reported brief comms jamming. No damage, no casualties.
USS Spruance disabled and seized vessel after it bypassed blockade. U.S. forces fired into the engine room.
Intercepted by USS Spruance, engine room fired upon, seized by US Marines.

USS Mason issued bridge-to-bridge warnings and fired warning flares after an IRGC FIAC element approached escort convoy to within 800 yards. Craft disengaged; no exchange of fire. Incident logged by 5th Fleet as provocation consistent with prior ceasefire-era pattern.
Vessel struck by suspected drone debris; small fire quickly extinguished by crew.
Slight damage from suspected drone strike, fire extinguished.
Vessel struck by loitering munition while waiting for naval escort.
Targeted and subsequently seized by Iranian forces; currently anchored in Iranian waters.
Attacked by Iranian gunboat; bridge sustained significant damage from gunfire and RPGs before being escorted to Iranian coast.
Fired upon by IRGC forces and became stranded on the Iranian coast.
No new vessel attacks at run cutoff. Elevated risk per IRGC 23-Apr statement; US Navy Task Force 55 maintains blockade screen 50nm east of Strait chokepoint.
No new vessel attacks at run cutoff; elevated risk per IRGC 25-Apr statement following Round 2 collapse. US Navy Task Force 55 maintains blockade screen.

UKMTO advisory: Aframax product tanker reported suspicious fast-craft approach 30nm SE of Larak Island; vessel maneuvered, no boarding or weapons fire. US Navy Task Force 55 redirected ISR overhead. Reported to UAE coastguard for follow-up.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.loc: Near Jask Island, Strait of Hormuz approach
VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Two ASCMs reportedly hit; vessel forced to turn back per IRGC; US/CENTCOM responses pending
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: IRGC/Fars: two anti-ship cruise missiles fired from Iranian shore battery near Jask Island struck a US Navy vessel after warnings to halt were ignored; ship 'unable to continue route' and turned back. CENTCOM acknowledges 'engagement event' but does not confirm hits or damage as of 0600Z; Pentagon spokesman 'comprehensive assessment in progress'. Maj. Gen. Ali Abdollahi (Khatam al-Anbiya HQ) reiterates 'any foreign armed force entering Strait of Hormuz will be subjected to attack.' Iranian parliament National Security Commission chair Azizi declares Project Freedom a 'ceasefire violation.'

VESSEL_ATTACKS.vessel: Iranian small boats (6-7 craft) near Hormuz
VESSEL_ATTACKS.loc: Strait of Hormuz, vicinity of US Navy escort group
VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: US destroyer and naval helicopters destroyed 6-7 Iranian small boats per CENTCOM after the boats attempted to interfere with Project Freedom escort of two US-flagged merchant vessels through Hormuz; Trump publicly stated 'seven small boats' shot down. No US/escorted casualties reported.
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: First confirmed direct US-Iran combat losses since pre-ceasefire kinetic phase. CENTCOM PAO confirms two US-flagged merchant vessels successfully transited Strait of Hormuz under escort. IRGC announces 'martyrdom' of unspecified personnel.
VESSEL_ATTACKS.source: CENTCOM PAO, Trump Truth Social, IRGC statement, Reuters

VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Successful escorted transit eastbound through Hormuz under Project Freedom escort (1 of 2 US-flag vessels confirmed transited overnight); no engagements affecting hull
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: First publicly named US-flag merchant transit under Project Freedom escort framework; underwriter terms set at +4.0% war-risk premium with US Navy escort discount of -0.6%
Struck by fast attack craft swarm and suicide drone.
Direct hit by loitering munition on accommodation block.
Struck by loitering munition while approaching the strait, minor damage to superstructure, proceeded under own power.
Inbound drone intercepted by coalition frigate nearby; vessel continued voyage unharmed.

VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Fire in engine room, cargo intact
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Missile strike caused a fire in the engine room; crew extinguished blaze, cargo remained intact but required towing.
Disabled by US CENTCOM for non-compliance with the naval blockade
Vessel struck by uncrewed surface vessel, minor damage sustained
Detained by IRGC for allegedly attempting to transit without authorization or disabled tracking.
Detained by IRGC for allegedly attempting to transit without authorization or disabled tracking.
Targeted by 5 Iranian one-way attack drones, which were intercepted by US CENTCOM.
Targeted by land-attack cruise missile, intercepted by coalition naval forces

VESSEL_ATTACKS.outcome: Minor damage from drone strike, proceeding to port
VESSEL_ATTACKS.detail: Struck by loitering munition 50nm off Muscat; superficial structural damage reported

INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: 7 of 12 IG P&I Clubs issued war-risk cancellation notices effective midnight Mar 5. Covered Iranian waters and full Gulf region.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: JWC emergency session — Listed Areas expanded
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Lloyd's JWC expanded to full Gulf: Bahrain, Djibouti, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar added. 48-hr cancellation clause invoked. Neil Roberts, JWC Sec., confirmed.
Mitsui Sumitomo halted all new war-risk underwriting near Iran, Israel and neighboring nations.
Coverage excluded from Gulf and Iranian waters as of midnight Mar 5. AWRP for standard MR tanker: $40,000/7-day HRA stay; younger MRs $80-120k/7 days.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Fitch: credit negative for US P&C insurers
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Fitch flagged credit-negative for US P&C insurers with Gulf marine war exposure. Jefferies: $1.75bn losses from ~7 vessels as of Mar 5.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Lloyd's List: double-digit millions per Hormuz trip
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Lloyd's underwriter: 10% minimum for VLCC. Hypothetical 5-yr VLCC on US charter: $10-14m per Hormuz voyage. Historical Tanker War peak was 5%.
US International DFC committed $20bn rolling reinsurance covering hull, machinery war risk and cargo for Gulf transit.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Lloyd's CEO: cover must stay available
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Lloyd's CEO Tiernan: critical cover stays available. LMA: ~1,000 vessels in Gulf worth >$25bn. Cover case-by-case at ~5% of vessel value.
Coverage costs reached 5% of ship value, 5x early war levels

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Chubb Named Main US Gulf Insurer
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: $20B plan with DFC to help Persian Gulf shipping resume
Over 1,000% increase since conflict began. Rates for transiting Gulf are 1%-3% of a vessel's hull value.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Major P&I clubs excluded Iranian and adjacent waters
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Gard, Skuld, and NorthStandard formally excluded these waters from standard war-risk policies.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: U.S. Government intervention in insurance market
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: President Trump ordered DFC to provide political risk insurance and guarantees at subsidized rates to ensure continued energy flow, as private insurers retreat.
War-risk insurance premiums reached 3.5% to 10% of vessel value. Many underwriters have completely withdrawn appetite for the Persian Gulf region.
Major syndicates withdraw hull coverage for Persian Gulf transit.
Additional premiums announced for Red Sea approaches.
Premiums for Gulf transits quoted as high as 7.5% of hull value. Cargo insurance ranges 1% to 2.5%.
Lloyd's Syndicates and P&I Clubs issuing 7-day cancellation notices for Gulf transits, effectively making some US/Israel-tied vessels uninsurable.
Lloyd's syndicates raise Gulf of Oman premiums to 8.5%
Major insurers pause new quotes for Persian Gulf transit

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: War Risk Premium Spike and Cancellations
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: War-risk premiums spiked to 3.5%-10% of vessel value. Major insurers (Gard, Skuld, London P&I Club) issued cancellation notices for war risk coverage in the Gulf.
War risk premiums for Persian Gulf transits surged to 1-3%, up from 0.1-0.25% pre-conflict.
Iran targeting ADCOP pipeline to prevent bypassing Hormuz.
Many insurers have issued notices of cancellation to renegotiate terms, and some have withdrawn from the region entirely, though coverage remains technically available.
DFC directed to provide political risk insurance to stabilize trade in the Gulf.
Western underwriters withdrawing automatic coverage for Persian Gulf; JWC expands Listed Areas. Cargo war risk up to 10-20%.
Premiums for Hormuz transits surged from under 1% to 3.5%-10% of vessel hull value, costing tens of millions per voyage.
Many P&I clubs struggling to secure reinsurance, leaving some vessels unable to obtain coverage for Hormuz crossings.
The Trump administration doubled its maritime insurance guarantee to $40 billion through the International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) to lure ships back.
JWC maintains Hormuz as absolute exclusion zone. No quotes provided for any Persian Gulf ports.
U.S. government doubled reinsurance guarantees for ships transiting the Strait to $40 billion, joined by AIG, Berkshire Hathaway, and Travelers.
Major syndicates at Lloyd's announce complete suspension of new policies for vessels entering the Arabian Sea north of 15°N.
Three major London P&I clubs issue 72-hour cancellation notices for Gulf war-risk.
Reinsurers halt backing for any vessel not paying the unofficial Iranian 'toll'.
The U.S. Development Finance Corporation backed a $20 billion reinsurance facility to stabilize the market. Commercial insurers still avoiding the Upper Gulf.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Transit fee proposed and selective uninsurability
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: Iran considering up to $2 million transit fee per ship for safe passage. Selective uninsurability for US/Israeli linked vessels continues.
Lloyd's List reports insurers are wary that reimbursing safe passage tolls paid to Iran could violate sanctions due to the IRGC being a designated terrorist organization.
U.S. DFC deployed a $20 billion Maritime Reinsurance program to stabilize the market.
phase: 3. NorthStandard added a specialized 'Iran Conflict layer' to its GlobeView platform for real-time threat assessments.
phase: 3. U.S. government doubled its Gulf reinsurance program to $40 billion to provide a safety net for underwriters.
Premiums surge; insurers completely withdraw coverage for vessels with US or Israel ties.
U.S. International Development Finance Corporation launched a $20 billion maritime reinsurance plan to provide coverage for U.S. and allied shipping that commercial insurers refuse to cover.
7 of 12 International Group P&I Clubs issued 72-hour cancellation notices for war-risk coverage in the Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman.
India establishes $1.5 billion sovereign guarantee fund to support its insurers. US offers TRIA coverage to encourage energy shipping.
Major insurers (Gard, Skuld, NorthStandard) cancelled war risk cover for the Gulf; premiums surged to 1% of ship value where available.

US naval blockade adds second layer of restriction atop IRGC toll regime. Lloyd's market expected to further widen exclusion zones. War risk premiums could reach 10-15% of hull value for any Gulf transit. Reinsurers pulling back from Middle East marine cover.
Lloyd's Market Association Joint War Committee expanded the listed area to encompass the broader Arabian Sea down to 10 degrees North.
War-risk insurance premiums softened to 0.35%-0.45% during the ceasefire but remain highly volatile following the US naval blockade order for the Strait of Hormuz.
Major reinsurers adopted a 'no-market' stance due to the blockade and mine risks, prompting carriers like Hapag-Lloyd to formally refuse passage.
War risk premiums for transiting the Strait of Hormuz surged to 3.0%-5.0% due to US Naval blockade and seizure risks.
Premiums reached up to 10% of vessel value. Insurers issuing Notice of Cancellation to hike rates.
Carriers implement war risk surcharges up to $3,000 per 40ft container for Middle East destinations.
Shipowners invoking London Blocking and Trapping Addendum due to US naval blockade and trapping in ports.
US government providing up to $40 billion in reinsurance guarantees to support neutral shipping avoiding Iranian ports.
Lloyd's market increased war risk premiums for Gulf of Oman transits to 3.5% of hull value.
Major syndicates freeze all remaining active war risk policies for vessels near Hormuz
U.S. government steps in with $40B in reinsurance guarantees for neutral vessels
The US International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) provides up to $40 billion in reinsurance guarantees to encourage shipping, limited primarily to US-linked insurers.
All 12 members of International Group of P&I Clubs maintain cancellation of war risk extensions.
Underwriters expand the war risk surcharge zone to include all of the Arabian Sea following recent escalating drone threats.
U.S. International Development Finance Corporation established a $40 billion reinsurance facility to provide political risk insurance for ships aligned with geoeconomic priorities.
IRGC reportedly charging an ad hoc toll of up to $2 million per ship for alleged 'mine clearance' in northerly transit lanes near Larak Island.
The Joint War Committee (JWC) of the London insurance market has expanded the 'Listed Areas' for hull war, piracy, and terrorism to include the entire Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Gulf.
A consortium of London insurers led by Beazley launched a $1 billion extra war risk facility to provide additional capacity for vessels transiting the high-risk zone.
Indian government approved the 'BMI pool' with a sovereign guarantee of ₹12,980 crore (approx. $1.55 billion) to provide affordable hull, cargo, and war risk insurance.

INSURANCE_EVENTS.event: Launch of $1 billion marine war consortium
INSURANCE_EVENTS.detail: A $1 billion marine war consortium led by Beazley was launched at Lloyd’s to provide specialized capacity for vessels attempting to resume Hormuz transits.
Major syndicates systematically suspend quoting new coverage for vessels entering the Persian Gulf.
Key reinsurers trigger 48-hour cancellation clauses for Middle East block policies citing unquantifiable risk.

The Joint War Committee (JWC) expanded Listed Areas to include Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, and the UAE. Almost any movement in the region now requires specialized, high-cost notification and additional premiums despite the recent ceasefire.
Global reinsurers exclude Strait of Hormuz from collateral lists. Domestic companies citing inability to move ships.
Premiums up to 3-7.5% of hull value (10% for US-linked ships). $200M tanker insurance jumped to nearly $10M per voyage.

DFC announces second tranche ($18B additional capacity) now underwriting tanker, container, and bulker war-risk cover for third-country vessels transiting JMIC convoys. Premium-to-hull ratios down to 5.5% (from 5.8% Apr 20). US-linked ships remain at ~10%. Tanker insurance for a $200M VLCC now quoting ~$9.2M per voyage (vs ~$10M Apr 20).

DFC announces second tranche ($18B additional capacity) now underwriting tanker, container, and bulker war-risk cover for third-country vessels transiting JMIC convoys. Premium-to-hull ratios down to 5.5% (from 5.8% Apr 20). US-linked ships remain at ~10%. Tanker insurance for a $200M VLCC now quoting ~$9.2M per voyage (vs ~$10M Apr 20).
London insurance market underwriters maintain maximum war risk premiums for all Gulf and Red Sea transits.
Lloyd's Market Association expands the designated High Risk Area further into the Arabian Sea.
Major syndicates refuse to underwrite calls to smaller regional ports outside protected zones.
Rates have surged to 1%–5% of a ship's total hull value per transit in the Gulf. Major insurers issued notices of cancellation for standard cover.
Iran reportedly began charging 'safe corridor fees' of approximately $1 million to $2 million per ship for vessels allowed to pass.
JWC extends high-risk area to cover entire Gulf of Oman and northern Arabian Sea.
Major syndicates issue 7-day notice of cancellation for vessels lacking naval escort.
Joint War Committee expands high-risk Listed Areas further into the Arabian Sea to longitude 65°E, increasing affected voyage zones.
Major syndicates begin refusing underwriting for vessels with direct Israeli or US ownership operating east of Fujairah.
Sanctions placed on over 50 companies and 19 specific vessels involved in Iranian petroleum shipments
Iran unveiled its own maritime insurance program to provide coverage for vessels transiting the strait under Iranian oversight
Joint War Committee expands listed areas further into the Arabian Sea
US government reportedly considering a $20 billion reinsurance program to revive shipping through the region.
War-risk premiums for the Persian Gulf skyrocketed to 3%-5% of vessel value amid ongoing blockades.
JWC maintains entire Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman as highest risk category for hull war, piracy, and terrorism

CONTAINER_RATES.note: CMA CGM $3,000/FEU emergency surcharge. Most carriers suspended Gulf bookings.
+$485/FEU (+20%) since conflict onset. Hapag-Lloyd $70-75/TEU surcharge eff. ~Mar 23.
+$583/FEU (+16%). Most impacted route after Dubai direct.

CONTAINER_RATES.route: Asia to US West Coast FBX01
CONTAINER_RATES.note: +$191/FEU (+10%). Diverted Atlantic Basin demand.
+19% WoW week of Mar 12. Source: Trans.INFO/Drewry Mar 14.
Rates spiked due to war surcharges and diversions
1% weekly increase, global fuel surcharges applied
Spot rates jumped 26% in the last month. 147 container ships sheltering in Arabian Gulf.
Rates rose 22%. 147 container ships sheltering in Arabian Gulf.
Rates plateau as rerouting becomes standard.
Early April spot rates hit $2,200-$2,700 (20-25% increase). Middle East/Red Sea route war surcharges spiked by nearly 200%.
Middle East surcharges driving rates up by nearly 200%. Far East to Europe up 20-25%.
Rates surged to over $7,000 per FEU by late March.
Jumped 12% in late March due to conflict and rerouting.
Major shipping firms continue Hormuz suspension. Iran selective reopening: China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, Thailand, Philippines flagged ships allowed.
Major carriers (MSC, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd) implement Emergency Fuel Surcharges of $60 to $190 per TEU.
119 container vessels (inc. 17 ULCVs) in holding pattern; port call frequency collapsed by 50%.
Rates surged to $3,500 by early April.
Rates reached $6,500 - $7,000 by early April.

CONTAINER_RATES.route: Far East to US West Coast
CONTAINER_RATES.note: Spot rates reached $2,430/FEU by early April (up 29%).
Spot rates heading toward $6,000 per FEU, an increase of over 270%.
MSC introduced $800 surcharge per container; CMA CGM imposed $3,000 per 40ft war-risk surcharge.
Rates to the region spiked by nearly 200% over the last month; carriers applied emergency fees up to $3,000/FEU.
Rates up roughly 40% to $2,645/FEU.
Rates spiked nearly 200%. War Risk Surcharges reach $3,000 per FEU.
Average spot rates heading toward $6,000 per FEU, an increase of over 270% compared to late February.
Rates climbed to approximately $2,430 per FEU, representing a 29-37% increase since the start of the conflict.
Rates climb again amid the $1M-$2M Iranian safe corridor fee
Approximately 119 container ships, including 17 ULCVs, remain in holding patterns or loitering inside the Arabian Gulf, unable to safely exit.
Spot rates surged toward $6,000 per FEU, a 270% increase compared to late February levels, as reported by Xeneta mid-April.
Rates rose 37% by mid-April due to 'ripple effect' congestion at major Asian transshipment hubs like Singapore and Port Klang.
Drewry WCI reported spot rates between $3,552 and $3,671 per FEU as of mid-April.

CONTAINER_RATES.route: Far East to US East Coast
CONTAINER_RATES.note: Spot rates for 40-foot containers reached approximately $3,382 in early April.
Rates plateauing after consecutive weekly increases following Cape of Good Hope rerouting.
May Phase 3 rates eclipsed March peaks, topping $7,500 per FEU due to renewed blockades.
Prices jumped to nearly $4,400 per FEU in May.
Rates stabilizing at high levels as Cape of Good Hope routing becomes permanent standard for Q2 2026

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: All 6 GCC states closed airspace. Emirates, Qatar Airways suspended.
US tanker set ablaze at Mina Salman, Bahrain.
UAE's main non-Hormuz port. Traffic reduced 40% by drone incidents.
Indian crew member killed. Tanker struck off Muscat.
Partial commercial reopening. Emirates resumed limited schedule.
DXB back to ~60% capacity — then suspended again on Day 11.
9 of 35 drones penetrated UAE defenses. Flights grounded again.
Primary Gulf ship bunkering suspended. Global shipping costs spike.
Entire airline fleet moved to Saudi after Muharraq depot fire.
Bahrain GP cancelled — estimated $400M economic loss.
Fire from debris. Hub serves 15% of global ship refueling.
Both Hormuz and Red Sea effectively closed

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: IRGC Navy prevented three container ships from transiting Strait of Hormuz
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Disruption of major shipping route, forced vessels to turn around.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Saint Kitts and Nevis-flagged container ship SELEN expelled from Strait of Hormuz
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Disruption of shipping, highlighted new requirements for 'mandatory permits'.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Container ship from earlier attack remained aflame
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Persistent danger to abandoned or damaged hulls in the region.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: MV Pacific Zircon struck by explosive USV off Bandar Abbas
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: 3rd confirmed tanker attack in Strait since Jan 2026. VLCC freight rates surged 15%. US Navy escorted 4 tankers through Strait on Mar 30.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: War-risk premiums reach 3.5-7.5% of hull value; 150+ vessels diverted or anchored
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: $9M insurance cost per transit for $300M tanker. Crew safety concerns now primary barrier to transit, not insurance availability.
Iran demanding fees from non-hostile vessels (Chinese, Turkish, Pakistani) permitted to pass Strait of Hormuz.
Shipping industry absorbing an additional €340 million ($370 million) per day in fuel costs globally due to rerouting.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Hormuz 98% blocked. 150+ tankers anchored. IRGC fast attack craft fire on tankers. Khor Fakkan port targeted.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Largest oil supply disruption in history. SPR drawdowns accelerating.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iran FM announces safe passage through Hormuz for 2 weeks if vessels coordinate with Iranian armed forces. 8 tankers transited Monday (up from <2/day in March). War-risk insurance still at 5-10% hull value. $2M Iranian transit fee per vessel proposed. US $20B reinsurance program active.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Ceasefire Agreement for Strait of Hormuz
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: U.S. and Iran reached a tentative two-week ceasefire to allow safe passage and extraction of approximately 800 vessels trapped in the Persian Gulf.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: 2,000 ships and 20,000 seafarers stranded. Major carriers like Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd maintain suspension of normal operations.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Global supply chain delays, 8 million bpd regional crude stranded

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: HORMUZ RE-CLOSED. Iran halts safe passage commitment after Israeli Lebanon strikes. Only a handful of vessels transited Apr 8 before closure. Tehran demands crypto tolls from ship owners. Lloyd's List reports tanker traffic remains near zero. Red Sea alternative route congested.

HORMUZ RE-CLOSED. Iran halts safe passage after Israeli Lebanon strikes. Only handful of vessels transited Apr 8 before closure. Tehran demands crypto tolls. Tanker traffic near zero. Red Sea alternative congested.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Most Western-linked tonnage continues rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope until the security situation is fully verified.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Most Western-linked tonnage continues rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope until the security situation is fully verified.
Brent crude plummeted to $92-94/bbl following ceasefire, but 250,000 TEU capacity remains displaced.
Iran demands tolls up to $2M for safe passage; ceasefire breaches reported including a pipeline attack.

Only 2 tankers transited Hormuz since ceasefire. Iran conditioning passage on toll payments up to $2M. NEC Director Hassett says even one tanker is a 'big deal.' 13M bpd of production remains shut in across Middle East. Ship owners unwilling to transit without clear safe passage guarantees.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Iran Traffic Separation Scheme and Tolls
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: phase: 3. Iran requires vessels to coordinate directly with IRGC Navy. Reports of an unofficial 'Tehran toll booth' demanding up to $2M per transit.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Iran charging $1M-$2M per ship for passage via Larak Island. 2,000-3,200 vessels stranded west of Hormuz.
Forced routing north of Larak Island
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: The IRGC issued a directive requiring all vessels to transit through Iranian territorial waters, specifically north of Larak Island, to avoid alleged naval mines.
Reports indicate Iran is attempting to institutionalize a toll-collection regime, proposing fees as high as $2 million per transit. The U.S. issued direct warnings against the practice.
The Suez Canal Authority introduced 15% discounts for large container ships in an attempt to lure traffic back amid widespread diversions around the Cape of Good Hope.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Hormuz transit remains near-zero — only 2 tankers since ceasefire. Iran demanding permission/toll ($1M+) for passage. IMO reports ~2,000 ships stranded including 230 loaded tankers. Insurance premiums remain at war-risk levels.

Hormuz transit remains near-zero — only 2 tankers since ceasefire. Iran demanding permission and toll ($1M+) for passage. IMO reports ~2,000 ships stranded including 230 loaded tankers. Insurance premiums remain at war-risk levels.

Hormuz transit remains near-zero — only 2 tankers since ceasefire. Iran demanding permission and toll (1M+) for passage. IMO reports ~2,000 ships stranded including 230 loaded tankers. Insurance premiums remain at war-risk levels.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Kuwait officially accused Iran and its proxies of launching drone attacks against vital facilities on Thursday evening.
U.S. Navy destroyers entered the Strait of Hormuz for mine clearance. Iran condemned the action as a ceasefire violation and threatened to attack.
Iran is demanding pre-approval and charging safe passage 'tolls' exceeding $1 million per ship, establishing a de facto commercial blockade.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Hormuz transits remain near-zero. ~800 freighters stuck in Persian Gulf holding pattern. Pre-war average ~130-135 ships/day reduced by ~95%. IRGC controls Northern Corridor for limited civilian passages only. Iran drafting transit fee law ($2M per vessel). US DFC offering $40B revolving reinsurance for hull/cargo/liability.

Hormuz transits near-zero. ~800 freighters stuck in Gulf holding pattern. Pre-war ~130-135 ships/day, now reduced ~95%. IRGC controls Northern Corridor for limited civilian passages. Iran drafting transit fee law ($2M/vessel). US DFC $40B revolving reinsurance for hull/cargo/liability active.
Hormuz near-zero transits. ~800 freighters stuck. Pre-war 130+ ships/day reduced ~95%. IRGC Northern Corridor limited. Iran transit fee law drafted. DFC 40B reinsurance active.
Hormuz near-zero transits. ~800 freighters stuck. Pre-war 130+ ships/day reduced ~95%. IRGC Northern Corridor limited. Iran transit fee law drafted. DFC $40B reinsurance active.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iran has begun charging tolls of over $1 million per ship for passage through the Strait of Hormuz.
USS Frank E. Peterson and USS Michael Murphy transited Strait of Hormuz to clear mines.
U.S. Navy ordered total blockade of Strait of Hormuz to interdict vessels paying Iranian protection fees.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Transit dropped 90%, 1,000 ships in holding pattern.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: CENTCOM forces begin implementing blockade of all maritime traffic entering/exiting Iranian ports at 10am ET per presidential proclamation. US Navy to intercept vessels in international waters that paid tolls to Iran. 320+ vessels already at anchor awaiting transit. Second layer of restriction atop IRGC toll regime.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: U.S. Naval Blockade of Strait of Hormuz
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: U.S. Navy ordered to clear mines and block Iranian oil exports following the collapse of peace talks in Islamabad.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: US blockade enforcement begins; Hormuz transits at historic lows
SHIPPING_EVENTS.details: CENTCOM enforces blockade on all vessels entering/exiting Iranian ports starting 10 AM ET. Non-Iranian port transits through Hormuz permitted. Windward data: only 17 vessels crossed Hormuz on Apr 12 (down from ~130 daily pre-war). Lloyd's List: shipping 'immediately halted' after blockade announcement. Tanker diversions to Oman's Sohar and Duqm ports increasing. Saudi East-West pipeline at max capacity.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: U.S. Central Command initiated and enforced a formal blockade of all vessels entering or leaving Iranian ports in the region.
Over 730 cargo and tanker vessels remain trapped or anchored in the Gulf, unable to transit due to the blockade and lack of insurance.
US Naval blockade of Iranian ports goes into effect. Traffic drops drastically with only two vessels exiting the Strait of Hormuz.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: US naval blockade Day 3; Hormuz transit down >90%
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: US blockade targets ships calling at Iranian ports. At least 3 vessels including 2 US-sanctioned tankers transited Hormuz on first full day. JMIC Bahrain reports daily traffic down >90%. Iran calls blockade 'piracy.' UK-led 40-nation coalition planning freedom of navigation operations. IEA characterizes as largest supply disruption in oil market history.
CENTCOM reports 100% interdiction rate redirecting merchant vessels from Iranian ports.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Six merchant vessels attempting to leave or enter Iranian ports intercepted and forced to turn back.
Dark fleet tankers attempting transits using AIS spoofing forced to return to Iranian ports by US Navy.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: USS Spruance Redirects Iranian Cargo Vessel
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Iranian-flagged cargo vessel attempting to evade US blockade after leaving Bandar Abbas was intercepted and redirected by guided-missile destroyer USS Spruance (DDG-111). Separate tanker observed departing Iranian port despite blockade per MarineTraffic data. CENTCOM states non-Iranian port traffic unimpeded.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Three major shipping lines paused all Hormuz transits pending security review.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Salalah and Jebel Ali report maximum capacity, vessels forced to anchor offshore

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz officially drops by more than 90 percent
Iran reportedly begins charging a $2 million 'safe corridor fee' per ship for non-Western vessels allowed to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Further increases operational costs for non-Western vessels (Turkish, Indian, Chinese-flagged) transiting the Strait.
Summit to discuss a multinational plan to restore freedom of navigation.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: France and the UK co-chaired a 40-nation summit to discuss a multinational plan to restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, envisioning a defensive mission involving mine-clearing vessels and naval escorts.
U.S. issued an ultimatum to Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours or face further strikes on critical infrastructure, which Iran rejected.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Several major naval escorts suspended operations temporarily due to increased mine risks, stranding 30+ tankers.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Daily traffic through the Strait of Hormuz reduced from ~140 ships to fewer than 15 due to U.S. blockade and Iranian transit restrictions.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Severe reduction in global shipping lane capacity.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Over 150 vessels loitering or anchored near the Strait of Hormuz, hesitant to transit due to blockade and insurance cancellations.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Major supply chain delays and congestion outside the Gulf.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: U.S. CENTCOM reported that at least 19 vessels attempting to enter or depart Iranian ports were intercepted and forced to turn around.

Iranian Foreign Minister declared the Strait open, but the IRGC and Parliament Speaker immediately countermanded this, stating the waterway remains closed to any vessel related to belligerent countries.
Iran officially announced the re-closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to the continued U.S. naval blockade, effectively ending the ceasefire at sea.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Transit blocked for most commercial traffic
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iran reversed its decision to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, citing the continuation of a U.S. naval blockade on its ports, returning control to its previous blocked state.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Market confusion and delayed transit resumptions.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Iranian Foreign Minister announced the strait was open, but IRGC imposed strict conditions and effectively maintained the blockade.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Total paralysis of commercial shipping in the region.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: IRGC warns that any vessel approaching the strait will be considered as cooperating with the enemy and targeted.

CENTCOM confirms 3 tankers turned back Apr 18-19 under blockade protocols; JMIC Bahrain logs 4 safe southbound transits with escort. Araghchi statement on 'Hormuz open' contradicted by on-water reality.

Joint Maritime Information Center logs 7 safe southbound transits and 2 northbound (cleared cargoes) under blockade convoy regime in 24h window. CENTCOM flags one near-miss boarding (MT Amber Pacific, see VESSEL_ATTACKS). No transit denials issued for third-country flagged tonnage carrying non-Iranian cargo.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Asia-EU containers via Cape adding ~14 days vs Suez baseline
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Maersk and CMA CGM extend Cape rerouting on Asia-Europe loops through May. Container spot rates Shanghai-Rotterdam hold at ~$5,800/FEU (+118% YoY). Linerex bulker rerouting now ~28% of Gulf-origin cargoes.

Joint Maritime Information Center logs 7 safe southbound transits and 2 northbound (cleared cargoes) under blockade convoy regime in 24h window. CENTCOM flags one near-miss boarding (MT Amber Pacific, see VESSEL_ATTACKS). No transit denials issued for third-country flagged tonnage carrying non-Iranian cargo.
First seizure of an Iranian-flagged vessel by the U.S. Navy during the conflict.
Iran attempting to formalize control by demanding $2 million USD per vessel for priority transit through Strait of Hormuz.
Transit dropped from 130 to 3 vessels per day. Military analysts estimate clearing mines for safe commercial navigation could take six months.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: JMIC Convoy Intake Resumes at +7 Vessels
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Joint Maritime Information Center convoy intake recovers to 7 vessels in 24h window (vs 3 in prior 12h on Apr 20) as escort mix adds FGS Hamburg and HMS Duncan to US destroyers. Fujairah anchorage wait time down to ~11h.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Mandatory combined maritime forces escorts are causing a significant 4-day transit backlog at key chokepoints.

Combined Maritime Forces escort queue wait times at Hormuz chokepoint continue to ease as coordination patterns mature; backlog estimated ~3 days vs the 4-day peak on Apr 21. Queue wait times for Fujairah bunker pickups near 9-10 hours (-1 to -2h vs prior day). Traffic throughput still elevated-risk vs pre-war baseline; continuation entry pending UKMTO advisory refresh.
Zero commercial transits through Strait of Hormuz recorded for the past 48 hours.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Severe delays in regional supply chains
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Naval escorts grouping vessels for transit through lower risk zones is causing massive staging delays.

Hormuz closure enters Day 11. Zero commercial transits through the Strait of Hormuz recorded for the prior 24-hour period. Cape of Good Hope rerouting remains the default backhaul path for VLCCs. Continuation entry pending IMO bulletin.
Approximately 2,000 ships and 20,000 mariners remain stranded within the Persian Gulf due to the strait closure and U.S. blockade.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Brent crude peaks at $126/barrel; global supply chain crisis.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: AIS aggregators (Kpler, MarineTraffic) report Strait of Hormuz transit volume holding ~5% of pre-war 178-vessel/day baseline. CENTCOM Apr 30 readout: 42 vessels redirected since Apr 13 blockade; 41 Iranian-affiliated tankers carrying ~69M bbl held off Bandar Abbas/Kharg/Jask. Cape of Good Hope reroute adds 14-18 days transit Asia-Europe. Source: Kpler, CENTCOM, MarineTraffic.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Hormuz traffic remains -95% vs pre-war; Cape reroute saturating
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: AIS aggregators (Kpler, MarineTraffic) report Strait of Hormuz transit volume holding ~5% of pre-war 178-vessel/day baseline. CENTCOM May 1 readout: 44 vessels redirected since Apr 13 blockade; 41 Iranian-affiliated tankers carrying ~69M bbl held off Bandar Abbas/Kharg/Jask. Cape of Good Hope reroute adds 14-18 days transit Asia-Europe; Suez/Bab al-Mandeb southbound transits +12% w/w as cargo shifts off Hormuz arbitrage. Source: Kpler, CENTCOM, MarineTraffic.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Hormuz traffic ~5% pre-war; CENTCOM blockade tally 48 Iranian vessels turned around in 20 days
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: AIS aggregators (Kpler, MarineTraffic) confirm Strait of Hormuz transit volume holding ~5% of pre-war 178-vessel/day baseline. CENTCOM May 2 PAO readout: 48 Iranian-affiliated vessels turned around since Apr 13 blockade onset (running 20 days); 41 Iranian-affiliated tankers carrying ~69M bbl held off Bandar Abbas/Kharg/Jask awaiting waiver. Cape of Good Hope reroute adding 14-18 days transit Asia-Europe; Suez/Bab al-Mandeb southbound transits +13% w/w. UNCTAD notes first signs of LNG-carrier tonnage shortage on Cape route as binding constraint independent of days-at-sea. Source: Kpler, CENTCOM, MarineTraffic, UNCTAD.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Project Freedom escort mission begins; Hormuz traffic falls to ~3% pre-war
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: CENTCOM kickoff Project Freedom 0500Z: destroyers, 100+ aircraft, unmanned platforms, ~15,000 personnel committed to escort neutral-flag commercial shipping out of Strait of Hormuz. AIS aggregators (Kpler, MarineTraffic) report Hormuz transit volume falls to ~5 vessels/24h (~3% of pre-war 178/day baseline) on Iranian threats and underwriter pull-back. CENTCOM May 4 readout: 50 vessels redirected since Apr 13 blockade; 41 Iranian-affiliated tankers (~69M bbl) held off Bandar Abbas/Kharg/Jask. Cape of Good Hope reroute saturating; Suez/Bab al-Mandeb southbound transits +18% w/w. Source: Kpler, CENTCOM, MarineTraffic, ABC, Times of Israel.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Project Freedom Day 2 — first US-flag merchant pair completes Hormuz transit; Iranian small-boat swarm destroyed
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: CENTCOM confirms two US-flagged merchant vessels (one Suezmax, one product tanker) completed eastbound Hormuz transit overnight under armed escort by USS Mason (DDG-87) and helicopter overwatch. Iranian IRGC Navy small-boat swarm of 6-7 craft destroyed during attempted interdiction; one Iranian cruise missile and several UAVs intercepted by escort group SM-2/RAM. Hormuz aggregate traffic Day 67 estimated 15-20 vessels/24h (~3-4% pre-war), of which ~6 under US escort.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.source: CENTCOM PAO, MARAD, Reuters, Lloyd's List

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: IMO confirms ~20,000 seafarers stranded on ~2,000 vessels in/around Hormuz
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: International Maritime Organization Secretary-General issued public statement on humanitarian dimension; renewed call for safe passage corridors. UAE/Oman maritime authorities coordinating welfare logistics.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Two US-flag merchants completed second escorted Hormuz transit overnight before pause announcement
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Maersk-operated US-flag MV Liberty Star and MV Pacific Sentinel completed eastbound transit at ~0630Z under USS Mason DDG-87 escort; no incidents. Trump pause announcement followed at ~1900Z.

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: IMO confirms approximately 20,000 seafarers stranded on circa 2,000 vessels in/around Hormuz (Day 68)
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: International Maritime Organization Secretary-General issued public statement on humanitarian dimension; renewed call for safe passage corridors. UAE/Oman maritime authorities coordinating welfare logistics. Number unchanged from prior reporting — pause has not yet translated to vessel release.
SHIPPING_EVENTS.impact: Humanitarian / logistical bottleneck persists despite pause

SHIPPING_EVENTS.event: Two US-flag merchants completed second escorted Hormuz transit overnight before Project Freedom pause
SHIPPING_EVENTS.detail: Maersk-operated US-flag MV Liberty Star and MV Pacific Sentinel completed eastbound transit at ~0630Z under USS Mason DDG-87 escort; no incidents. Trump pause announcement followed at ~1900Z effectively suspending further escorted transits.
UAE and US 5th Fleet mandate convoy formations for all liquid bulk transits in the Gulf.
Vessel backlog at Jebel Ali reaches 14 days due to enhanced naval inspections and reduced pilot availability.
Maersk and MSC announce total rerouting of all Middle East bound cargo via Cape of Good Hope, officially suspending Persian Gulf calls.
Port of Fujairah reports a massive 40% drop in vessel calls for bunkering as ships entirely avoid the region due to resumed hostilities.
Coalition naval forces initiate a structured convoy system for essential energy shipments transiting the Strait of Hormuz to mitigate attacks.
CENTCOM reported redirecting 109 commercial ships and disabling 4 for non-compliance
Reports of unofficial MoU framework to restore commercial shipping in 30 days if U.S. lifts blockade
Naval escorts for commercial vessels reduced due to increased operational tempo elsewhere
US and Iran negotiating a 60-day MoU for unrestricted shipping and mine removal in exchange for lifting the US naval blockade.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: Day 31 record salvo (47 BMs) presented as 'all 47 struck targets without interception'
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: UAE MoD confirmed 39 of 47 intercepted. 8 leakers tracked — 3 sea-falls, 5 territory impacts (no casualties).

IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: IDF assessment: 60-70% of pre-war targets destroyed. US intelligence confirms only one-third of drone fleet destroyed. Partial truth used for propaganda framing.

IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: Videos identified as AI-generated content designed to mock Western leaders and boost domestic morale.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: US aircraft carrier severely damaged and sinking in Persian Gulf.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC claims 'full control' of Strait of Hormuz, warns of 'deadly vortex'
IRAN_DISINFO.assessment: Partially false. IRGC naval presence in strait is degraded after coalition strikes on fast-attack craft and coastal batteries. US CENTCOM confirms non-Iranian port transits continue. IRGC overstates operational control to deter blockade enforcement.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC claims to have sunk a US Arleigh Burke-class destroyer in the Persian Gulf.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC shot down a U.S. warplane and captured the pilot; AI-generated videos depicted successful missile strikes on U.S. warships.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: AFP confirmed videos were old clips from Libya and a 2025 incident in Pakistan.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC shot down an F-35 Lightning II near Qeshm Island.
IRAN_DISINFO.refutation: CENTCOM confirmed all U.S. aircraft accounted for; IRGC made this false claim at least six times.

IRAN_DISINFO.claim: IRGC warns of 'unrevealed military capabilities far beyond the enemy's imagination' if conflict resumes

Fuel rationing is real but claim of famine is inflated.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Strikes on Yazd Missile Base and industrial targets
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Combined forces targeted the deeply buried underground complex in Yazd, marking the 6th strike on the site. Additional strikes hit the Marine Industries Organization (MIO), Parchin Military Complex in Tehran, and the Iran Marine Industrial Company (SADRA) in Bushehr.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple strategic and industrial facilities successfully struck.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Strikes on Imam Ali and Dezful Missile Bases
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Heavy strikes on the Imam Ali Missile Base using bunker-buster munitions. A separate strike targeted bulldozers attempting to clear debris at the Dezful Missile Base.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 9 aboveground structures and 2 tunnel entrances destroyed at Imam Ali; heavy equipment destroyed at Dezful to deny tunnel access.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Extensive strikes on Imam Hussein University (IRGC's military academy) and ballistic missile/UAV production facilities in Tehran. A petrochemical plant in Tabriz was also struck.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Facilities degraded; widespread power outages in Tehran and Alborz provinces; IRGC Navy Commander and Tehran security commander eliminated.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: A new wave of U.S.-Israeli strikes hit northern and eastern Tehran.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: CENTCOM confirms 10,000+ targets struck; Tehran court/MoI destroyed
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM Adm. Cooper confirms US has struck 10,000+ targets. IDF hit Tehran Revolutionary Court, Ministry of Intelligence (10 sites), Quds Force HQ. Strikes reported in 26 of 31 Iranian provinces. UK hosting Hormuz diplomatic conference.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 10,000+ total US targets; IDF struck thousands more. Tehran institutions systematically degraded.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Extensive wave of attacks targeted regime sites in Tehran, including areas near the former U.S. Embassy compound. CENTCOM released an operational update confirming over 12,300 targets struck since the campaign began.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple targets successfully engaged; operations continue.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Campaign enters its second month, maintaining unrelenting firepower with B-2 stealth bombers and F-35s targeting IRGC headquarters, missile depots, and air defense systems.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.targets: IRGC headquarters, missile depots, air defense systems
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Ongoing degradation of Iranian defense industrial capacity.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM reported over 12,300 targets hit and 155 Iranian vessels destroyed since start of campaign. Ultimatum issued to reopen Strait of Hormuz by April 6.
Extensive wave of strikes conducted by IAF against regime sites and the former U.S. Embassy compound.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: US-Israel strikes continue. Trump: US to hit Iran 'extremely hard' for 2-3 weeks. IDF planning 3 more weeks of operations to degrade Iran defense industry. B1 bridge in Karaj struck twice (2 civilian KIA). Iran steel plants remain offline (6-12mo recovery). Pasteur Institute extensively damaged.

US-Israel strikes continue. Trump: US to hit Iran extremely hard for 2-3 weeks. IDF planning 3 more weeks of operations to degrade Iran defense industry. B1 bridge in Karaj struck twice (2 civilian KIA). Iran steel plants remain offline (6-12mo recovery). Pasteur Institute extensively damaged.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Massive fireball observed, facility heavily damaged

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Ongoing air campaign against hardened targets

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Bridge struck; Iranian state media reported at least 8 deaths and 95 injuries.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Coalition airstrikes on IRGC-N coastal defense cruise missile sites

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Significant disruption of localized air defense network.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: U.S. forces strike Isfahan nuclear site
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. forces targeted Isfahan, home to one of Iran's primary nuclear sites, as part of the campaign's final stages.
Extensive waves of strikes across Iran including the destruction of the B1 (Bileghan) Bridge connecting Tehran to Karaj to disrupt missile transport.
Elimination of Makram Atimi, commander of a central ballistic missile unit in Kermanshah, and several battalion commanders.
Airstrike hit near the Bushehr nuclear facility, killing a security guard and damaging a support building.
Israel expanded operations by striking Hezbollah infrastructure in Beirut, including the destruction of a bridge in eastern Lebanon.
B-52 bombers conducted long-range precision strikes focusing on destroying Iran's remaining naval and missile production assets.
Three U.S. aircraft (F-15E, A-10, MQ-9) lost to hostile ground fire over Iran and Iraq. A Combat Search and Rescue operation recovered one F-15E crew member; the WSO remains missing.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Loss of 3 aircraft; 1 crew member missing in action

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Iran claimed to have downed an American C-130 transport plane and two Black Hawk helicopters in southern Isfahan during a U.S. rescue mission.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Claimed loss of U.S. C-130 and two Black Hawks.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. intelligence confirmed the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in a targeted strike on his compound during the operation's initial phases.
Pentagon added Operation Epic Fury to its casualty database, reporting 13 U.S. service members killed and 365 wounded.
Multiple U.S. aircraft (F-15E, A-10, MQ-9) lost or damaged due to hostile fire from Iraqi-based ground forces.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Loss of F-15E, A-10, and MQ-9 aircraft.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Precision strike on IRGC UAV assembly facility
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US and coalition forces conducted precision airstrikes against an IRGC drone assembly plant to degrade regional threat capabilities.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Facility rendered inoperable, destroying multiple UAVs in production.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Final strikes on Iranian oil facilities
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Final US/coalition strikes reported on oil facilities on Lavan and Siri islands on the day the ceasefire was implemented.
The Lavan Oil Refinery on Lavan Island, Iran, was struck causing a major fire, occurring concurrently with the announcement of a fragile ceasefire.
A temporary two-week ceasefire, brokered by Pakistan, officially took effect at 2:02 a.m. Tehran time. Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Ceasefire implemented; commercial vessels resume transiting Strait of Hormuz.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: The ceasefire holds despite high tensions. Diplomatic teams prepare for negotiations in Islamabad scheduled for April 10.
The United States, Israel, and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire. Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and enter negotiations regarding its nuclear program.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Hostilities paused; Strait of Hormuz reopened.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper announced the conclusion of the operation, stating that core strategic objectives were achieved and Iran's projection capabilities neutralized.
The ceasefire remains in place. U.S. forces maintain an air defense umbrella alongside regional partners while broader peace negotiations continue.

President Trump and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth declared 'total and complete victory', stating the campaign shattered Iran's ballistic missile capabilities and air defenses. Trump warned of renewed strikes if Iran fails to comply with terms.
Vice President JD Vance travels to Islamabad, Pakistan to begin formal negotiations aimed at a permanent peace accord. Iran's President Pezeshkian warns 'hands remain on the trigger'.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Peace Talks Collapse & Naval Blockade Ordered
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Islamabad peace talks officially collapsed after 21 hours. President Trump ordered a full naval blockade of all Iranian ports.
A full naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Arabian Gulf and Gulf of Oman went into effect at 10:00 AM ET. Major U.S. airstrikes in Iran paused.
The IDF launched fresh strikes against Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon.
A U.S.-led naval blockade of all Iranian ports went into effect at 5:30 p.m. Iranian time to stop oil exports and imports, enforced by CENTCOM from the Gulf of Oman.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Blockade active; large-scale airstrikes paused due to a strained two-week ceasefire.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Operation Epic Fury transitions from active bombardment to a strategic maritime blockade. CENTCOM begins enforcing a full blockade of all Iranian ports and coastal areas to prevent maritime traffic and oil exports.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Blockade active; major airstrikes remain paused under fragile ceasefire.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: U.S. officially launched 'Operation Economic Fury', an escalation of financial sanctions accompanied by a naval blockade in the Persian Gulf.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Strict control of maritime traffic in the Persian Gulf established; ceasefire holds.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Potential nuclear agreement and regional truce
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: President announced U.S. is very close to a deal with Iran to hand over its enriched uranium. A 10-day humanitarian truce was also brokered between Israel and Lebanon.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Pending nuclear agreement; 10-day Israel-Lebanon truce initiated.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US and coalition forces conducted targeted strikes against newly identified air defense batteries and drone launch sites.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Precision strike on IRGC missile facilities
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition forces conducted precision airstrikes targeting underground ballistic missile storage and launch sites in western Iran.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Multiple secondary explosions confirmed; facility rendered non-operational.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: ISR tempo holds; no new kinetic strikes Day 53
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM J3 reports no Epic Fury kinetic strikes during ceasefire Day 14. ISR tempo remains elevated: 24h on-station for RC-135, MQ-9, and P-8A packages. Coverage concentration on Kermanshah, Yazd, and Bandar Abbas. Target folders kept warm pending ceasefire expiration (Apr 22).

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.event: Coalition airstrikes on drone launch facilities
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US and coalition forces conducted precision strikes against unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) storage and launch sites.
U.S. announced an indefinite extension of the temporary ceasefire following diplomatic intervention by Pakistan.
IRGC seized container ships MSC Francesca and Epaminondas, and attacked the Euphoria in response to U.S. blockade.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: 2 ships seized, 1 attacked; diplomatic talks stalled

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: CENTCOM reported that coalition forces have directed a total of 31 vessels to turn around or return to port to prevent export of Iranian oil and import of military supplies.
U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) reported destroying six Iranian small boats that attempted to interfere with commercial transit.
US and coalition forces resumed strike operations following the conclusion of the D49-D65 ceasefire period.
Targeted strikes against Iranian UAV launch facilities to degrade offensive capabilities.
Near-miss UAV/missile event in Salalah; 2 foreign nationals wounded by debris. Oman MoFA: 'most likely off-axis Iranian munition.' Under investigation.
Sorties focused on air defense degradation.
Combined air and naval strikes targeting logistics networks supporting Iranian coastal operations.
F-15E strike eagles targeted early warning radar facilities to open an air corridor.
Tomahawk cruise missiles launched from naval assets hit IRGC fast attack craft pens.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Heavy damage to piers and docked vessels

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Precision standoff munitions struck unmanned aerial vehicle assembly lines.
CENTCOM announces strikes on coastal air defense sites.
Pentagon briefing details precision strikes against underground facilities.
Precision strike on identified missile assembly facility.
Interception of multiple hostile UAVs over the Persian Gulf.
Suppression of coastal air defense radar systems.
U.S. Central Command forces struck a ground control station and downed several Iranian attack drones after Iranian forces fired on commercial vessels.
IRGC targeted an American airbase in Kuwait with missiles and drones. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted several of these threats.
U.S. struck a ground control station in the southern port of Bandar Abbas following Iranian drone attacks on commercial ships.
Iran launched a ballistic missile and drone attack targeting a U.S. airbase in Kuwait; intercepted by Kuwaiti air defenses.
Coalition aircraft conducted a precision strike on an IRGC UAV storage and assembly facility.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Target destroyed; multiple secondary explosions observed.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: US Navy guided-missile destroyer intercepted an anti-ship ballistic missile launched toward the Persian Gulf.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Intercept successful; no damage to coalition assets or commercial traffic.

EPICFURY_TIMELINE.detail: Coalition forces targeted early warning radar installations and surface-to-air missile batteries.
EPICFURY_TIMELINE.outcome: Radar systems disabled; SAM battery sustained heavy damage.

EPICFURY_BDA.postStrikeAssessment: Struck for the sixth time since the operation began.
9 aboveground structures and 2 tunnel entrances destroyed.

Struck, contributing to widespread regional power outages.
Struck by new wave of U.S.-Israeli airstrikes

EPICFURY_BDA.assessment: IAF has air supremacy in most Iranian airspace. 100+ air defense systems and 120+ detection systems destroyed. 75% of Iran TEL fleet destroyed. Isfahan steel plants forced offline. IDF has 'thousands more targets' in Tehran.

EPICFURY_BDA.postStrikeAssessment: Annihilated; 10 major ships confirmed sunk, 150+ vessels out of action
Under ongoing bombardment by GBU-57 MOPs; damage assessment pending

EPICFURY_BDA.postStrikeAssessment: Destroyed, massive cave-in of primary tunnel networks

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.detail: United States: Pause Project Freedom escort missions while retaining blockade. Intent: Test whether pause produces verified Iranian fire cessation and unlocks signable agreement; preserve military leverage via blockade if pause collapses. Indicator: Trump Truth Social post; Pakistan/Saudi/UAE/Bahrain coordinated request acknowledged.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.move: Pause Project Freedom escort missions while retaining blockade
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.intent: Test pause-then-agreement sequencing; preserve leverage via blockade

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Pezeshkian 'utter madness' quote against IRGC
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.detail: Iran (Pezeshkian-Araghchi axis): Public rebuke of IRGC; sustain Beijing/Wang Yi diplomatic track; review US response to 14-point plan. Intent: Reassert civilian/MoFA primacy over IRGC rogue operations; signal to mediators (Pakistan/Oman/China) that political track has Tehran governmental backing. Indicator: Pezeshkian 'utter madness' quote; Wang Yi-Araghchi Beijing meeting.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.move: Public rebuke of IRGC; sustain Beijing diplomatic track
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.intent: Reassert civilian primacy; signal political-track backing

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Khatam al-Anbiya joint command denial of UAE strikes
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.detail: Iran (IRGC / Khatam al-Anbiya): Deny UAE strike attribution while threatening 'crushing response.' Intent: Preserve plausible deniability on rogue UAE action while maintaining deterrent posture; avoid public split with Pezeshkian government. Indicator: Khatam al-Anbiya official statement contradicting Pezeshkian within same news cycle.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.move: Deny UAE strikes while threatening crushing response

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Wang Yi-Araghchi Beijing meeting; 'political solution' framing
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.detail: China: Wang Yi-Araghchi Beijing meeting; reaffirm 'political solution.' Intent: Insert China as guarantor partner of any agreement; counter US-only mediation narrative; secure Iranian energy supply lines. Indicator: MFA China readout; Araghchi 'political crisis cannot be resolved through military means' quote.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.intent: Insert China as guarantor partner; secure energy lines

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Near-miss UAV/missile event in Salalah; 2 foreign nationals wounded by debris.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Confirmed ceasefire violation. Most likely off-axis Iranian munition, shattering Omani neutrality.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Oman MoFA confirms 2 foreign nationals wounded; event under investigation.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Iran reviewing US peace proposal but prioritizing control of Strait of Hormuz and missile reconstitution.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran using negotiations to secure legitimate control of the Strait of Hormuz and funds to rebuild missile sites.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Negotiating posture driven by IRGC Commander Ahmad Vahidi.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Saudi Arabia pressured US to halt 'Project Freedom' naval operation after 36 hours.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Regional powers actively constraining US military options to prevent structural erosion of global order.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Halt of Project Freedom operation following Saudi pressure.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Failure of overwhelming US force to compel regime collapse forces recalibration of Gulf alignments.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Pentagon rhetoric failing to produce political outcomes without a post-conflict framework.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Limits of US power highlighted by lack of regime collapse despite military strikes.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: China's strategic calculus shifting due to disruption of global petroleum and seaborne fertilizer shipments.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: War has put the US on a weaker footing vis-a-vis China, which is exploiting US strategic distraction.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Impending food security crisis and disruption of 20% of global petroleum.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Resumption of high-value targeting
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran's leadership has authorized the targeting of key infrastructure, abandoning the previous restraint observed during the ceasefire.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Increased drone swarm attacks on regional infrastructure noted in ISW daily update.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Diplomatic back-channel communication
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Despite active hostilities, low-level diplomatic channels remain open via Oman to prevent broader regional spillover.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Crisis Group reports ongoing quiet talks in Muscat between secondary officials.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Deployment of advanced ballistic missiles
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Introduction of new medium-range ballistic missile systems indicates a shift towards a prolonged attrition strategy.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: FDD analysis of satellite imagery showing deployment of mobile launchers to western provinces.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Rhetorical softening on specific demands
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iranian state media has reduced emphasis on immediate withdrawal of regional forces, possibly signaling openness to localized pauses.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Carnegie analysis of Supreme National Security Council press releases.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Reconstitution of IRGC missile command structures
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran is preparing for a sustained attrition campaign and prioritizing the survivability of its strategic strike assets.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Relocation of key command and control nodes to hardened subterranean facilities following the breakdown of the Oman ceasefire.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Omani backchannel diplomatic freeze
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Diplomatic off-ramps are currently inaccessible, increasing the likelihood of protracted military engagements.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Muscat has paused mediation efforts, citing a lack of credible de-escalation commitments from Tehran after the Day 68 ceasefire violation.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Moderation in northern proxy deployment
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran seeks to preserve Lebanese Hezbollah's strategic deterrence capabilities rather than commit them to the current active operations phase.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Observed reduction in offensive posturing and cross-border shelling by Hezbollah units, despite the resumption of broad hostilities.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Nuclear enrichment facility lockdown
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Tehran is fortifying critical infrastructure against anticipated deep-penetration pre-emptive strikes by adversarial forces.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Movement of advanced centrifuge cascades deeper into the Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant and deployment of additional point-defense systems around Natanz.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Drone strike on Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant generator
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Demonstrates precision targeting of nuclear-adjacent infrastructure, elevating risk perception for regional actors.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: IISS reports a drone strike targeting the generator supplying electricity to the Bushehr plant.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: NPT Review Conference collapse
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Diplomatic off-ramps are closing as international consensus fails specifically over the Iran nuclear file.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Carnegie reports the NPT Review Conference ended without consensus due to irreconcilable differences over Iran.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Intensification of Israeli strikes in Lebanon
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Horizontal escalation creates a strategic dilemma for Tehran regarding the survivability of Hezbollah.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Crisis Group documents intensified strikes in Nabatieh from May 22-24.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: US political endorsement of anti-Hezbollah operations
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: US rhetoric supports expanding the theater of conflict to Lebanon, reducing restraint on allied operations.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: US Secretary of State affirms Israel's right to intensify operations against Hezbollah.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: US retaliatory strikes in southern Iran
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Continuation of active kinetic operations and maritime insurgency dynamics in the Strait of Hormuz.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: US conducts fresh strikes in southern Iran following drone attacks on commercial shipping.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: IRGC Aerospace Force decentralizes command structure for mobile missile units
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Indicates preparation for sustained, dispersed active operations to mitigate the impact of command-and-control degradation.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: ISW reports shifts in Iranian command architecture allowing local commanders to initiate launches without direct central approval.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Resumption of Omani backchannel mediation focusing on target limitation
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Despite active phase operations, both sides are signaling a desire to contain the conflict by keeping strikes restricted to military targets.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Crisis Group notes diplomatic flurry via Muscat aimed at establishing red lines against civilian infrastructure strikes.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Resumption of Deep-Penetration Strikes
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran's decision to authorize deep-penetration ballistic strikes signifies an explicit departure from the D49-D65 ceasefire norms, aiming to reset deterrence at a higher escalation rung.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: MoD reports indicate successful intercepts of MRBMs targeting strategic bases, a significant shift from localized skirmishes.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.source: Institute for the Study of War (ISW)

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Diplomatic Backchannel Reactivation via Oman
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Quiet Omani mediation efforts suggest ongoing attempts to establish new red lines despite active military engagements.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Flight tracking and reporting note unscheduled Omani diplomatic flights to Tehran shortly after the resumption of strikes.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Naval Posturing in the Strait of Hormuz
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: IRGC-N exercises involving fast-attack craft swarms indicate preparation for asymmetric escalation if conventional strike exchanges intensify.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Satellite imagery confirms abnormal massing of IRGC-N assets near key maritime chokepoints.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Resumption of Deep Strikes
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iran has shifted back to a high-intensity strike posture, attempting to overwhelm regional air defenses.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: MoD reports confirm 45+ ballistic missile intercepts over coalition airspace following the ceasefire collapse.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Hardened Negotiating Stance
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Despite the active conflict, backchannel signals indicate Iran is attempting to establish a new escalation baseline rather than entering total regional war.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Crisis Group analysis of intercepted diplomatic cables and ongoing but calibrated tactical strikes.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Attrition Warfare Transition
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Iranian forces are prioritizing sustained, lower-tier drone swarms to systematically deplete coalition interceptor stockpiles.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: MoD situational updates record continuous waves of UAVs requiring Patriot and THAAD engagements.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: MoU Negotiation
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Tentative deal to extend ceasefire and lift blockades in the Strait of Hormuz.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Crisis Group reported a one-page MoU for a 60-day ceasefire has been largely negotiated.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Naval Operations in Strait
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Ongoing US-Iran military friction over control of the Strait of Hormuz.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: US Navy issued notice to challenge IRGC protection racket and force requirements for vessels.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: US Political Pressure
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: Economic and political costs pushing US administration toward finalizing a deal.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Brookings analysis noted war is a liability ahead of midterms due to high gas prices.

STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.indicator: Military Threat Posture
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.assessment: US threatening resumption of full attacks if negotiations fail.
STRATEGIC_CALCULUS.evidence: Defense Secretary Hegseth stated at Shangri-La Dialogue that US is ready to restart attacks on Iran.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: House Foreign Affairs closed session on 60-day clock and Gulf force posture.
$200B request messaging; carrier sustainment and munitions restock.
$20bn Gulf marine war-risk backstop announced (public summary).

Congress considering Iran war supplemental funding package to replenish military stockpiles. Cost estimates range from $6.2B-$13.4B for Operation Epic Fury through March 19. Pentagon reportedly requesting $200B supplemental.

War Powers Resolution votes failed in House (212-219) and Senate. Democrats criticizing lack of clarity on war justification and objectives. Senator Blumenthal expressed dissatisfaction with classified briefings.

Pentagon $200B supplemental request faces stiff opposition from Democrats and some Republicans. Rep. Lauren Boebert declared 'I am a no on any war supplemental.' Sen. Chip Roy demanding game plan details.
Trump posts on Truth Social: 'we are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great military efforts in the Middle East.'
President Trump indicated war approaching completion: 'We are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts in the Middle East'
Treasury Sec. Bessent says US has 'plenty of money' to fund Iran war but rules out tax increases. Defends recent Iran/Russian oil sanctions relief.
Trump ultimatum: Iran must open Strait of Hormuz 'completely, without threat' or US will target power plants. Warned of massive oil field strikes if Iran threatens Qatar.

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt stated that the U.S. was 'ahead of schedule' in Operation Epic Fury, originally estimated to last four to six weeks, and was 'very close' to meeting its core objectives.
President Trump echoed Leavitt's statements, describing operations as 'extremely' ahead of schedule.

White House confirmed a 15-point action list had been sent to Tehran (reportedly via Pakistani mediators) for ending the conflict. Leavitt insisted talks were 'productive' and 'continuing' despite initial Iranian rejections.
White House Press Secretary Leavitt warned President Trump was prepared to 'unleash hell' if Iran refused a deal, stating the Iranian regime had been 'defeated militarily.'
President Trump announced a delay in threatened strikes on Iran’s energy infrastructure, extending the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to April 6, citing 'very well' negotiations.
During a cabinet meeting, President Trump mentioned taking control of Iran’s oil as an 'option,' comparing it to past U.S. strategy in Venezuela.
Pentagon weighed deploying up to 10,000 additional troops to the region to provide the President with more military options, adding to existing paratroopers and Marines.
President Trump announced a 10-day extension of the pause on striking Iranian energy infrastructure, moving the deadline to April 6, 2026.

Special Envoy Steve Witkoff confirmed that the U.S. presented Iran with a 15-point peace proposal via intermediaries, including demands for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and cease nuclear enrichment.
President Trump stated that taking control of Iran’s oil remains 'an option,' though he declined to elaborate, warning Iranian leaders to agree to terms 'before it is too late.'

Military and intelligence officials held a closed-door briefing for lawmakers, where Rep. Nancy Mace (R-SC) expressed 'grave concerns' about the lack of an exit strategy and opposed the deployment of ground forces.
President Trump claimed the U.S. military had 'decimated' 99% of Iranian capabilities and that the U.S. has 'already won the war.'
President Trump confirmed that negotiations are ongoing but insisted that Iran must 'open up' the Strait of Hormuz, jokingly suggesting renaming it the 'Strait of Trump.'

The administration continues to maintain that 'talks are going very well' on social media, while simultaneously preparing for potential military escalation if the April 6 deadline passes without a breakthrough.
Leaders from U.S. Space Command and Strategic Command testified before the Senate Armed Services Committee regarding the conflict and future defense authorizations.
Reports indicated that U.S. Navy ships and submarines had fired over 850 Tomahawk missiles since the operation began on February 28, surpassing the total used in the 2003 invasion of Iraq.
President Trump posted on Truth Social that the military operation was 'going GREAT.'
Secretary of State Marco Rubio emphasized the goal of ensuring Iran never obtains a nuclear weapon.

Trump stated US could end campaign within 2-3 weeks: 'We'll be leaving very soon.' Requirement: Iran put 'into the stone ages' without nuclear capability. 'Iran doesn't have to make a deal' for US to leave.
Trump told allies to 'go get your own oil' from Hormuz, warned US 'won't be there to help.' Administration indicated willingness to end campaign even if Hormuz remains closed.
IRGC broadcast threats against Microsoft, Apple, Google, Meta via state media — accused of aiding US/Israeli strikes. Warned employees to evacuate ME facilities.
Emergency funding discussions initiated in Congress to replenish munitions expended during ongoing Operation Epic Fury strikes.
President Trump confirmed the new Iranian leadership requested a ceasefire, but stated the U.S. will not consider it until the Strait of Hormuz is free and clear.
UK hosts international conference on reopening Strait of Hormuz. Multiple GCC, EU, and Asian nations attend. Araghchi rejects talks: 'trust level at zero.' April 6 Hormuz deadline 4 days away.
CENTCOM Adm. Cooper confirms US has struck 10,000+ targets; IDF thousands more. Operations in 26 of 31 Iranian provinces. Cooper: campaign 'on plan or ahead of plan.'
