GlobalDefenseCorp reports China supplying weapons and material support to IRGC through Russia, Turkmenistan, and Pakistan. Beidou navigation receivers for Shahed drones confirmed.
US_POLICY_EVENTS.topic: China weapons transfer to Iran confirmed

US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Pentagon press briefing outlines the expansion of strategic airlift operations to support Operation Epic Fury.
Pentagon officials confirm ongoing logistical and intelligence support for Phase 3 of Operation Epic Fury.
Congressional committees review proposed supplemental funding packages to sustain allied operations through Phase 3.
Pentagon press briefing discusses ongoing logistical support and accelerated weapons shipments for Operation Epic Fury.
Classified briefing held in Congress regarding the current status and funding needs of Operation Roaring Lion.
U.S. Central Command reported over 12,300 targets struck and more than 155 Iranian vessels damaged or destroyed. Total coalition sorties have exceeded 15,000.
Congress reviews emergency supplemental funding requests for Operation Epic Fury.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Trump says no deal needed to end war, predicts 2-3 weeks. US intel: Iran unwilling to negotiate. Iran demands guaranteed ceasefire + Hormuz sovereignty + reparations. US 15-point proposal rejected as 'maximalist, unreasonable'. Migrant worker casualties highlighted in WaPo reporting.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: The Senate approved a new emergency supplemental funding package to resupply interceptors for Operation Epic Fury.
White House authorizes immediate expedited transfer of THAAD interceptors and precision-guided munitions to CENTCOM stockpiles for allied support under Operation Epic Fury.
U.S. officials report the Iranian Navy has been absolutely destroyed and 80-85% of Iran's air defense architecture neutralized.
The White House announced a fast-tracked supplemental defense package to sustain Operation Epic Fury and resupply critical munitions.
Pentagon press briefing announces accelerated shipment of interceptors to CENTCOM and Israel under Phase 3 of Operation Epic Fury.
White House confirms accelerated defense equipment deliveries to support Operation Epic Fury.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Trump threatens Iranian infrastructure escalation
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Trump: 'US hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran. Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants.' SecDef Hegseth fires Army Chief of Staff Randy George and two senior officers.
Department of Defense requests expedited delivery of precision-guided munitions and interceptors to support ongoing operations under Operation Epic Fury.
Senate Armed Services Committee holds closed-door briefing on regional intelligence sharing and coalition logistics.

UN General Assembly votes 143-9 to authorize multinational intervention to reopen Strait of Hormuz under Uniting for Peace procedure. US and UK begin joint naval planning for mine-clearing and escort operations. 72-hour implementation timeline.
Congress approves expedited munitions transfer under emergency defense appropriations for Operation Epic Fury.
Pentagon announces establishment of new logistical hubs to support Operation Roaring Lion.
President Donald Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum (expiring April 6) for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning the U.S. will 'unleash hell' if not complied with.
U.S. and Iranian forces are engaged in a tense search for a missing American F-15E pilot downed over the Gulf. A U.S. A-10 Warthog involved in the search also crashed.
House Armed Services Committee advanced emergency supplemental funding for Operation Epic Fury logistics.
National Security Council confirmed deployment of additional Patriot batteries to CENTCOM AOR.
Department of Defense announced full integration of regional air defense networks with allied partners.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Trump posts expletive-laden ultimatum on Truth Social: 'Open the Fuckin' Strait, you crazy bastards, or you'll be living in Hell.' Sets final deadline for 8PM ET Apr 6 ('Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day'). Threatens to destroy every power plant and bridge in Iran.

Deadline day. Trump's pause on Iranian energy infrastructure strikes expires at 8PM ET. Analysts warn destroying 10-15 critical transmission nodes would cascade into nationwide blackout lasting to summer 2027.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: President Trump stated that the operational objectives for Operation Epic Fury are nearing completion, noting the Iranian Navy has been effectively neutralized.
Emergency supplemental defense funding bill debated in Congress to replenish depleted munitions from over 15,000 coalition strikes.
Pentagon briefs on ongoing coalition efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz amid continued regional proxy hostilities.
U.S. financial cost for Operation Epic Fury reaches an estimated $42.8 billion to date, with an approximate $1 billion per day ongoing cost.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Trump extends Hormuz deadline to Tuesday 8pm ET (Apr 8). Threatens to 'blow everything up' — power plants 'burning and exploding, never to be used again' if no deal. Says Iran's 10-point response 'not good enough' but 'significant step'. Trump admits 'doesn't know' if winding down or escalating.
Trump Announces 2-Week Ceasefire
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Trump suspends bombing for 14 days contingent on Iran's 'COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING' of Strait of Hormuz. VP Vance to lead US delegation at Islamabad talks beginning Apr 10 (Friday). Pakistan PM Sharif mediating. Iran's SNSC accepts, claims US accepted Iran's 10-point peace plan. Iran demands: sanctions lift, end all regional wars, Iranian control of Hormuz transit coordination, $2M transit fee per vessel.
Reports emerged that the United States, Israel, and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire known as the Islamabad Accords following weeks of escalating conflict.
President Trump maintained a 'maximum pressure' military strategy, threatening to strike Iranian civilian infrastructure (such as power plants) if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.
Congressional Record indicates emergency committee meeting to authorize Phase 3 munition replenishments for Operation Epic Fury.
Pentagon press briefing confirms deployment of additional Patriot batteries to Middle East staging areas to support Coalition operations.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: CEASEFIRE DAY 2 - LEBANON CRISIS THREATENS TRUCE
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Iran re-closes Strait of Hormuz citing Israeli Lebanon strikes. US demands immediate Hormuz reopening. VP Vance confirms US delegation for Islamabad talks Friday (Apr 10) under Pakistan mediation. Netanyahu declares ceasefire does not include Lebanon. Israeli strikes kill 182 in Lebanon (highest single-day toll), 100+ Hezbollah targets in 10 min. Iran FM Araghchi warns ceasefire cannot survive if Lebanon assault continues. UN SG Guterres calls for restraint.
Congress introduces emergency supplemental funding bill to replenish US stockpile of interceptors and provide logistical support for Operation Epic Fury.
Department of Defense announces expedited deployment of an additional THAAD battery and Patriot missile systems to the CENTCOM area of responsibility.
White House confirms the start of a two-week ceasefire to allow for diplomatic negotiations and the reopening of global shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz.
Pentagon press briefing details US force posture to monitor the ceasefire while the State Department initiates the negotiations framework.

U.S. officials, including Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, declared the military objectives of Operation Epic Fury 'accomplished.' Iran agreed to enter negotiations, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and abandon its pursuit of nuclear weapons.

Iran warns ceasefire violations will have 'explicit costs' as Israel launches largest wave of Lebanon strikes (50 jets, 160 bombs, 100 targets in 10 min). Vance confirms Lebanon not included in ceasefire terms. Iran Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf: 'time is running out.' Israel agrees to begin direct negotiations with Lebanon.

VP Vance leads US delegation (with Witkoff, Kushner) to Islamabad for direct talks with Iran delegation led by Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Araghchi. Pakistan PM Sharif hosts. Iran's 10-point proposal on table: nuclear enrichment halt, Hormuz oversight, US force withdrawal, proxy group protections. Trump: 'There will be no enrichment of Uranium.'
Pentagon press briefing confirms extended regional deployments in support of Operation Roaring Lion.
Congressional leaders propose supplemental funding package to sustain air defense replenishments.
CENTCOM released updated figures confirming 13 U.S. service members killed and 381 wounded during the 40-day Operation Epic Fury.
President ordered U.S. forces to remain in 'armed monitoring' positions following the two-week ceasefire agreement with Iran.
CENTCOM Commander declared the operation a generational military defeat for the Iranian regime, confirming 90% of the Iranian Navy was destroyed.
House Armed Services Committee reviews emergency supplemental funding for Operation Epic Fury defense systems.
White House and Pentagon reiterate strategic coordination with Israel regarding Operation Roaring Lion.

VP Vance leads US delegation (with Witkoff, Kushner) to Islamabad. Iran Deputy FM Takht-Ravanchi leads Iranian side. Pakistan PM Sharif calls it 'make-or-break moment.' Iran's 10-point plan as basis. Key sticking points: Lebanon ceasefire inclusion, frozen assets release, Hormuz reopening timeline. Trump posts 'optimistic' message but warns Iran to 'make a deal.'
White House and War Department declare the operation a decisive military victory, stating that over 13,000 targets were struck and 90% of the Iranian navy was destroyed.

A U.S. delegation travels to Pakistan for ceasefire talks. Meanwhile, domestic political backlash grows as officials, including NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani, condemn the estimated $5 billion cost of the initial operation.
House Armed Services Committee advances bipartisan resolution condemning regional escalation and reaffirming support for allied defense.
Department of Defense confirms deployment of additional Patriot batteries to the region in support of Operation Epic Fury.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Islamabad marathon talks — trilateral US-Iran-Pakistan session began past 2 AM local time. Technical experts working on maritime traffic, financial assets, and legal issues. Key sticking points: Hormuz sovereignty (Iran rejects joint control), Lebanon inclusion, sanctions relief, frozen assets. Trump claims US 'win' regardless. No draft agreement confirmed.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: High-level negotiations are currently underway in Islamabad involving U.S. Vice President JD Vance and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi to maintain the two-week ceasefire.

U.S. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth announced that 'every single objective' was achieved, with over 90% of the Iranian Navy sunk and air defense architecture dismantled. Iranian leadership agreed to negotiations regarding its nuclear program and reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

Islamabad peace talks collapse after 21+ hours. VP Vance says Iran refused to commit to abandoning nuclear weapons program. Trump announces immediate naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz via Truth Social: US Navy to block 'any and all Ships' entering/leaving Hormuz and interdict vessels that paid IRGC tolls. CENTCOM implements at 10am ET. Pakistan FM Dar urges both sides to uphold ceasefire. Major escalation during nominal ceasefire period.

Bipartisan concern over blockade escalation. Senate Armed Services Committee requests emergency briefing from SecDef Hegseth. House Foreign Affairs ranking member calls blockade 'reckless escalation that risks sinking the ceasefire.' Pro-administration voices argue blockade is enforcement of existing sanctions, not a ceasefire violation.
President Trump announced a full naval blockade of Iran, signaling a shift to a strategy of total economic and logistical isolation until a new nuclear and security agreement is reached.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: US naval blockade of Iranian ports takes effect; China and Russia urge restraint
US_POLICY_EVENTS.details: CENTCOM enforces blockade starting 10 AM ET. China FM Wang Yi calls on international community to preserve 'extremely fragile' ceasefire. Russia FM Lavrov stresses Moscow believes ceasefire agreements apply to Lebanon. Iran FM Araghchi accuses US of 'maximalism, shifting goalposts, and blockade'. Iran Parliament speaker warns nation will 'not give in'. US Energy Secretary Chris Wright says oil prices will peak in coming weeks as tanker traffic resumes.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: The Trump administration publicly disputed with Pope Leo XIV, urging the Vatican to stick to matters of morality after the Pope called for an immediate end to the conflict and the newly imposed U.S. naval blockade.
U.S. forces remain at high readiness as broader peace negotiations begin with transitional Iranian leadership.

White House confirms second round of US-Iran talks 'under discussion' but no firm date set. Trump tells NY Post talks 'could happen over next two days' in Islamabad. VP Vance expected to lead delegation again. Naval blockade of Iran enters Day 3 — at least 3 vessels including 2 sanctioned tankers transited Hormuz on Day 1. UK PM Starmer declines to join blockade. Iran FM Araghchi says sides were 'inches away' from deal before US 'maximalism.'
U.S. officials confirm strict adherence to interim ceasefire as preparations for diplomatic talks in Islamabad are finalized.
Diplomatic talks underway in Islamabad; U.S. formally demanding the permanent abandonment of Iran's nuclear program and guaranteed opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir arrives in Tehran carrying 'new message' from Washington. Iranian team to review proposals before deciding on second round of US-Iran negotiations. Both sides give 'in principle agreement' to extend ceasefire past Apr 21 deadline. Trump tells NY Post second round could happen 'over the next two days.' VP Vance expected to lead US delegation again.

CENTCOM confirms blockade 'fully implemented.' USS Spruance (DDG-111) redirected Iranian-flagged cargo vessel attempting to evade blockade after leaving Bandar Abbas. Additional tanker spotted departing Iranian port via MarineTraffic data despite blockade. CENTCOM reiterates non-Iranian port traffic is not impeded.
U.S. ongoing negotiations regarding the permanent reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and a new framework for Iran's nuclear program following the April 8 ceasefire.
Pentagon press briefing confirms US forces are shifting to a regional deterrence and stabilization phase following the cessation of major hostilities.
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent officially announced Operation Economic Fury at a White House briefing, targeting Iran's oil transportation infrastructure and regime elites via secondary sanctions.

Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth, Gen. Dan Caine, and CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper briefed on the naval blockade enforcing Operation Economic Fury. The blockade applies to all vessels; at least 13 ships retreated.

U.S. maintains a 'world-class blockade' indefinitely while a fragile ceasefire holds following major combat operations. The administration signaled a preference for a diplomatic deal, with VP J.D. Vance involved.
U.S. maintains the naval blockade of Iranian ports initiated on April 13, leveraging the temporary Pakistan-brokered ceasefire to prevent IRGC force reconstitution.

U.S. officials warned they are prepared to resume 'immediate fighting', potentially targeting Iranian energy infrastructure, if Islamabad negotiations over the nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz fail. The U.S. continues to enforce a naval blockade of Iranian ports to maintain pressure.
U.S. has transitioned to Operation Economic Fury, a naval blockade and maximum-pressure campaign intended to prevent the Iranian regime from reconstituting its military and industrial base.
House Defense Appropriations Subcommittee advances $10B emergency military aid package to replenish Iron Dome and David's Sling interceptors for Operation Epic Fury.
Secretary of Defense confirms the deployment of additional THAAD and Patriot batteries to the CENTCOM AOR in support of allied air defense networks.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.topic: Second Round of Islamabad Talks Scheduled
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir returns from Tehran carrying Iranian counter-proposal on nuclear framework and Hormuz reopening sequencing. White House confirms second round of US-Iran indirect talks tentatively scheduled for Apr 19-20 in Islamabad, with Qatari and Omani observers. CENTCOM maintains blockade Day 5 under unchanged rules of engagement.

CENTCOM reports USS Spruance, USS Momsen, and allied frigate HMAS Hobart coordinating interdiction screen at Strait approaches. Two Iranian tankers redirected back to Bandar Abbas; no boardings. Third-country transit compliance at 97% per JMIC Bahrain. Treasury updates OFAC guidance on permitted humanitarian shipments.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.topic: Rubio Presses E3 on Snapback Sanctions
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Secretary of State Rubio publicly urges France, Germany, UK to accelerate a decision on reimposing UN Security Council snapback sanctions against Iran, warning that Tehran is 'violating the existing agreement and nearing the capability to develop a nuclear weapon.' Markets read statement as pressure tactic ahead of Apr 21 US-Iran ceasefire expiry and second Islamabad round (still unscheduled).

CENTCOM maintains Iran-port blockade under unchanged rules of engagement. USS Spruance / USS Momsen / HMAS Hobart interdiction screen at Strait approaches continues. No new boardings reported; one Iranian tanker redirected back to Bandar Abbas. Third-country compliance 97% per JMIC Bahrain. Treasury updates OFAC guidance permitting humanitarian shipments under escort.

White House reiterates ceasefire (effective Apr 8) expires Apr 21 absent extension. No date confirmed for second round of US-Iran indirect talks in Islamabad despite Pakistani push. JD Vance team on standby; Witkoff-Kushner channel reports 'active scheduling discussions.' Contingency planning underway at CENTCOM for post-expiry escalation scenarios.
House Armed Services Committee reviews rapid acquisition requests for interceptor missiles under Operation Epic Fury.
House Armed Services Committee approves expedited transfer of precision-guided munitions to Israel in support of Operation Epic Fury.
Department of Defense confirms deployment of additional intelligence and reconnaissance assets to the CENTCOM AOR for Operation Roaring Lion.
White House and Pentagon officials brief Congress on the ongoing naval blockade and status of Operation Epic Fury.
Department of War issues a statement condemning Iran's renewed closure of the Strait of Hormuz, affirming the US blockade remains intact.
Legislative proposals introduced to secure emergency supplemental funding for extended naval operations.
SecDef statement reaffirms standing rules of engagement for the Strait of Hormuz blockade remain unchanged pending verified Iranian compliance with transit-protocol undertakings.

State Dept readout of Rubio-Lavrov call notes continued deconfliction on Eastern Mediterranean air/maritime picture; no substantive change on Iran file. Muscat back-channel described as 'technical-only.'

State Dept confirms US technical delegation (chaired by Deputy Secretary for Political Affairs) continues third day of indirect exchanges with Iranian counterparts through Omani interlocutors. Focus: verification mechanics for IAEA monitoring of advanced centrifuge stocks and a phased-lifting matrix linked to Strait transit protocols. No proximity talks scheduled.

CENTCOM J3 reports continued Title 10 blockade operations in Strait of Hormuz and Gulf approaches. Three Iranian-flagged tankers turned back Apr 19-20; two third-country vessels cleared under transit-protocol inspection at Fujairah waypoint. Rules of engagement unchanged pending verified Iranian compliance.

House Foreign Affairs Committee classified briefing on ceasefire compliance and blockade metrics. Chair statement afterward emphasizes 'sustained bipartisan support for verification-linked posture' but notes growing minority concerns over humanitarian impact of prolonged blockade on Iranian civilian fuel imports.

SecState Rubio holds GCC+1 (Jordan) video conference. Communiqué: united position on ceasefire extension contingencies, coordinated reinsurance support for affected shippers, and shared threat-picture on Houthi residual capacity after Day 51 Khamis Mushait intercepts.

U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth and Gen. Dan Caine announced that primary military objectives, including the destruction of Iran's offensive naval and missile industrial base, have been achieved.

CENTCOM announced that the USS Spruance (DDG 111) intercepted and seized the Iranian-flagged tanker M/V Touska in the north Arabian Sea, firing its 5-inch gun into the engine room to disable it after warnings were ignored. This marks the first significant use of force to enforce the naval blockade since the April 8 ceasefire.
Pentagon briefs Congress on Operation Epic Fury supplemental funding requirements.

VP JD Vance, Envoy Steve Witkoff, and Senior Advisor Jared Kushner land in Islamabad for second round of ceasefire-extension talks. Pakistani FM Dar hosts opening session; Iranian delegation presence still unconfirmed at wheels-down, with Iranian MFA signaling 'review of conditions' before engagement. Ceasefire currently expiring Apr 22.

White House issues executive order expanding US naval authority to inspect third-country vessels reasonably suspected of Iranian crude trans-shipment via STS. Treasury OFAC publishes general license extension for humanitarian cargo (food/medicine) to Iranian ports through Jun 30.
Pentagon press briefing confirms accelerated deployment of interceptor stockpiles to CENTCOM AOR in support of Operation Epic Fury.
Senate Armed Services Committee approves expedited transfer of PAC-3 and THAAD interceptors to CENTCOM and Israel to sustain Operation Epic Fury.
Pentagon press briefing outlines the immediate transfer of advanced precision-guided munitions and additional THAAD batteries to CENTCOM in support of Phase 3 operations.
Pentagon authorizes expedited transfer of advanced precision-guided munitions and interceptors to support Operation Epic Fury and replenish stockpiles.
Senate approves additional defense funding for Operation Roaring Lion.
White House press briefing announces new sanctions against Iranian logistics networks.

White House / DoD continuation: ceasefire monitoring posture maintained across Fifth Fleet AOR; US Naval blockade of IRGC ghost-fleet tankers sustained. No new emergency security-package tranche announced at run cutoff. Vance-led diplomatic transit continues preparation for Islamabad Round 2. Continuation entry pending readout refresh.
House Armed Services Committee advances emergency military aid package to support Operation Epic Fury.
DoD announces accelerated delivery of interceptors and precision-guided munitions to US Central Command and Israeli forces.

Trump reaffirmed 23-Apr order authorizing US Navy to destroy any Iranian vessel observed laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz; National Security Council reviewed rules of engagement with CENTCOM. Ceasefire framework formally extended again to allow Iranian government to consolidate around a proposal.

Secretary-level confirmation that Pakistan-hosted Round 2 talks are targeted for early next week; Omani and Qatari mediators coordinating shuttle between US and Iranian delegations. US reiterates blockade remains tied to Hormuz reopening.

Senate Foreign Relations closed briefing on mine-warfare risk and blockade legal basis; no markup of War Powers resolution scheduled. House Armed Services oversight hearing on interceptor draw-down slated.

Trump cancelled the planned US delegation travel to Islamabad after Iranian FM Araghchi departed Pakistan. Trump statement: 'We have all the cards. We're not going to spend 15 hours in airplanes all the time going back and forth to be given a document that was not good enough.' Witkoff and Kushner trip aborted on tarmac.

SecDef Hegseth reaffirmed CENTCOM authority to 'shoot to destroy' any Iranian vessel observed laying mines in or near the Strait of Hormuz, citing Trump 23-Apr executive directive. Order extended to include support craft/tenders staging mine-warfare logistics.

Secretary of State convened Omani, Qatari, and Pakistani mediators on a multilateral call to assess prospects for a Round 2 reschedule. State publicly maintained blockade remains tied to 'complete, immediate, safe' Hormuz reopening. No direct US-Iran channel reactivation announced.

Senate Foreign Relations leadership signaled willingness to consider War Powers resolution markup if blockade extends past 30 days without diplomatic offramp. House Armed Services scheduled closed hearing on interceptor stockpile draw and CENTCOM resupply timelines.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: WPR 60-day clock reaches deadline; White House floats 'ceasefire loophole' to extend
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Under the 1973 War Powers Resolution, May 1 is the 60-day mark from Trump's Mar 2 letter to Congress notifying of Iran hostilities. White House floats argument that the Apr 8 ceasefire technically 'terminated hostilities' for WPR purposes, even though US naval blockade and CENTCOM forward posture continue. Senate Foreign Relations leadership (D + ranking R) signal markup of S.J.Res War Powers resolution next week. House Armed Services holds closed hearing on interceptor stockpile draw and CENTCOM resupply.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: CENTCOM Apr 30 readout: 42 vessels redirected; 41 Iranian-linked tankers held
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: CENTCOM PAO: 42 vessels redirected since Apr 13 blockade start; 41 Iranian-affiliated tankers carrying ~69M bbl held off Bandar Abbas, Kharg, Jask. Estimated value of held crude >$6B. Trump (Apr 30) describes blockade as 'somewhat more effective than the bombing'; reaffirms blockade continues until nuclear deal. Treasury OFAC issues FAQ on tanker-by-tanker waiver process. Source: CENTCOM PAO, Treasury OFAC.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Iran-only Hormuz proposal in stasis; nuclear talks postponed
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Iranian FM Araghchi remains in Tehran post-Moscow consultations; refuses direct US contact. Pakistani FM/PM Sharif hosting Sultan Haitham (Oman) for parallel consultations May 1-2. Trump cancelled Witkoff/Kushner Pakistan trip Apr 25; no US delegation expected this week. Iran proposal: stage-1 mutual blockade lift, postpone nuclear-program talks. White House reportedly views proposal as inadequate.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Trump 'state of collapse' rhetoric escalates; WH says 'Iran has chosen its path'
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Trump Truth Social (May 2 morning ET): 'Iran chose collapse. Blockade continues until full nuclear dismantlement and ballistic-missile cap. No talks until Iran proves seriousness.' WH Press Secretary briefs that WPR 60-day clock elapsed but admin holds 'ceasefire loophole' that no kinetic operations are underway. Senate Foreign Relations Committee schedules emergency closed session May 4. Pakistani PM Sharif and Sultan Haitham continue mediation; no Witkoff/Kushner trip announced.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Bipartisan WPR resolution gathering signatures
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Sen. Kaine (D-VA) and Sen. Lee (R-UT) circulate joint privileged WPR resolution requiring affirmative authorization for any kinetic operations vs Iran beyond defensive AD; staff aides confirm 41 senators committed to co-sponsor by May 5. House Speaker non-committal on companion measure. White House Counsel briefing prepared.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Trump 'reviewing' Iran 14-point counterproposal; tells reporters 'can't imagine acceptable'
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: POTUS tells reporters Saturday/Sunday rotation that he had not yet reviewed but would later review the new Iranian peace offer relayed by Pakistani mediators, writing he 'can't imagine that it would be acceptable in that they have not yet paid a big enough price.' Fars/Tasnim leak Iran's 14-point counterproposal to US 9-point framework; principal divergence is timeline (Iran 30 days vs US 60 days) and sequencing of Hormuz reopen vs sanctions-relief verification. Secretary Rubio dismissive in Sunday morning interviews; National Security Adviser briefs POTUS on text overnight.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Bipartisan WPR resolution co-sponsor count rises to 43
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Sen. Kaine (D-VA) and Sen. Lee (R-UT) joint privileged WPR resolution gains two additional co-sponsors over weekend (43 total) requiring affirmative authorization for any kinetic operations vs Iran beyond defensive AD. House Speaker non-committal on companion measure; House Foreign Affairs ranking member signals interest. White House Counsel briefing prepared for Monday cabinet-level review.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: CENTCOM reports 48 Iranian vessels turned around in 20-day blockade
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: CENTCOM PAO Sunday readout: 48 Iranian-affiliated vessels turned around since Apr 13 blockade onset; 41 Iranian-flagged or affiliated tankers (~69M bbl combined) held off Bandar Abbas/Kharg/Jask awaiting Treasury OFAC tanker-by-tanker waiver. Task Force 55 maintains screen across Strait of Hormuz/Gulf of Oman; CSG-12 (Lincoln) and CSG-9 (Eisenhower) sustain dual-CSG presence. POTUS reaffirms blockade continues until deal struck.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Project Freedom escort mission commences; warship struck near Jask
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: CENTCOM 0500Z: Project Freedom operationally commences with destroyers, 100+ aircraft, unmanned platforms, and ~15,000 personnel committed. Within hours, IRGC/Fars reports two anti-ship cruise missiles struck a USN vessel near Jask Island after warnings to halt were ignored; vessel reportedly turned back. CENTCOM PAO confirms an 'engagement event' but defers damage assessment. Pentagon press secretary states 'comprehensive assessment in progress' and reiterates Project Freedom will continue. POTUS NSC convenes 0700 EDT.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Trump publicly rejects Iran 14-point counterproposal as 'unacceptable'
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: President Trump tells Israel's Kan broadcaster he has reviewed Iran's 14-point counterproposal and deemed it 'unacceptable'; says 'I'm not satisfied' with the timeline divergence (Iran 30 days vs US 2 months) and Tehran's framing on nuclear/missile 'national assets.' State Department response channeled through Pakistani mediators in Doha and Muscat; Iran FM spokesman Baghaei confirms Tehran is reviewing US response. WPR 60-day deadline +3.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: CENTCOM elevates Iraq force-protection to Charlie
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Force-protection at Ain al-Asad and Erbil elevated from Bravo+ to CHARLIE on Project Freedom kickoff and Jask warship strike; Task Force Iraq personnel restricted to base; non-essential convoys paused. Kataib Hezbollah issues mobilization order calling on fighters to heighten readiness for 'war of attrition with the US' and warns Erbil/KRG against 'colluding with foreign forces.' US Embassy Baghdad public-affairs cycle increases.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: CENTCOM confirms first US-flag merchant pair transits Hormuz; six Iranian boats destroyed
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: CENTCOM PAO 0530 readout: two US-flagged merchant vessels completed Hormuz eastbound transit overnight; six (Trump statement: seven) IRGC Navy small craft destroyed during attempted interdiction; no US casualties or hull damage reported. White House National Security Council convened 0700 PDB session. SECDEF Hegseth and CJCS Gen. Caine briefed press at 1100 ET.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: State Department rejects Iranian precondition to suspend Project Freedom
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Spokesperson Tammy Bruce: Iran's demand that the US suspend Project Freedom escort sorties as a precondition to talks resumption is 'not a serious proposal.' Pakistani backchannel via Doha and Muscat remains technically open per Pakistani FM Dar; substantive US-Iran direct contact paused.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: WPR 60-day deadline +4 — bipartisan resolution of disapproval moves in Senate
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Sens. Kaine (D-VA) and Lee (R-UT) move War Powers Resolution joint resolution to floor; Senate Foreign Relations Chair Risch (R-ID) offers competing resolution authorizing Project Freedom escort missions for 90-day period. Procedural vote expected late this week.

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: Trump pauses Project Freedom citing 'Great Progress' toward Iran agreement; blockade remains
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: POTUS Truth Social post at ~1900Z May 5 (effective May 6 ops cycle) pauses escort missions for 'short period of time' to allow agreement finalization with 'Representatives of Iran.' Blockade explicitly retained. Pakistan, Saudi, UAE, Bahrain joint request cited as proximate cause. Pakistani PM Sharif publicly thanks Trump for 'courageous leadership.'
US_POLICY_EVENTS.source: Truth Social, NBC News, CBS News, Al Jazeera, Reuters

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: DefSec Hegseth: 'The ceasefire is not over.'
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Pentagon press conference at ~1400Z; DefSec Hegseth states ceasefire framework remains intact despite Day 66-67 kinetic exchange and Project Freedom pause; CENTCOM tasked to maintain blockade with reduced escort tempo. SecDef declines to characterize Iranian Khatam al-Anbiya denial of UAE strikes.
US_POLICY_EVENTS.source: Pentagon press briefing, Haaretz live blog, Reuters

US_POLICY_EVENTS.event: WPR 60-day deadline +5; Senate procedural holds extended on competing resolutions
US_POLICY_EVENTS.detail: Sens. Kaine (D-VA)/Lee (R-UT) pull WPR joint resolution from immediate consideration pending Project Freedom pause assessment; SFRC Chair Risch (R-ID) competing resolution authorizing Project Freedom escort missions for 90-day period also held. Speaker Johnson signals House mirror calendar.
Congress passes emergency supplemental funding package to resupply David's Sling and Arrow interceptors.
Department of Defense confirms expanded intelligence sharing and logistical support for Operation Epic Fury amidst escalating regional strikes.

U.S. and Iranian negotiators are in Washington and Islamabad attempting to extend the ceasefire for another 60 days and reach a memorandum of understanding (MOU) regarding Iran's nuclear program. President Trump indicated that Iran is 'negotiating on fumes'.
CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper testified before the Senate that the operation crippled Iran's military, destroying 90% of its defense industrial base.
President Trump reportedly held off on a planned follow-up strike on Iran as negotiations continued in Islamabad.
A Congressional Research Service report revealed the U.S. lost or damaged 42 aircraft (including MQ-9s and F-15Es) during the conflict, with total military costs reaching $29 billion.
The U.S. imposed new sanctions on Iranian agencies attempting to control shipping in the Strait of Hormuz amid an ongoing naval blockade.
Space Force officials testified that U.S. space capabilities were kinetically and cyber-targeted for the first time, prompting calls for new electronic warfare investments.
House Defense Appropriations Subcommittee advances $4.2B supplemental package for Operation Epic Fury interceptor resupply.
DoD confirms expedited airlift of THAAD and Patriot PAC-3 interceptors to CENTCOM following elevated threat assessments.
White House and Treasury announce new coordinated sanctions targeting Iranian UAV and ballistic missile manufacturing networks.
Congress passes a $4.5B supplemental defense package dedicated to interceptor replenishment and intelligence support for Operation Epic Fury.
Pentagon press briefing confirms the deployment of an additional THAAD battery to the CENTCOM AOR to reinforce regional air defense architectures.
White House issues new secondary sanctions targeting third-party dual-use technology suppliers facilitating Iranian UAV production.
Congress introduces emergency legislation to fast-track $2.5 billion in interceptor replenishments (Arrow-3, David's Sling) for Operation Epic Fury.
DoD confirms CENTCOM is sharing integrated early warning radar feeds and coordinating regional airspace defense with the IDF.
Senate approves $12 billion emergency supplemental funding for Operation Epic Fury munitions replenishment and CENTCOM logistics.
DoD confirms the deployment of two additional Patriot missile batteries and a THAAD system to the Middle East theater.
Senate Armed Services Committee fast-tracks an emergency $14B munitions replenishment package specifically designated for Operation Epic Fury logistics.
DoD confirms accelerated shipment of THAAD interceptors to CENTCOM AOR and reiterates US naval forces are actively defending against proxy launches.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Coordinated with opening US strikes.
Reporting: Arrow/Patriot expenditure rate unsustainable without resupply.
Continued deep strikes; public emphasis on rocket factories.

Netanyahu declared major strategic victories after 20 days: ballistic missile production and uranium enrichment capabilities effectively destroyed. Operation Roaring Lion continues with sustained high-intensity operations.
Over 4,500 injured in Israel since operation began. 13 confirmed civilian deaths. Iranian missiles wounded 115 people in Israel's south. No new fatalities in past 48 hours despite ongoing barrages.
IDF Chief Zamir states Iran campaign at 'halfway' stage. Iran's fire at Diego Garcia demonstrates Europe 'within range'
IDF Chief: Fight with Hezbollah 'only just begun'. Military spokesperson states war to continue several more weeks.

FDD analysis: Operation Roaring Lion systematically dismantling regime's military nervous system. Israeli operational flexibility allows real-time targeting at 1,500km range. Iran's ballistic missile production capacity now zero.
Israeli military assessments show 80-85% of Iran's air defense architecture destroyed. Zero Iranian ballistic missile production capacity remaining.
U.S. Central Command confirmed the death of Admiral Alireza Tangsiri, commander of the IRGC Navy, following an Israeli airstrike.
The IDF announced the killing of Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri, Commander of the IRGC Navy, along with Navy Intelligence Head Behnam Rezaei and other senior officers, in airstrikes on Bandar Abbas.
The Israeli Air Force (IAF) conducted strikes near Mashhad in northeastern Iran, marking the furthest point reached in the campaign to date.
The IDF reported dropping approximately 70 bombs on dozens of military sites in Kermanshah and Dezful, specifically targeting ballistic missile launch and storage facilities.
Prime Minister Netanyahu reportedly ordered the military to maximize the destruction of Iran's arms industry within a 48-hour window.
Iran launched 11 waves of ballistic missiles at Israel, targeting Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, and northern regions. A direct hit in Kafr Qasim and impacts in Modiin were reported.
Israel conducted 'wide-scale' strikes in the heart of Tehran, targeting infrastructure and missile production sites. Defense Minister Israel Katz warned that operations would 'escalate and expand.'
Iranian state media (IRNA) reported that the Arak heavy-water plant and a uranium processing plant (yellowcake production) were struck by Israeli forces.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: Regional Attacks by Iran and Proxies
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Iran and its proxies intensified drone and missile attacks against Arab states, including Saudi Arabia (Riyadh and Eastern Province), the UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan, targeting energy infrastructure and airports.
Iran blocked two Chinese-flagged vessels from transiting the Strait of Hormuz, enforcing a 'controlled passage system' that excludes ships linked to the US or Israel.
Intelligence reports indicated that Russia began sending drones to Iran to bolster its defenses against the US-Israeli campaign.
Early Saturday morning, the IDF announced a new wave of strikes on 'regime targets' throughout Tehran. Witnesses reported at least 10 intense explosions and plumes of black smoke over the capital.
An Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia wounded at least 10–12 US troops and damaged several aerial refueling planes.
An Iranian ballistic missile carrying a cluster warhead struck central Israel. One man was killed in Tel Aviv, and six bomblets caused damage across residential areas.

The Israeli Air Force (IAF) conducted overnight airstrikes on Bandar Abbas, killing Alireza Tangsiri, Commander of the IRGC Navy, and Behnam Rezaei, the Navy's intelligence head. Tangsiri was identified as a primary figure responsible for efforts to close the Strait of Hormuz.

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that the operation was continuing at 'full force' and stated that the threat of a Hezbollah ground incursion had been neutralized, shifting focus to a buffer zone in southern Lebanon.
The IDF confirmed a strike on the Arak Heavy Water Plant, a critical site for Iran’s plutonium production and nuclear weapons program.
The UAE Air Defense Forces intercepted 6 ballistic missiles and 9 drones. Since the start of the conflict, the UAE reported intercepting over 2,200 Iranian projectiles.
The IAF reported a 90% interception rate for drones launched by Iran and Hezbollah. U.S. officials noted they could only confirm the destruction of about one-third of Iran's total drone fleet.
The IDF reported over 600 attacks from Hezbollah within a single 24-hour period, targeting northern and central Israel.

The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) reported that regional energy facilities and civilian infrastructure had been targeted by approximately 5,000 Iranian missile and drone strikes since the commencement of hostilities.
IDF stated 60-70% of pre-war Iranian military targets destroyed, with 100% of critical targets scheduled for destruction by Mar 31. Operation Roaring Lion 'continuing at full force' per Netanyahu.
IDF assessed Iran can sustain 5-20 ballistic missiles daily for extended period despite strikes. Iran fired BMs with cluster munitions hitting Kafr Qasim, injuring civilians.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: Iran drone attacks on AWS facilities
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Iran shifted toward economic targets — drone strikes on AWS data center facilities in UAE and Bahrain reported. IRGC threatened US tech companies.
Operation Roaring Lion sorties strike subterranean command-and-control centers and degrade remaining mobile air defense networks.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: Ongoing Strike Sorties and Lebanon Ground Ops
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Israeli forces surpassed 800 strike sorties and are simultaneously engaged in a full-scale ground operation in Lebanon against Hezbollah.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF strikes Tehran Revolutionary Court and MoI
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IDF hit dozens of command sites in Tehran overnight including Tehran Revolutionary Court (destroyed), 10 Ministry of Intelligence sites, and Quds Force HQ. Explosions near Azadi Stadium, Milad Tower.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF planning 3+ more weeks of operations
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IDF officials confirmed planning at least 3 more weeks to degrade Iran's defense industry. 'Thousands' of targets remain. Strikes in 26 of 31 provinces.
According to ISW and FDD reports, coordinated ground offensives in the designated theater advanced to their next objectives.
IAF executes suppression of enemy air defenses in key western sectors to secure Phase 3 air superiority under Operation Roaring Lion.
Precision strikes target regional command and control facilities, significantly degrading proxy coordination capabilities.
Precision strikes conducted against key command infrastructure as part of the next phase of Operation Roaring Lion.
Multiple successful interceptions reported over central airspace following a coordinated barrage.
Israel and the U.S. continue a strategy of unrelenting force against remaining Iranian mobile missile units and proxies to prevent reconstitution of military power.
IAF neutralizes reconstituted radar installations as part of Operation Roaring Lion.
Israeli Defense Forces launched secondary airstrikes under Operation Roaring Lion targeting reconstructed missile launch sites.
Under Operation Roaring Lion, IAF elements neutralize hardened IRGC ballistic missile launch infrastructure in the western Zagros mountains.
Over 15,000 targets struck across Iran. Israeli and U.S. officials report widespread neutralization of Iranian military infrastructure.
Under Operation Roaring Lion, the IAF conducted deep penetration strikes against IRGC underground complexes.
Operation Roaring Lion forces neutralize underground drone launch facilities in western Iran.
IDF conducts targeted strikes on command centers under Operation Roaring Lion.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.operation: IDF strike kills Iranian ballistic missile chief Makram Atimi in Kermanshah
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IDF announced precision strike in Kermanshah killed Makram Atimi, head of Iran's central ballistic missile unit, along with several battalion commanders. Major blow to Iran's BM command structure.

Iranian long-range SAM, guided by Russian satellite intelligence, shoots down a US Air Force E-3G Sentry AWACS over the northern Persian Gulf. All 31 crew killed. Loss of primary airborne C2 node severely degrades coalition air coordination. CENTCOM rushing E-7A Wedgetail to theater as replacement.
Israel continues Operation Roaring Lion by striking 'core systems' in Tehran, targeting missile storage and R&D facilities.
IAF targeted key smuggling routes and dual-use infrastructure in southern Lebanon.
Air defense arrays successfully intercepted two exoatmospheric ballistic missiles over the Red Sea.
Unit 8200 initiated operations disrupting logistics routing networks utilized by adversary forces.
Israeli Air Force executes targeted strikes against secondary IRGC command nodes and remaining underground ballistic missile manufacturing sites.
Intensified artillery and aerial exchanges across the Northern border in the ongoing 2026 Lebanon War with Hezbollah.
Arrow and David's Sling systems successfully intercept a renewed barrage of ballistic missiles launched from within Iranian territory.

Israeli and Coalition strikes continue against remaining Iranian reconstitution sites. Intelligence reports indicate Mojtaba Khamenei has assumed the role of Supreme Leader as the regime's central command remains fragmented.

Israel struck South Pars petrochemical plant at Asaluyeh, Kharg Island oil hub, and 3 Tehran airports (targeting Iranian aircraft/helicopters) on Apr 7 before ceasefire took effect. Train travel to Mashhad cancelled. Two South Pars electricity units knocked offline. Ceasefire now in effect; no new Israeli strikes reported Apr 8.
Israel agreed to a two-week ceasefire with the United States and Iran, known as the Islamabad Accords, temporarily pausing major hostilities.
The Israeli Ministry of Defense announced an emergency plan to accelerate the production of Arrow 3 interceptors to sustain air defense capabilities against ongoing ballistic missile threats.
Times of Israel reports successful IAF decapitation strikes against forward IRGC command posts in western Iran.
Haaretz reports sustained drone sorties over the Gulf enforcing no-fly zones and neutralizing hostile UAV launch sites.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: LEBANON ESCALATION. Israel launches largest coordinated strike of the war: 100+ Hezbollah targets in 10 min across Beirut, southern Lebanon, and Bekaa Valley. Lebanon health ministry reports 182 killed (highest single-day toll). Netanyahu: ceasefire 'does not include Lebanon, not end of war, all goals will be achieved.' IAF struck South Pars (Asaluyeh) on Apr 6 before ceasefire. Downed F-15E crew (Apr 3) both recovered Apr 5. A-10 lost and 2x C-130 destroyed in rescue op.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Israeli Air Force (IAF) conducts deep penetration strikes targeting underground ballistic missile production facilities and command-and-control bunkers.
Arrow 3 defense systems successfully intercept a barrage of medium-range ballistic missiles over the Red Sea, neutralizing threats before re-entry.
IDF suspends kinetic strikes under Operation Roaring Lion as the two-week ceasefire takes effect, maintaining full air defense readiness.
Israeli Air Force shifts to high-altitude intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) operations to ensure Iranian compliance.
A comprehensive ceasefire officially took hold, concluding 38 days of major combat operations under Operation Roaring Lion. The region remains in a transitional state as major hostilities halt.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: Lebanon Offensive Continues; ISR Over Iran
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IAF maintains high-altitude ISR flights over Iran for ceasefire compliance monitoring. IDF continues operations against Hezbollah in Lebanon — ceasefire explicitly excludes Lebanon per US/Israel. Lebanon declared national day of mourning (254 killed, 1,160 wounded from Apr 8-9 Israeli strikes). Israel agrees to begin direct negotiations with Lebanon.
Israeli Air Force executes coordinated strikes on subterranean logistical hubs under Operation Epic Fury.
Multiple long-range hostile projectiles successfully intercepted by multi-layered defense systems.
Following the Iran ceasefire, IDF launched a massive wave of strikes against Hezbollah, with 50 fighter jets striking 100 targets across Lebanon.
IDF confirmed the elimination of Ali Youssef Harshi, the personal secretary and nephew of Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem, in Beirut.
Hezbollah fired a rocket barrage toward the Galilee. IDF Chief of Staff clarified Israel is not in a ceasefire with Hezbollah and remains ready to resume operations in Iran.
Iranian state media confirmed the death of former Foreign Minister Kamal Kharazi from wounds sustained during an April 1 joint U.S.-Israeli airstrike in Tehran.
Israeli Air Force executes precision strikes against IRGC command nodes in support of Operation Roaring Lion.
Israeli Navy intercepts illicit arms shipments intended for proxy forces in the Red Sea.

IAF continues Operation Eternal Darkness strikes on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon despite Iran ceasefire. Netanyahu reiterates Lebanon not included in ceasefire. IRGC warns of 'regret-inducing response' if Lebanon attacks continue. Israel offered to restrain strikes during Islamabad talks per Vance.
Ceasefire holds as intelligence assessments confirm the degradation of Iran's ballistic missile and drone infrastructure following 38 days of combat.
The fragile two-week ceasefire continues tentatively, though Israeli forces monitor the region as both sides accuse each other of breaking commitments.
IDF artillery units engage hostile launch sites across the Blue Line in response to rocket fire.
Israeli Air Force conducts targeted strikes on weapons depots near Damascus as part of Operation Roaring Lion.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF struck 200+ Hezbollah targets in Lebanon in past 24h. Strikes in Beirut, Bekaa Valley, southern Lebanon. Lebanon Health Ministry: cumulative 2,020 dead (248 women, 165 children, 85 health workers), 6,436 wounded. ~100 killed in last 24h alone. Iran paused Hormuz traffic in response, warned of 'regretful response.' Israel/US insist ceasefire does not cover Lebanon.

IDF struck 200+ Hezbollah targets in Lebanon in past 24h. Strikes in Beirut, Bekaa, south Lebanon. Lebanon Health Ministry cumulative: 2,020 dead (248 women, 165 children, 85 health workers), 6,436 wounded. ~100 killed in last 24h. Iran paused Hormuz traffic in response; warned 'regretful response.' Israel/US insist ceasefire excludes Lebanon.
IDF struck 200+ Hezbollah targets in Lebanon in 24h. Cumulative: 2,020 dead, 6,436 wounded. ~100 killed last 24h. Iran paused Hormuz. Israel/US: ceasefire excludes Lebanon.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Israel intensifies military operations against Hezbollah in Lebanon, expanding the 2026 Lebanon War despite the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire.
Following the April ceasefire, U.S. and Israeli forces remain at a high state of readiness in the region to oversee the implementation of ceasefire terms, despite the cessation of active combat.

IDF continues strikes on Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon despite ceasefire. At least 12 killed, 35 wounded in latest strikes. Israel rejects ceasefire extension to Lebanon; Washington talks with Lebanese officials scheduled for next week. IDF and US forces remain at high readiness across region. Netanyahu reportedly supportive of Hormuz blockade.
The fragile two-week ceasefire is under severe strain due to continued military skirmishes in Lebanon and the failure of the Islamabad Talks.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: Operation Silver Plow and High Alert
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir placed the military on a heightened state of readiness due to Iranian retaliation threats. IDF ground forces completely surrounded the Hezbollah stronghold of Bint Jbeil in Lebanon.
Forces maintain high readiness while the Strait of Hormuz begins to reopen for commercial shipping under international monitoring.

Israel continues strikes in Lebanon, asserting ceasefire does not apply. Netanyahu supports US naval blockade. IDF maintains high readiness. Lebanon Health Ministry reports cumulative toll exceeds 2,020 killed and 6,436 wounded since March 2. Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 18 on April 11.
IDF conducts comprehensive assessments confirming the functional destruction of Iranian air defense arrays and missile launch sites.
Israel maintaining interim ceasefire as diplomatic talks proceed, following the collapse of Iran's organized military response.

Israel maintaining ceasefire with Iran as diplomatic talks proceed. IDF alert level raised significantly; military instructed to prepare for immediate resumption of fighting. Operation Eternal Darkness continues in Lebanon — IAF strikes southern Lebanon, southern Beirut suburbs, and Beqaa Valley targeting Hezbollah. Israel insists Lebanon not covered by US-Iran ceasefire. IDF readying UAV fleet for potential next phase.
Israeli forces remain in a state of high readiness in the region to ensure compliance with the ceasefire terms.
IAF continues post-strike BDA (Bomb Damage Assessment) and overwatch flights over suspected IRGC subterranean facilities to verify ceasefire compliance.
The conflict remains in a ceasefire phase. Israel continues to maintain a 'special home front situation' while dealing with displacement and monitoring for secondary contagion risks.
A 10-day truce between Israel and Hezbollah is announced in Lebanon, temporarily freezing cross-border military operations.

With commercial transit briefly reopening through the Strait of Hormuz, Israeli military leadership assesses the domestic backlash concerning unfulfilled core goals of Operation Roaring Lion, including incomplete elimination of the nuclear program.

Israeli officials joined the U.S. in warning of a return to combat, signaling readiness to target Iran's energy infrastructure if the current state of 'managed instability' breaks down and negotiations collapse.
Israeli forces remain on high alert, with leadership indicating readiness to resume Operation Roaring Lion strikes if enrichment activities or missile production are detected.
Israeli Air Force F-35I Adir stealth fighters strike alleged IRGC drone manufacturing and ballistic missile assembly facilities deep in western Iran.
Israeli Navy Shayetet 13 commandos successfully intercept and board a vessel attempting to smuggle advanced anti-ship cruise missiles in the Red Sea.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: Operation Eternal Darkness — Lebanon Strikes Continue
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IAF conducts overnight strikes against Hezbollah command nodes in southern Lebanon and Beqaa Valley. IDF maintains force readiness at Northern Command. Netanyahu cabinet reaffirms position that US-Iran ceasefire does not cover Israel-Hezbollah front. Arrow 3 and David's Sling batteries held at elevated alert.

10-day Lebanon ceasefire (effective midnight Apr 17 Beirut time) in Day 2. IAF reports 'isolated rule-of-engagement responses' to Hezbollah reconnaissance UAVs; Netanyahu cabinet reaffirms Iran ceasefire does not cover Lebanon theater. Arrow 3 / David's Sling batteries held at elevated alert. IDF Northern Command maintains force readiness; no ground incursions reported.
Israeli Air Force strikes deeply buried IRGC command and control nodes using heavy precision munitions.
David's Sling and Arrow systems successfully engage a coordinated barrage of ballistic missiles.
Israeli Air Force executes multiple sorties against strategic C2 nodes and subterranean missile infrastructure in the northern sector.
IDF conducts comprehensive aerial reconnaissance over suspected relocated Iranian missile sites.
Operation Roaring Lion forces transition to maximum readiness in response to Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz.
Israeli command coordinates with US forces regarding joint air defense and potential coastal strike options.
Arrow-3 engagement on a long-range trajectory transiting Jordanian airspace; debris recovered in Beit Shean Valley. IAF ISR sortie rate at ~0.7x pre-Lebanon-ceasefire baseline.

Arrow-2 and Iron Dome batteries active in overnight window; IAF reports 2 long-range trajectories intercepted approaching Negev airspace. Fragment recovery underway near Dimona corridor (no damage to facility). IAF ISR sortie rate ~0.75x pre-Lebanon-ceasefire baseline.

Israeli Strategic Cabinet convened by PM to review Day 52 posture. Unattributed briefing indicates sustained support for the US-led blockade framework, but some pressure to avoid visible preemption activity that could complicate Muscat back-channel. No operational decisions announced.

IDF Northern Command holds posture at Lebanese border following UNIFIL de-confliction call. No kinetic exchanges recorded since Day 49; Hezbollah signaling remains consistent with Lebanon ceasefire parameters.

Arrow-2 and Iron Dome batteries active in overnight window; IAF reports 2 long-range trajectories intercepted approaching Negev airspace. Fragment recovery underway near Dimona corridor (no damage to facility). IAF ISR sortie rate ~0.75x pre-Lebanon-ceasefire baseline.

Over the weekend of April 18-19, two Israeli soldiers were killed and 12 wounded by explosive traps in southern Lebanon. A Hezbollah operative was also killed after approaching an IDF unit in a threatening manner.

The IDF struck a loaded Hezbollah rocket launcher on the morning of April 20, citing it as an imminent threat and a violation of the April 16 humanitarian truce. Israeli officials reiterated the truce will only be extended past April 26 if Hezbollah agrees to disarm.
IDF advances operations to secure border regions in ongoing Operation Roaring Lion.

IDF Spokesperson confirms overnight IAF strike on a Hezbollah mobile SSM battery relocating in the northern Beqaa. Secondary explosions observed. No Israeli casualties. Operation Roaring Lion posture remains 'active defense' on the northern front to prevent opportunistic escalation during Gulf ceasefire window.

PM Netanyahu chairs Strategic Cabinet reviewing DIA and Aman joint assessment on Iranian MRBM stockpile reconstitution pace. Cabinet reaffirms coordination with CENTCOM on intelligence-sharing and force-protection posture.
Times of Israel reports successful IAF strikes against newly identified IRGC drone assembly facilities under Operation Roaring Lion.
IAF completes a renewed wave of SEAD operations targeting mobile air defense systems over western and central Iran.
Israeli Air Force neutralizes deeply buried IRGC command nodes and subterranean drone launch sites in coordinated strikes.
IAF conducts targeted deep-penetration strikes on remaining IRGC ballistic missile infrastructure and regional command centers.
IAF executes precision strikes on missile launch facilities.
IDF forces consolidate defensive positions and intercept incoming drone swarms.

IAF continues monitoring posture over residual IRGC ballistic-missile infrastructure; no deep-penetration strikes reported at cutoff. Arrow-3 and David's Sling maintain layered BMD coverage. Continuation entry pending IDF spokesperson readout.
IAF strikes underground IRGC missile production facilities and command nodes in western Iran.
Arrow 3 and David's Sling successfully intercept incoming medium-range ballistic missiles outside the atmosphere.
Arrow-3 and David's Sling held layered posture; two long-range UAV tracks intercepted over Jordan Valley overnight. No impacts inside Israel.

IDF Northern Command observed 24-hour quiet following Trump 23-Apr announcement of 3-week Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extension; limited Hezbollah movement detected near Litani but no LAF-reported violations.
Standing CAP over central Israel maintained; refueler sortie generation unchanged. No strike packages launched; long-range ISR continuing over eastern Syria and western Iraq.

Despite Trump 23-Apr announcement of 3-week Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extension, IDF Northern Command reported continued Hezbollah ATGM and small-UAS activity along Litani; IDF responded with limited artillery and air strikes. Hezbollah leadership publicly called the ceasefire 'meaningless'.
Arrow-3 and David's Sling held layered shield; one long-range UAV track engaged over Negev. No impacts inside Israel. CAP over central Israel maintained.

IDF executed limited air strikes on Hezbollah weapons-storage sites in Bekaa Valley after intelligence-derived ATGM caches identified; Lebanese Army delineated unaffected zones. No reported civilian casualties at run cutoff.
Standing CAP plus long-range ISR sorties continuing over eastern Syria and western Iraq; refueler sortie generation unchanged. No strike packages launched against Iranian targets at run cutoff.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF General Staff briefed on resumption-of-strikes options; readiness raised one notch
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Per Times of Israel (Apr 30) and Israeli broadcaster reporting, Trump and IDF Chief of Staff briefed on contingency for resumption of kinetic operations against Iran 'as early as next week' if WPR-deadline diplomatic offramp fails. IAF Standing CAP over central Israel maintained; refueler sortie generation up ~10%; long-range ISR continuing over eastern Syria and western Iraq. No strike packages launched. Israel Home Front Command quietly raises civil-defense readiness in northern districts. Source: Times of Israel, Channel 12 (Israel).

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF retains heightened readiness; cabinet meets on Iran options
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Israeli cabinet convenes Saturday-night security session per Channel 12 reporting; PM and Defense Minister briefed by IDF CoS on resumption-of-strikes options package (long-range strike, layered deception, prep for retaliatory wave). IAF Standing CAP continues over central Israel; refueler sortie generation up ~12% w/w; long-range ISR over eastern Syria, western Iraq sustained. Home Front Command quietly raises civil-defense readiness in Galilee and Tel Aviv districts. No strike packages launched; readiness consistent with 48-72h posture window. Source: Times of Israel, Channel 12, KAN.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF retains heightened readiness; cabinet weighs Iran options after May 2 night session
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Israeli cabinet held Saturday-night security session per Channel 12; PM and Defense Minister briefed by IDF CoS on resumption-of-strikes options package (long-range strike, layered deception, prep for retaliatory wave). Sunday morning: IAF Standing CAP continues over central Israel; refueler sortie generation up ~13% w/w; long-range ISR over eastern Syria, western Iraq sustained. Home Front Command holds civil-defense readiness at elevated tier in Galilee and Tel Aviv districts. No strike packages launched; readiness consistent with 48-72h posture window. Source: Times of Israel, Channel 12, KAN.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF strikes ~50 Hezbollah-linked sites in southern Lebanon over rolling 24h
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IDF spokesperson says airstrikes targeted approximately 50 Hezbollah-linked sites in past 24h including alleged command centers and repurposed buildings. Lebanese Health Ministry tally cites ~40+ killed May 2, 73 killed since Apr 30, 163 injured. Targets included Siddiqine (Tyre), areas between Kafra and Yater (Bint Jbeil), Al Smaaiyah (Tyre), and Shaaitiyeh. Local media report white-phosphorus use in Marjayoun district (Seriane, Taybeh). Cumulative Lebanese deaths since Mar 2 cross 2,500 per Lebanese authorities. Source: IDF spokesperson, Lebanese MoH, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, RTE.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF retains heightened readiness; cabinet briefed on resumption package
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IDF maintains elevated readiness; cabinet receives Sunday update on long-range strike package and layered deception options. IAF Standing CAP continues over central Israel; refueler sortie generation flat w/w; long-range ISR over eastern Syria, western Iraq sustained. Hezbollah-Israel exchange of strikes across Lebanon border continues at low intensity; Israel carries out 'widespread strikes' in southern Lebanon per AJ; Hezbollah fires 'several drones' at IDF troops. Source: Times of Israel, Channel 12, AJ.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF activates RBR posture; cabinet emergency session on Hormuz strike
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IDF Chief of Staff briefs PM Netanyahu and Defense Minister at 0700 on Hormuz warship strike implications; cabinet convenes emergency security session at 0900. IAF Standing CAP density increased ~30% over central Israel; refueler sortie generation +18% d/d; long-range ISR over eastern Syria, western Iraq, and Persian Gulf approaches sustained. Home Front Command quietly raises civil-defense readiness in Galilee, Tel Aviv, and Beersheba districts. Hezbollah-Israel ceasefire (extended to mid-May) faces fresh pressure; Israel carries out widespread strikes in southern Lebanon overnight; Hezbollah fires 'several drones' at IDF troops near Metula. No Israeli strike packages launched against Iran yet; readiness consistent with 24-48h Israel-options window. Source: Times of Israel, Channel 12, KAN, AJ.

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF maintains RBR posture; Northern Command on heightened alert
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: PM Netanyahu chairs second consecutive Security Cabinet session; IDF Northern Command on heightened alert for potential Hezbollah opportunistic activity coincident with US-Iran Hormuz exchange. IAF maintains continuous CAP over northern airspace; Arrow-3 and David's Sling batteries on highest readiness. No kinetic Israeli activity against Iran reported. Defense Minister Katz publicly affirms support for US Project Freedom and reiterates Israel will respond 'decisively' to any Iranian or proxy strike.
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.source: IDF Spokesperson, Times of Israel, Haaretz

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.event: IDF holds posture; FM Sa'ar welcomes Project Freedom pause with caveats
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: IDF maintains elevated readiness but does not raise alert tier on May 6; no kinetic IDF activity in Iranian/Iraqi/Syrian airspace last 24h. FM Sa'ar statement: 'Israel supports any pause that translates into verifiable Iranian missile/UAV cessation. We assess Iranian intent based on action, not declarations.' PM Netanyahu absent from public statements pending coalition consultations. Hezbollah-IDF Lebanon line continues to trade limited fires per Day 67 pattern.
ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.source: IDF Spokesperson, Times of Israel, Reuters

ISRAEL_OPS_TIMELINE.detail: Israeli Air Force conducts targeted strikes against suspected IRGC underground command bunkers in retaliation for the Oman ceasefire violation.
Arrow 3 defense systems successfully intercept a barrage of medium-range ballistic missiles targeting the Tel Aviv metropolitan area.

Israeli forces have crossed the Litani River and are operating deep within Lebanese territory to dismantle Hezbollah infrastructure. PM Netanyahu emphasized operations will persist until security is restored to the north.
The Institute for National Security Studies (INSS) published a summary noting tactical deterrence was restored, but the Iranian regime remained resilient under new leader Mojtaba Khamenei.
Israeli officials expressed concern that U.S.-led peace talks in Islamabad might exclude Israeli priorities, such as permanent restrictions on Iran's ballistic missile program.
Israel held a ceremony for the arrival of its first KC-46 Gideon refueling aircraft, a critical asset for long-range strike operations.
The IDF reported that approximately 2,500 Hezbollah fighters had been killed since the start of Operation Roaring Lion.
IAF executes precision strikes against alleged IRGC logistics nodes and forward command posts in eastern Syria under Operation Roaring Lion.
Israeli Shayetet 13 units reportedly conduct covert sabotage operations against arms smuggling vessels in the Red Sea corridor.
David's Sling batteries successfully intercept a coordinated salvo of medium-range ballistic missiles targeting the Haifa port industrial zone.
IDF spokesperson confirms the destruction of a subterranean precision-guided munition (PGM) factory linked to proxy forces.
IAF F-35I Adir aircraft execute deep penetration strikes against IRGC underground ballistic missile facilities near Isfahan.
Israeli integrated air defense systems successfully intercept a swarm of 12 incoming Shahed-type munitions launched from Syrian territory.
Coordinated precision strikes neutralize a Quds Force forward operating command node in western Iraq.
IAF executes precision strikes on hardened ballistic missile launch sites in western Iran, significantly degrading immediate launch capabilities.
Israeli multi-layered defense system neutralizes a coordinated barrage of 20+ loitering munitions targeting northern strategic infrastructure.
Under Operation Roaring Lion, Israeli F-35I Adir aircraft strike IRGC underground ballistic missile storage facilities in central Iran.
IAF conducts deep-penetration strikes targeting underground drone assembly facilities in western Iran and proxy supply lines in Syria.
IDF confirms the Arrow 3 air defense array successfully intercepted an incoming medium-range ballistic missile over the Red Sea.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Flagship CSG. Air wing: VFA-11/31/81/136 (F/A-18E/F), VAQ-129 (EA-18G). Positioned for deep-strike sorties into Iran.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Surged from San Diego. Air wing: VFA-22/94/113/25 (F/A-18E/F). Primary Gulf CAP and tanker escort.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: 26th MEU embarked. AV-8B/F-35B + AH-1Z. Hormuz transit escort duty.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Mason (DDG-87)
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Ballistic Missile Defense picket. SM-3 Block IIA inventory. Shot down 3 Iranian BMs on Day 1.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Screen for Lincoln CSG. Tomahawk strike platform. 30+ TLAMs fired in first 72 hours.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: 154 Tomahawk tubes. Pentagon confirmed SSGN involvement without naming vessel. Assessed as Ohio.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Clearing reported mine-laying in Hormuz shipping lanes. Operating with UK RFTG (HMS Diamond, RFA).

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: HMS Prince of Wales (R09)

US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Response Force Task Group. 8× F-35B (809 NAS). Providing southern Arabian Sea air cover.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US Central Command Gulf Assets
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Conducting precision strikes with long-range missiles and hunting Iranian drone/missile launchers

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Abraham Lincoln
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: EA-18G Growlers launching from flight deck in support of Operation Epic Fury as of March 7

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS George H.W. Bush
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Conducting continuous combat air patrols in support of Operation Epic Fury.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Gerald R. Ford
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Supporting Strait of Hormuz reopening and providing regional air superiority.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Theodore Roosevelt
US_NAVAL_FORCES.status: Active Operations in Gulf of Oman
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Conducting continuous combat air patrols to secure maritime routes.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Ronald Reagan Carrier Strike Group
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Positioned near the Strait of Hormuz to secure shipping lanes during the ceasefire.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS George Washington
US_NAVAL_FORCES.status: Active deployment in Fifth Fleet AOR
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Providing sustained air cover and deterrence capabilities.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: Strait of Hormuz Patrols
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Despite the destruction of Iranian naval assets, ship-tracking data indicates the Strait of Hormuz remains heavily populated by Iran-linked vessels.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Arleigh Burke
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Providing regional ballistic missile defense.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US 5th Fleet
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Operations focused on reopening the Strait of Hormuz after Iranian closure, amid global energy market disruptions.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US Naval Blockade Task Force
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Tasked with enforcing the full naval blockade of Iran announced on April 13.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US Naval Blockade — Day 1 Enforcement
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: CENTCOM begins enforcing blockade of all maritime traffic entering/exiting Iranian ports at 10 AM ET Apr 14. Non-Iranian port Hormuz transits unimpeded. Trump warns Iranian vessels approaching blockade will be 'eliminated'. CTF-152 and USS Arleigh Burke (DDG-51) lead interdiction patrols.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: CENTCOM Fleet
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Monitoring Strait of Hormuz reopening.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Abraham Lincoln
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Maintained regional presence in the Gulf following participation in Operation Epic Fury.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: US Central Command Naval Forces
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Deployed to ensure compliance with ceasefire terms and oversee the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Harry S. Truman
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Patrolling the Gulf of Oman.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: CENTCOM Blockade Fleet
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: A dozen ships and dozens of aircraft enforcing the blockade on all vessels entering or leaving Iranian ports. Over 10,000 sailors and Marines involved.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: U.S. Naval Blockade Force
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Enforcing naval blockade of Iranian ports during the ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Spruance
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Disabled and seized Iranian tanker M/V Touska on April 19 in the north Arabian Sea.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Harry S. Truman
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Operating in the Fifth Fleet AOR enforcing maritime security and providing early warning radar.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Positioned in Eastern Mediterranean for contingency operations and strategic deterrence.

US_NAVAL_FORCES.name: USS Ralph Johnson
US_NAVAL_FORCES.note: Engaged and destroyed three anti-ship ballistic missiles in the Red Sea over the past 48 hours.
