GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.def: Ballistic missile, Cruise missile, Drone/UAV — daily intercept counts where MoD reports.
MoD often reports intercepted / detected; gap may include sea falls or leakers.
High = official MoD; Medium = derived; Low = fragmentary or incident-only.
Foreign direct investment — long-term stakes in real assets abroad.
Sovereign wealth fund — state-owned pools (often oil-linked) investing globally.
Force majeure — contractual suspension due to events beyond control.

US codename for joint US-Israeli military operations against Iran launched February 28, 2026. Objectives include destroying Iran's missile capabilities, annihilating its navy, severing proxy support, and preventing nuclear weapons acquisition.
Israeli codename for coordinated strikes with the US under Operation Epic Fury, with Israel focusing on leadership decapitation strikes while US handles large-scale capability degradation.

Iran's strategic oil export hub in Persian Gulf handling 90% of crude exports. Contains deep-water ports for supertankers and major storage facilities. Key economic pressure point targeted by US forces in March 2026.
US tactic of sequential strikes on same targets to maximize casualties, first reported in Iran conflict. Involves initial strike followed by second attack on rescue/response personnel.
Targeted elimination of leadership command structure. Opening phase of Epic Fury involved unprecedented Israeli decapitation strikes killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and senior officials.
Newly developed Israeli air-launched ballistic missile used in Iran strikes. Fired from F-15s during largest combat sortie in Israeli history (200+ fighter jets).
Near-total maritime closure by Iran beginning March 4, 2026. Affects 20% of global oil/LNG transit with over 3,000 vessels stranded. Central strategic issue of the conflict.
Kuwait's largest oil refinery (730,000 bpd capacity) repeatedly targeted by Iranian drone strikes. One of Middle East's largest facilities affected by conflict expansion.
Previous US operation against Iran in June 2025 involving limited nuclear target strikes. Predecessor to more comprehensive Operation Epic Fury.
Socio-economic demographic of over 220,000 Indian nationals repatriated from GCC/Iran due to 2026 conflict. Driving reverse migration and secondary city real estate growth in India.
Temporary governing body established March 1, 2026 to exercise functions of Iran's head of state after Supreme Leader's death
Iranian drone attack on Kuwait's largest oil facility (730,000 bpd capacity) on March 21, 2026
Congressional Authorization for Use of Military Force being discussed by US lawmakers to formally authorize Iran war operations
Iranian intercontinental ballistic missile attack on US-UK base 4,000km from Iran, demonstrating extended range capability
Proposed US operation to occupy or blockade Iran's critical oil export hub to cripple Iranian economy
Remote-controlled explosive boats used by Iran to attack vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, representing a growing asymmetric threat in the narrow waterway.
US tactic reportedly used to maximize casualties by striking the same target twice in succession.
Iranian military strategy that decentralizes capabilities and allows for swift replacement of leaders, enabling continued war of attrition despite leadership losses.
Legal concept invoked by Gulf energy producers when forced to halt exports due to war conditions beyond their control.
Process by which E3 (UK, France, Germany) can trigger reinstatement of UN sanctions on Iran if nuclear negotiations fail.
Underground nuclear weapons facility in Iran reportedly destroyed by US-Israeli strikes on March 3, 2026.
Iranian warship class, all four vessels reportedly destroyed by US-Israeli forces during the war.

Iran's military response operation against US-Israeli strikes. Fourth in series of operations using this codename, involving ballistic missile and drone attacks on targets across Middle East including Gulf states.

Newly developed Israeli air-launched ballistic missile system fired from F-15 fighter aircraft. Made combat debut during opening strikes of Iran war, representing significant advancement in Israeli standoff strike capability.
US Special Operations Command Central unit established December 2025 to deploy low-cost one-way attack drones in Middle East. First-of-its-kind squadron operating LUCAS drones under SOCOM leadership.

GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.term: LUCAS Drone (Low-cost Unmanned Combat Attack System)
GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.def: US one-way attack drone reverse-engineered from captured Iranian Shahed-136. Costs ~$35,000 per unit, operated by Task Force Scorpion Strike. Features enhanced satellite communications and AI-enabled targeting vs original Shahed design.

US Army next-generation ground-launched ballistic missile replacing ATACMS. Range over 500km, can carry two missiles per HIMARS pod vs one ATACMS. Made combat debut against Iran in Operation Epic Fury.

Legal declaration by QatarEnergy suspending contractual LNG delivery obligations due to Iranian strikes on Ras Laffan facilities. Affects 17% of Qatar's LNG export capacity for potentially 3-5 years, impacting global energy markets.
Iranian blockade of critical Persian Gulf waterway through which ~20% of global oil/LNG normally transits. Over 3,000 vessels stranded in region, creating major global energy supply disruption.

Sinking of Iranian Navy frigate by US submarine USS Charlotte near Sri Lanka on February 28, 2026. First submarine combat sinking since Falklands War and first by US submarine since WWII. 104 Iranian personnel killed.
Iranian hypersonic ballistic missile system operationally deployed during the 2026 war, featuring high maneuverability and speed capabilities
Israeli air-launched ballistic missile fired from F-15s, newly developed system used in combat operations against Iran
Proposed US military operation to blockade or occupy Iran's strategic Kharg Island oil hub to cripple Iran's economy
Additional insurance costs for ships transiting high-risk areas like Strait of Hormuz during the Iran conflict
Tactical vulnerability where ballistic missiles equipped with cluster bomblets become difficult to intercept after payload opens and releases submunitions
Disruptions to Iran's covert oil tanker network used to evade sanctions, affecting global oil supply chains
Use of SpaceX's military satellite constellation providing high-bandwidth, unjammable communications for autonomous drone swarms in contested environments

Presidential directive signed by Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on March 24, 2026, declaring a national energy emergency due to oil supply threats from the Iran War, authorizing government procurement of fuel and emergency measures
Brigade-sized rapid deployment unit within the 82nd Airborne Division, approximately 3,000 soldiers ready to deploy anywhere in the world within 18-24 hours
Underground Iranian nuclear facility (Kūh-e Kolang Gaz Lā) near Natanz, buried up to 100 meters deep, assessed by Western intelligence as potential new enrichment plant construction site
Covert Iranian nuclear weapons development site northeast of Tehran, targeted by Israeli strikes for secretly developing critical nuclear weapons components
A significant Iranian nuclear facility located in Yazd Province, which was a target of strikes during the conflict.
An Iranian uranium processing facility located in Yazd Province, which was a target of strikes during the conflict.

A critical geopolitical chokepoint and narrow sea passage connecting the Persian Gulf with the Arabian Sea, essential for global oil shipments. Its potential closure has significant economic implications.

A peace proposal presented by the Trump administration to Tehran via Pakistan, demanding the dismantling of Iran's nuclear program, ending uranium enrichment, and ensuring freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.
Integrated coastal defense network deployed by UAE naval forces to intercept low-altitude suicide drones.
Extended-range variant of the Karrar UCAV observed in recent swarm attacks.
A multi-national defensive protocol activated in response to escalating hostilities on war day 33.
An Iranian high-altitude, long-endurance unmanned combat aerial vehicle, observed operating in Gulf airspace during the late March and early April 2026 escalations.
A joint GCC maritime patrol operation initiated on Apr 2 to secure the Strait of Hormuz.
A joint GCC air defense protocol activated during Phase 3 to synchronize radar data across the Arabian Peninsula.
UAE-led coalition defensive posture enacted on Apr 1 to intercept medium-range ballistic missiles and drone swarms over the Persian Gulf.
A revised maritime defense protocol activated on Apr 2 across Gulf ports to counter asymmetric naval threats.
A United Nations Security Council resolution drafted to condemn Iranian strikes on GCC infrastructure and demand an immediate ceasefire.
The destruction of an incoming ballistic missile outside the Earth's atmosphere, significantly minimizing debris risk to civilian populations below.
A joint defensive maneuver organized by GCC forces observed in the northern Gulf region.
Joint GCC aerial patrol operation initiated on Apr 1 to secure critical infrastructure.
A joint GCC naval and air defense operation initiated in late March 2026 to secure the Strait of Hormuz and the Arabian Gulf from asymmetric maritime threats.
Upgraded Iranian surface-to-air missile system deployed along the Gulf coast, noted in intelligence reports for posing increased risks to regional aviation.
An Iranian long-range surface-to-air missile system recently deployed near coastal battery sites, as observed on Apr 2.
A newly deployed air-to-surface variant of the Zulfiqar ballistic missile, observed in recent strikes.
A joint GCC naval escort mission initiated on Apr 03 to protect commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz from asymmetric maritime threats.
An upgraded variant of the Iranian heavy artillery rocket, first identified in debris from an intercepted strike on Apr 02.
A medium-range ballistic missile introduced into the conflict on Apr 03, featuring an advanced guidance system to bypass electronic countermeasures.
A multi-layered naval defense established near the strait.
A declared maritime no-go area severely restricting commercial shipping, formally enacted on Apr 04.
New variant of Iranian loitering munition observed in Apr 6 strikes on GCC oil infrastructure.
Multinational naval and air defense coordination framework activated on Apr 5 to protect energy transit in the Strait of Hormuz.
A 14-day ceasefire agreement announced on Apr 8, mediated by Pakistan, to pause hostilities between Iran and Gulf forces, with negotiations scheduled for Apr 10.
Iranian policy established on Apr 8 allowing limited commercial transit through the Strait of Hormuz under Iranian military management and proposed transit fees.
A joint GCC air defense framework activated to counter coordinated drone swarms targeting coastal infrastructure.
A joint GCC naval patrol operation established on Apr 9 to secure maritime routes in the Persian Gulf amid heightened conflict.
Sporadic breaches of the April 8 ceasefire occurring in the pre-dawn hours, primarily involving rogue drones.
The emergency ruling body of the Iranian government formed after the reported death of the Supreme Leader.
Upgraded Iranian mobile surface-to-air missile system, analogous to the S-400, used to defend launch sites from counter-strikes.
A fragile two-week ceasefire agreement taking effect around Day 40, aiming to halt large-scale strikes and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
Mediated negotiations commencing on Apr 10 in Pakistan between U.S. and Iranian representatives to stabilize the ceasefire.
A defensive naval and air deployment initiated on Apr 09 to intercept low-altitude threats over coastal waters.
A term for the major military conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran that began on Feb 28 and ended with a fragile ceasefire on Apr 8.
The framework establishing a fragile two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran, brokered by Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Apr 8.
Israel's massive air campaign in Lebanon striking Hezbollah targets, which Iran claims violates the US-Iran ceasefire agreement.
A conflict tracking dashboard (also referred to as the OSS Report) providing live military, economic, and casualty metrics.
A multi-national task force established to coordinate missile defense and early warning systems across the GCC, heavily active during Phase 3 of the conflict.
Iraqi Shia paramilitary group claiming responsibility for secondary drone launches against Kuwaiti islands.
A coordinated air defense operation on Apr 12 resulting in the interception of multiple drones originating from Kish Island before reaching the UAE airspace.
A newly deployed anti-ship cruise missile variant observed on Apr 13 targeting commercial shipping lanes near the Strait of Hormuz.
A joint GCC air defense initiative announced to coordinate regional interception capabilities against incoming drones and missiles.
Upgraded solid-fueled short-range ballistic missile deployed by Iran in the Apr 14 strikes against GCC infrastructure.
IRGC operational name for the second wave of strikes launched on Apr 13 and Apr 14.

A series of peace negotiations brokered by Pakistan following the Apr 7 ceasefire announcement, aimed at converting the fragile truce between the U.S.-Israeli coalition and Iran into a lasting agreement.

A formal diplomatic demand submitted by Iran to the UN Security Council on Apr 14, seeking financial compensation from Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, and Jordan for alleged complicity in the Feb 28 strikes.
An economic warfare measure implemented by the United States on Apr 14, targeting all vessels entering or departing Iranian ports in response to Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
A U.S.-led naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz and the Gulf of Oman, fully implemented on Apr 15 to halt Iranian maritime trade.
An alternative designation used by some analysts and media outlets to refer to the 2026 Iran War.
A regional radar and sensor network enhanced during the conflict to detect low-altitude incoming threats.
High-altitude radar balloons deployed by GCC forces in Phase 3 to detect low-flying drones.
A new variant of Iranian solid-fueled short-range ballistic missile identified by CENTCOM on Apr 16 during the attacks on GCC infrastructure.
A modified medium-range ballistic missile first identified in strikes on Apr 15, featuring enhanced terminal evasion capabilities against regional air defenses.
A modified jet-powered variant of the Shahed series loitering munition, observed with increased terminal velocity, first identified on Apr 17.
A decentralized naval mining and fast-attack boat doctrine employed by the IRGC Navy to disrupt commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, intensified since Apr 15.
A newly deployed Iranian hypersonic ballistic missile variant, first identified during the Apr 16 barrage against GCC critical infrastructure.
Joint GCC naval patrol operation initiated on Apr 17 to secure the Strait of Hormuz from asymmetric fast-attack craft.
Extended-range variant of the Shahed-136 loitering munition, featuring enhanced evasion capabilities and confirmed in theater starting Apr 17.
Emergency Security Control of Air Traffic, implemented by Gulf nations including Kuwait and the UAE in mid-April 2026, leading to widespread commercial flight cancellations.
A joint GCC air defense initiative launched on Apr 18 to coordinate counter-UAS strategies.
A new variant of Iranian ballistic missile first observed on Apr 18 targeting coalition naval assets.
An enhanced extended-range variant of the Zolfaghar ballistic missile identified in strikes on Apr 17.
A joint GCC maritime patrol initiative launched on Apr 18 to secure the Strait of Hormuz.
A tactical doctrine employing coordinated fast-attack craft and loitering munitions to restrict commercial maritime transit through the Strait of Hormuz.
An advanced Iranian mobile air defense system, noted for upgraded radar capabilities observed targeting Gulf drones during the April offensive.
A tactical ballistic missile variant newly deployed by IRGC forces against coastal infrastructure, featuring a specialized terminal guidance system.
A petrochemical plant in Ruwais, UAE, which became a target of Iranian strikes during the conflict.
A major refinery in Bahrain targeted by persistent missile attacks.
A maritime area declared by GCC-aligned forces on Apr 21 restricting civilian and commercial vessels near the Iranian coast, enforced by naval patrols.
A modified variant of the Shahed-136 loitering munition, observed with supplementary fuel tanks since Apr 21, allowing deeper penetration into the Arabian Peninsula.
The U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports aimed at strangling Iran's economy during the 2026 conflict.
A container ship seized by Iran's IRGC in the Strait of Hormuz on Apr 22.
A container ship seized by Iran's IRGC in the Strait of Hormuz on Apr 22.
A joint naval patrol framework established by GCC states in early May 2026 to secure commercial transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.definition: Trump-administration suspension (announced ~1900Z May 5, effective May 6 ops cycle) of US Navy escort missions through the Strait of Hormuz. The complementary US-led blockade against Iranian commercial maritime traffic remains in force. Cited rationale: 'Great Progress' toward a final agreement with 'Representatives of Iran,' tabled by Pakistan/Saudi/UAE/Bahrain joint request. Duration: 'short period' — undefined.

IRGC-led joint operational command for Iranian air defense and strategic strike forces. On May 6, 2026, issued formal statement denying any Iranian operations against the UAE in 'recent days' while warning of 'crushing response' if attacked — contradicting Iranian President Pezeshkian's same-day public rebuke of IRGC over those strikes.

GLOSSARY_ENTRIES.def: The return to active hostilities across multiple fronts starting on May 4 (War Day 66), ending the 17-day ceasefire.
An isolated airspace violation over Oman on May 6 (War Day 68), marking an exception to Oman's typical non-engagement status.
A confirmed ceasefire violation involving an interception event over Omani territory on May 6.
Joint Air Defense Command. A formalized PGCC integrated air and missile defense initiative operationalized after early war communication gaps. Began directing allied intercepts around Day 75.
Smaller, harder-to-detect loitering munition increasingly utilized by Iranian-aligned forces for localized strikes on coalition critical infrastructure in late May.
A joint naval and aerial escort operation implemented by GCC states to protect commercial shipping assets transiting the Strait of Hormuz against fast-attack craft and UAV threats.
An advanced Iranian ballistic missile equipped with a hypersonic glide vehicle payload, reported to have been deployed against high-value GCC air defense nodes starting in mid-May.
A coordinated offensive tactic observed in Phase 3 where low-RCS drones are clustered closely with decoy electronic warfare emitters to confuse coastal Early Warning radars.
An advanced Iranian surface-to-air missile system variant identified actively tracking coalition aircraft during the late May offensives.

Extended range and enhanced payload variants of Iranian loitering munitions observed with increased frequency in Phase 3. They feature upgraded terminal guidance systems resistant to standard GCC electronic warfare countermeasures.

The unified regional missile and air defense coordination network activated by the GCC Secretariat. Used heavily since the ceasefire violation to coordinate interceptor trajectories across Saudi, Bahraini, and UAE airspace.

A joint maritime security operation initiated by the US 5th Fleet and GCC naval forces to secure critical energy shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman against fast-attack craft and USVs.
An IRGC designation for the contested maritime area near the Strait of Hormuz where civilian shipping and oil tankers are actively targeted or seized following the May 04 ceasefire collapse.
The official Iranian domestic designation for the renewed regional military campaign that commenced on May 04 after the failure of the Muscat Protocol negotiations.

Gulf Integrated Air Defense Network; a unified command structure sharing real-time tracking data across GCC states, fully operationalized during Phase 3 of the conflict to counter synchronized drone and missile salvos.
An unofficial risk-management designation adopted by maritime insurance syndicates after May 18 to categorize the high-threat navigational corridor between Oman and Iran.
Joint Air Defense Shield - Gulf. A coordinated multi-national air and missile defense architecture activated by GCC states during Phase 3 of the conflict to counter saturation strikes.
An advanced Iranian solid-fueled short-range ballistic missile featuring terminal maneuverability, heavily utilized against fortified military nodes in late May 2026.
Extended-range coastal defense cruise missile utilized by IRGC Navy in recent Phase 3 anti-shipping strikes.
UAE's multi-layered electronic warfare protocol designed to blind drone swarms before they cross the coastal threshold, newly implemented in Phase 3.

items.name: Bahrain AP vs BDF methodology
items.desc: Mar 5 AP: 75M intercepted (65 destroyed), 124D (88 downed, 36 LANDED). BDF official uses 'intercepted and destroyed' combining methods. True penetration higher than BDF implies. RECOMMEND: Use BDF cumulative but flag AP methodology.

items.name: Saudi aggregate gap
items.desc: Saudi MoD issues fragmented regional bulletins (3-6/day). No single cumulative. Overlap risk when summing. Aviation A2Z '25 BMs + 151 drones by Day 12' is estimated aggregate, not official.

items.name: Qatar post-Mar 10 gap
items.desc: Most comprehensive Qatar total (151M, 69D, 2 aircraft) dates to Mar 10. No updated cumulative through Mar 16. True total substantially higher.

items.name: Kuwait window inconsistency
items.desc: Some bulletins cover 24h, others 48h. Army GHQ and National Guard issue separate reports — double-count risk. Breaking Defense '97 BM / 283 drones' at ~36h consistent with later FDD tallies.

items.name: Wikipedia UAE injury date error
items.desc: Wikipedia states '141 injured as of 10 March' but 141 is from the March 14 MoD statement. Mar 10 actual: 122 injuries.

items.name: Qatar Feb 28 vs Mar 1 timing
items.desc: Big Qatar salvo (65 BM) reported in Feb 28 headlines but MoFA presser dated Mar 1. Likely: strikes began late Feb 28, presser issued Mar 1 covering first 24h.

items.name: Unofficial overlay confidence
items.desc: Rows marked U-High/U-Med/U-Low are sourced from third-party, local, or Arabic-language reporting that has not been formally confirmed by official MoD statements. U-High = 2+ strong non-official sources or 1 strong source + imagery. U-Med = 1 strong source. Treat as supplementary layer only.

COUNTRY_SUMMARY.name: UAE
COUNTRY_SUMMARY.keyTargets: Al Dhafra, Al Minhad, DXB, Jebel Ali, Ruwais, Fujairah

cadence.note: UAE MoD publishes same-day cumulative. Near-certain daily update.

COUNTRY_SUMMARY.name: Qatar
COUNTRY_SUMMARY.keyTargets: Al Udeid AFB, Ras Laffan, Mesaieed, Hamad Intl

cadence.note: Qatar publishes ~60% of days. Post-Mar 10 gap is 23 days — severely overdue. Prolonged silence may indicate infrastructure damage or reporting collapse.

COUNTRY_SUMMARY.name: Bahrain
COUNTRY_SUMMARY.keyTargets: 5th Fleet HQ, Sitra/BAPCO, Muharraq, Seef, Israeli Embassy

cadence.note: BDF issues cumulative checkpoints every 2-4 days. Delta reconstruction required. Last checkpoint Mar 20 — 13 days overdue as of Apr 2.

COUNTRY_SUMMARY.name: Saudi Arabia
COUNTRY_SUMMARY.keyTargets: PSAB Al-Kharj, Ras Tanura, Shaybah, Riyadh/Embassy

cadence.note: Saudi issues 3-6 regional bulletins/day with overlap risk. No national cumulative. Treat totals as minimums.

COUNTRY_SUMMARY.name: Kuwait
COUNTRY_SUMMARY.keyTargets: Ali Al Salem, Camp Arifjan, Port Shuaiba, KWI Airport

cadence.note: Army GHQ and National Guard publish separately. Back-filled from cum checkpoints (97 BM / 283 drones by Day 2, 221 BM / 407 drones by Day 11, 226 BM / 414 drones by Day 13). Days 4,6 added from back-calc.

COUNTRY_SUMMARY.name: Oman
COUNTRY_SUMMARY.keyTargets: Duqm Port, Salalah Port, Sohar Industrial

cadence.note: Oman targeted by drones only — no ballistic or cruise missiles in entire conflict. Likely spared due to role hosting US-Iran talks. ONA/local press primary sources.

COUNTRY_SUMMARY.name: Iraq
COUNTRY_SUMMARY.keyTargets: Erbil Airport, US Embassy Baghdad, Mala Qara

cadence.note: Iraq reporting is facility-specific, not national. Reuters/Al Jazeera more reliable than Iraqi official feeds for same-day incident detail.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: US deploys 3rd carrier strike group to Arabian Sea
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: USS Harry S. Truman CSG ordered to Arabian Sea, joining Lincoln and Eisenhower groups. Pentagon cites force protection for 45,000+ US personnel across 7 GCC bases. Largest naval concentration in region since 2003.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Back-channel talks via Oman stall after Day 27 escalation
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Omani-mediated ceasefire channel went silent after Iran's largest single-day ballistic salvo on Day 27. Oman FM confirmed 'pause in dialogue' — first public acknowledgment of mediation role. US NSC said preconditions remain unmet.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Hormuz Strait transit drops to 40-year low — 8.2 mbpd
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Lloyd's tracking shows daily Hormuz oil transit fell to 8.2 mbpd from pre-war 17.4 mbpd. Insurance war-risk premiums now exceed 5% of cargo value. Three VLCC operators suspended Gulf calls entirely.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Global urea supply crisis — 30% of world supply transits Hormuz
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Qatar and Saudi Arabia account for ~30% of global urea exports, all routing through Hormuz. Fertilizer prices spiked 45% since Day 1. USDA warns of downstream impact on spring planting costs across SE Asia, Brazil, and Sub-Saharan Africa.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Iran launches record 47 ballistic missiles in single Day 31 salvo
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: IRGC fired 47 BMs across UAE, Kuwait, and Bahrain in coordinated 90-minute window — largest single-day ballistic count of the conflict. UAE THAAD/Patriot intercepted 16/16. Kuwait reported 7 impacts in unpopulated areas.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Brent crude breaches $103 — highest since 2022 energy crisis
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Brent hit $103.35/bbl as insurance markets effectively closed for Gulf-loading cargoes. Goldman Sachs raised Q2 forecast to $110 base case, $130 disruption scenario. GCC equity markets down 15-22% from pre-war levels.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: UAE interceptor stockpile below 50% — burn rate unsustainable
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: UAE has expended an estimated 48% of total interceptor inventory across THAAD, Patriot PAC-3, NASAMS, and Pantsir systems. At current daily burn rate of ~25 interceptors/day, critical systems reach depletion within 3-4 weeks without resupply.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: UN Security Council emergency session — Russia and China block resolution
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: US-drafted UNSC resolution demanding immediate Iranian ceasefire vetoed by Russia and China. Moscow cited 'legitimate security concerns' of Iran. France and UK voted in favor. Arab League issued separate condemnation.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: LNG spot prices surge 35% — Qatar exports face war-risk surcharge
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Asian LNG spot hit $18.50/MMBtu as Qatar Ras Laffan terminal reported 2 drone intercepts near facility perimeter. Buyers invoking force majeure reviews on long-term contracts. Japan and South Korea activating strategic reserves.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IRGC disinformation campaign claims 650 US KIA — actual count: 13
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: IRGC Telegram channels and Iranian state media circulating fabricated US casualty figures of 650 killed. Verified US casualties: 13 KIA, ~200 WIA across GCC bases. Pentagon conducting daily press briefings to counter narrative.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Dubai real estate index falls 20% in 5 sessions — FDI reviews accelerating
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Three of four major GCC sovereign wealth funds conducting active investment reviews covering force majeure clauses and potential asset divestiture. Gulf SWFs collectively manage ~$5 trillion. No confirmed capital repatriation yet.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Bahrain 5th Fleet HQ targeted — 3 BMs intercepted, facility operational
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: IRGC targeted US 5th Fleet headquarters at NSA Bahrain with 3 ballistic missiles, all intercepted by Patriot battery. No damage to facility. BDF reports cumulative 139 BM and 238 drone engagements since Day 1.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: ADNOC Ruwais refinery hit — 850k bpd capacity offline for 72 hours
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Drone swarm penetrated NASAMS coverage and struck ADNOC Ruwais refinery condensate processing unit. Facility operated at reduced capacity for 72 hours before partial restoration. UAE's largest refinery complex.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: GCC joint statement calls for 'immediate and unconditional ceasefire'
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: GCC foreign ministers issued joint communiqué from emergency Riyadh summit demanding Iranian ceasefire and calling for UN-supervised de-escalation. Statement notably avoided requesting US withdrawal from region.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Desalination plants across GCC operating on backup power protocols
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Kuwait, UAE, and Bahrain activated emergency power protocols for critical desalination infrastructure. Gulf states depend on desalination for 80-90% of freshwater. No disruptions reported but contingency measures indicate threat assessment elevation.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Russian satellite intelligence confirmed feeding Iranian targeting — enabled E-3 Sentry shoot-down
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: US intelligence confirms Russia is providing real-time satellite imagery and signals intelligence to Iran via secure data link. This intelligence directly enabled the Iranian shoot-down of a US Air Force E-3 Sentry AWACS — the airborne command and control platform that coordinates all coalition air operations. Loss of the E-3 severely degrades battlefield coordination, sensor fusion, and early warning capability across the theater. This represents the single largest intelligence compromise of the conflict.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over Iran — Russian satellite intelligence implicated in targeting
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A US Air Force F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down over central Iran by Iranian air defenses. The pilot was rescued via CSAR operation; WSO status remains MIA with search ongoing. Iran is offering a 0K bounty. An A-10 Warthog was also lost during the rescue mission (pilot ejected safely over Kuwait), and two CSAR Black Hawks took small arms fire. Intelligence assessment indicates Russian satellite data provided precise tracking of the F-15E flight path, enabling the Iranian SAM engagement. This is the first US fixed-wing combat loss to enemy fire since the conflict began.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: E-3 Sentry AWACS destroyed — coalition loses primary airborne C2 node
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: An E-3 Sentry airborne warning and control system aircraft was shot down by an Iranian long-range SAM, killing all crew aboard. The E-3 serves as the "brain" of coalition air operations — managing fighter coordination, threat detection, and sensor fusion across the entire battlespace. Its loss creates a critical gap in command and control that cannot be quickly replaced. Only ~30 E-3s exist worldwide. CENTCOM is rushing E-7A Wedgetail assets to theater as partial replacement, but full C2 capability restoration will take weeks.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: UN General Assembly votes to authorize multinational intervention to reopen Strait of Hormuz
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The UN General Assembly passed a resolution (143-9, 12 abstentions) authorizing multinational naval intervention to forcibly reopen the Strait of Hormuz to international shipping. The resolution invokes "Uniting for Peace" procedure after Russia and China vetoed Security Council action on Day 28. The resolution calls for a multinational mine-clearing and escort operation with a 72-hour implementation timeline. Iran has declared any intervention force will be treated as hostile. This is the most significant multilateral authorization since the Gulf War.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: NSA confirms Russian GLONASS/satellite data sharing with IRGC Aerospace Force
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Classified NSA intercepts reveal a dedicated data link between Russian military satellite constellation and IRGC Aerospace Force targeting centers. Russia is providing near-real-time synthetic aperture radar imagery, enabling Iran to track coalition aircraft movements and naval positions despite coalition EMCON measures. This intelligence pipeline has been active since approximately Day 25 but was only confirmed on Day 33.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: UK convenes 40-nation Hormuz summit — military planners draft mine-clearing operation
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: UK-hosted virtual summit draws 40 nations to discuss reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Military planners begin drafting a combined mine-clearing and escort operation. IMO reports approximately 2,000 vessels stranded. Iran signals willingness to allow selective transits for China and Russia-flagged vessels only, further isolating Western shipping.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Brent crude surges past IEA April crunch warning — Hormuz still closed Day 36
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: IEA issues emergency supply crunch warning as Hormuz blockade enters Day 36. Brent surges on the assessment. WTI briefly surpasses Brent for the first time due to US domestic demand shift. Strategic petroleum reserves being drawn down at accelerated rates across IEA members.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: CSAR cascade — A-10 destroyed, two Black Hawks damaged during F-15E pilot rescue
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The combat search and rescue operation to recover the downed F-15E crew triggered a cascade of additional losses. An A-10 Warthog providing close air support was struck by Iranian fire; the pilot ejected over Kuwait and was recovered safely but the aircraft was destroyed. Two CSAR Black Hawks took small arms fire during the extraction — crew members were injured but both helicopters returned safely to base. Total Day 35: 2 fixed-wing aircraft destroyed (F-15E + A-10), 2 rotary-wing damaged but recovered.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Russian Satellite Data Enables Downing of U.S. E-3 AWACS and F-15E
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Iranian air defenses downed a U.S. E-3G Sentry AWACS and an F-15E Strike Eagle utilizing targeting data provided by Russian satellites, marking a major escalation in foreign intelligence support. A subsequent intense combat search and rescue (CSAR) operation saw an A-10 Warthog destroyed and two Black Hawks damaged, with an F-15E crew member still missing.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: UNGA Authorizes Hormuz Intervention as U.S. Issues 48-Hour Ultimatum
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The UN General Assembly voted 143-9 under the 'Uniting for Peace' framework to authorize a multinational military intervention to forcefully reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Capitalizing on the mandate, President Donald Trump issued a strict 48-hour ultimatum expiring April 6, warning of severe consequences if Iran fails to restore maritime transit.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IDF Precision Strike Eliminates Iranian Ballistic Missile Chief
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The IDF executed a targeted precision airstrike in Kermanshah, successfully killing Makram Atimi, the head of Iran's central ballistic missile unit. This decapitation strike significantly degrades Tehran's command and control capabilities over its strategic missile forces amidst ongoing regional missile exchanges.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: WTI Crude Surpasses Brent Amidst Severe April Supply Crunch Warnings
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude has surpassed Brent in pricing for the first time, driven by extreme shifts in U.S. domestic demand and the ongoing blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. The International Energy Agency (IEA) issued a stark warning regarding an imminent April supply crunch as global reserves rapidly dwindle.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IRGC Launches 94th Wave of Operation True Promise 4
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Iranian forces initiated their 94th strike wave against Israeli industrial and military sites utilizing Khorramshahr ballistic missiles. The sustained volume of missile launches indicates Iran continues to maintain significant medium-range ballistic missile stockpiles despite ongoing coalition interdiction efforts.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: US and Allied Forces Initiate 2-Week Ceasefire with Iran
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: President Trump has announced a temporary two-week ceasefire agreement, pausing major combat operations across the Gulf theater. The diplomatic breakthrough follows a final wave of Israeli pre-ceasefire strikes and an earlier US ultimatum threatening the destruction of Iranian power infrastructure.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Global Crude Benchmarks Plummet 9% on Ceasefire News
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: WTI crude crashed by approximately 9% within 30 minutes of the ceasefire announcement, while Brent crude dropped back below the $100 per barrel threshold. Markets are reacting rapidly to the de-escalation, aggressively pricing in the anticipated resumption of Gulf energy exports.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Coordinated Efforts Begin to Reopen Strait of Hormuz to 150+ Blocked Tankers
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Following the ceasefire agreement, Iran has committed to reopening the Strait of Hormuz under a coordinated international framework. Naval authorities and commercial operators are now organizing the safe transit of over 150 tankers that had been anchored and blocked from exiting the Gulf.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Pre-Truce Iranian Drone Strike Hits Airbase as US Extracts Downed Pilots
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Just prior to the diplomatic breakthrough, an Iranian drone strike targeted a coalition airbase, bringing total reported US casualties for the conflict to 13 KIA and over 300 WIA. Concurrently, US combat search and rescue (CSAR) teams successfully extracted downed F-15E and A-10 aircrews from the theater.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Iran Re-Closes Strait of Hormuz Following Israeli Strikes, Whipsawing Oil Markets
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Tehran abruptly revoked its commitment to safe commercial passage through the Strait of Hormuz in direct response to Israel's bombardment of Lebanon, trapping several vessels that had just initiated transit. After an initial 12% drop in Brent crude on ceasefire optimism, prices have sharply reversed course as the critical maritime corridor is sealed once again.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Gulf Defenses Intercept Massive Post-Ceasefire Barrage of 124 Munitions
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A heavy bombardment comprising 94 suicide drones and 30 ballistic missiles was directed at Gulf states on April 8, complicating the newly announced truce. Allied air defenses successfully intercepted the swarm, with the UAE neutralizing 17 ballistic missiles and 35 drones as US Patriot batteries provided critical screening.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: 15 US Personnel Treated for Injuries Following Ali Al Salem AFB Attack
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The Pentagon confirmed that 15 American service members are currently receiving medical treatment for wounds sustained during the April 7 attack on Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait. All personnel are reported to be in stable condition, underscoring the ongoing human toll preceding the fragile ceasefire.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Iran Re-Closes Strait of Hormuz in Retaliation for Israeli Lebanon Strikes
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Tehran has revoked its safe passage guarantees through the Strait of Hormuz, directly linking the blockade to Israel's recent bombardment of Beirut. Oil prices quickly rebounded 3% to the $96-97 range after ADNOC leadership confirmed the vital transit corridor remains effectively closed to commercial shipping.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IDF Escalates Northern Front with Sweeping 10-Minute Beirut Bombardment
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Israel launched its largest coordinated strike of the conflict against Hezbollah, hitting over 100 targets across Beirut within a 10-minute window. Iran has warned that these ceasefire violations will exact 'explicit costs,' actively threatening the broader framework of the regional de-escalation agreement.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Gulf Air Defenses Intercept 120+ Iranian Projectiles Post-Ceasefire Declaration
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A massive barrage of 94 drones and 30 ballistic missiles was fired toward Gulf states shortly after the initial ceasefire declaration, heavily testing regional air defense networks. UAE and Kuwaiti batteries successfully intercepted the incoming threats, prompting the Pentagon to expedite emergency deployments of additional THAAD and Patriot systems.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Iran Attempts to Levy $1 Million Transit Tolls as Strait of Hormuz Remains Effectively Closed
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Despite the halt in major combat operations, commercial transit through the Strait of Hormuz remains near-zero, with only two tankers successfully crossing since the ceasefire. IRGC naval remnants are reportedly demanding explicit permission and extortionary tolls exceeding $1 million per vessel for safe passage, effectively maintaining an unacknowledged blockade. Global energy markets are reflecting this uncertainty, with Brent crude stabilizing at $96.69 per barrel as traders weigh the diplomatic talks against the physical supply bottleneck.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IDF Sustains Northern Border Operations Against Hezbollah Post-Ceasefire
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The Israeli Defense Forces are continuing localized operations and airstrikes in southern Lebanon, maintaining military pressure on Hezbollah infrastructure despite the broader US-Iran de-escalation framework. Israeli command has signaled that northern airspace defense interceptions and interdiction missions will remain active until proxy supply lines from Syria are verifiably severed. This bifurcated conflict dynamic complicates the broader regional stabilization efforts currently underway in Pakistan.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IDF Sustains Lebanon Operations Amid Regional Ceasefire Monitoring
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Israeli defense forces continue targeted operations in Lebanon, maintaining localized military pressure despite the broader regional ceasefire framework being negotiated in Pakistan. Concurrently, coalition and Israeli military intelligence are focused on rigorous ceasefire monitoring and verifying the neutralization of high-value targets struck during the final phases of Operation Roaring Lion.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Hormuz Transits Drop 95% as 800 Freighters Stranded in Persian Gulf
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has essentially halted, leaving roughly 800 freighters trapped in a holding pattern within the Persian Gulf. As transits plummet from a pre-war average of 135 ships per day to near-zero, JPMorgan analysts warn that a prolonged closure could drive Brent crude up to $120 per barrel.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IDF Strikes 200+ Hezbollah Targets in Lebanon Over 24 Hours
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Israeli forces have significantly escalated their northern campaign, striking over 200 Hezbollah positions across Beirut, the Bekaa Valley, and southern Lebanon. The Lebanese Health Ministry reports approximately 100 fatalities in the last 24 hours alone, bringing the cumulative toll in Lebanon to 2,020 dead and over 6,436 wounded.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Pentagon Deploys Additional Patriot Batteries for Operation Epic Fury
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The U.S. Department of Defense has accelerated the deployment of supplemental Patriot missile defense batteries to the Middle East theater in direct support of Operation Epic Fury. This strategic repositioning aims to harden allied airspace and U.S. staging areas against the persistent threat of Iranian ballistic missile and drone saturation strikes.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: U.S. Initiates Full Naval Blockade of Strait of Hormuz
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Following the failure of diplomatic talks, the U.S. has officially commenced a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Enforcement operations began at 10:00 AM ET, with CENTCOM forces actively boarding and inspecting transiting vessels to sever Iranian maritime movement.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Brent Crude Surges 8% to $103/bbl on Blockade Implementation
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Global oil markets reacted violently to the initiation of the U.S. naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, with Brent crude surging 8% to breach the $103 per barrel mark. The sudden supply choke-point escalation threatens cascading downstream effects on global energy markets.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Senate Armed Services Demands Emergency Briefing on Blockade Escalation
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Mounting bipartisan concerns over the strategic implications and potential blowback of the newly enforced Hormuz blockade have prompted the Senate Armed Services Committee to demand an emergency closed-door briefing from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: US Imposes Full Naval Blockade on Iran; CENTCOM Initiates Vessel Inspections
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A full US naval blockade of Iranian ports has officially taken effect as of Day 46, with CENTCOM forces actively boarding and inspecting vessels. The unprecedented move aims to sever Iran's maritime logistics, driving Strait of Hormuz transits to historic lows.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Global Oil Markets Rattled as Brent Crude Surges 8% to $103/bbl Following Blockade Announcement
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: In direct response to the collapse of the Islamabad talks and the newly announced US naval blockade, Brent crude prices spiked 8% to $103 per barrel. The total enforcement of the blockade raises severe concerns over prolonged supply disruptions from the Persian Gulf.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: China and Russia Condemn US Naval Blockade; Senate Committee Demands Emergency Briefing
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Following the enforcement of the US naval blockade on Iran, China and Russia have publicly urged restraint, warning of severe global economic fallout. Domestically, bipartisan concerns over the escalation led the Senate Armed Services Committee to request an emergency briefing from Defense Secretary Hegseth.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Senate Demands Emergency Defense Briefing on Blockade Escalation Risks
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Escalation concerns have prompted bipartisan action in Washington, with the Senate Armed Services Committee requesting an urgent briefing from Defense Secretary Hegseth regarding the total naval blockade. Concurrently, the Trump administration engaged in a rare public dispute with Pope Leo XIV over the Vatican's criticism of the escalating conflict.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Israel Sustains 'Operation Silver Plow' in Lebanon Despite Regional Ceasefire Efforts
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Israeli forces are continuing military operations in Lebanon, sustaining 'Operation Silver Plow' despite broader regional ceasefire negotiations and maritime monitoring. This persistent northern front activity threatens to complicate the fragile diplomatic progress and regional stabilization efforts.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Commercial Vessel Struck by Unidentified Projectiles Amid Blockade Enforcement
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A commercial vessel navigating restricted waters was struck by two unidentified projectiles on Day 46, causing an onboard fire that has since been stabilized. The attack underscores the severe security risks remaining in the Gulf as the U.S. naval blockade restricts Iranian port access.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: CENTCOM Declares Naval Blockade 'Fully Implemented' as USS Spruance Intercepts Iranian Vessel
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The US naval blockade of Iran has reached full implementation, reducing Strait of Hormuz transit by over 90 percent. In a major maritime enforcement action, the USS Spruance (DDG-111) successfully intercepted and redirected an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel attempting to breach the perimeter.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: US-Iran Ceasefire Holds at Day 48 While Israeli Operations in Lebanon Continue
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Direct engagements between US and Iranian forces remain halted, keeping total US casualties static at 13 KIA and 399 WIA with 354 returned to duty. However, the regional pause has not extended to the Levant, where Israeli military operations in Lebanon proceed uninterrupted despite the broader strategic freeze.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Interim Ceasefire Holds Between US and Iran as Israeli Operations Continue in Lebanon
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The interim ceasefire between US and Iranian forces remains fully intact with zero new casualties reported on Day 48. Concurrently, Israeli military operations in Lebanon are continuing unabated, actively operating outside the scope of the broader regional pause.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Israel Maintains High Force Readiness and Continues Lebanon Operations
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Although the interim ceasefire is largely holding across the primary Gulf theater, Israeli Defense Forces are maintaining strict force readiness protocols. Operations in Lebanon continue unabated, reflecting a bifurcated approach to regional security amidst broader de-escalation efforts.
sources: ISW (Institute for the Study of War)

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Hezbollah Claims Strike on Israeli Naval Vessel Amid Ongoing 'Operation Roaring Lion'
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: While the broader regional ceasefire holds, Hezbollah claimed responsibility for a strike on an Israeli military vessel off the Lebanese coast. The incident occurs as Israel maintains high force readiness and conducts extensive reconnaissance under 'Operation Roaring Lion', highlighting the continued volatility of the northern front despite the pause in major operations against Iran.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Hormuz Transit Hits Historic Low as U.S. Issues Ultimatum on Permanent Reopening
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Commercial shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has reached a milestone decline, effectively halting energy exports from the upper Gulf despite the cessation of major hostilities. U.S. negotiators have issued a back-channel ultimatum linking the easing of the ongoing naval blockade to a permanent, uninhibited reopening of the strait and a revised nuclear framework.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Pentagon Shifts Posture to Deterrence Phase, Warns of Immediate Strikes on Iranian Energy Assets
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The US Department of Defense has officially shifted its operational footing to a regional deterrence and stabilization phase following major combat operations. However, US officials issued a stark Day 50 warning that they remain prepared to resume immediate combat fighting, explicitly identifying Iranian energy infrastructure as the primary target if the current ceasefire collapses.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IDF Completes 'Operation Roaring Lion' Reconnaissance Following Hezbollah Naval Attack
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The Israeli Defense Forces have completed a strategic review under 'Operation Roaring Lion' and declared full operational readiness for resumed strikes. This heightened alert posture immediately follows a Day 49 Hezbollah attack on an Israeli military vessel off the coast of Lebanon, testing the boundaries of the temporary cessation of hostilities.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Severe Commercial Vessel Backlog Paralyzes Anchorages Outside Hormuz
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A critical backlog of commercial vessels is paralyzing anchorages outside the Strait of Hormuz following the suspension of multinational escort convoys. Despite a recent France-UK summit aimed at establishing a new maritime security framework, global shipping conglomerates are refusing to transit the chokepoint without guaranteed naval protection.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: US and GCC+1 Solidify Ceasefire Extension Contingencies
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Secretary of State Marco Rubio convened a secure video conference with GCC leaders and Jordan to align on ceasefire extension contingencies. Concurrently, US technical delegations are in their third day of talks, indicating a coordinated diplomatic push to maintain the current operational pause while House committees review blockade metrics.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IRGC Fast-Boats Abort VLCC Approach Amid US Escort Intervention
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: An IRGC Navy fast-boat element attempted to close on a southbound Very Large Crude Carrier (VLCC) but aborted the approach following the vectoring of a US Navy P-8 Poseidon and interposition by a Coast Guard escort. This marks continued Iranian testing of the maritime blockade rules of engagement despite the broader ceasefire posture.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Convoy Throughput Rebounds as Suez Arbitrage Tightens
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The Joint Maritime Information Center in Bahrain has observed a rebound in convoy throughput, suggesting localized easing of maritime risk premiums under the current US/Coalition escort regime. However, Suez routing arbitrage remains tight, indicating that broader shipping markets remain cautious about the longevity of the de-escalation phase.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Israel Strikes Hezbollah Launchers Amid Strategic Cabinet Review of Northern Posture
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The Israeli Strategic Cabinet convened to assess northern border security following a series of skirmishes and routine air defense intercepts. IDF forces executed a preemptive strike on a Hezbollah rocket launcher, ensuring the Gulf ceasefire does not enable opportunistic northern front escalation.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Islamabad Round 2 Opens with Ceasefire 24 Hours from Expiration
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: US VP JD Vance, Envoy Witkoff, and Senior Advisor Kushner arrive in Islamabad for a second round of ceasefire-extension talks hosted by Pakistani FM Dar. Iranian delegation presence remains unconfirmed at wheels-down. Ceasefire expires Apr 22 (Day 54). Core disputes remain unchanged: US demands unconditional Hormuz reopening and firm non-nuclearization commitment; Iran demands full termination of the naval blockade before substantive negotiations. Qatar and Oman co-mediating logistics. Outcome over the next 24-36 hours will dictate whether the conflict re-enters kinetic phase or transitions to an extended armistice framework.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Brent Eases ~2% as DFC Reinsurance Tranche 2 and Lloyd's Broaden Coverage
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Crude prices trimmed as ceasefire-extension optics improve with the Islamabad delegation's arrival and JMIC convoy intake recovers to 7 vessels in 24 hours. DFC $18B second tranche of war-risk reinsurance came online, driving war-risk premiums on third-country tonnage down to ~5.5% of hull (from 5.8%). US-linked ships remain at ~10%. Fujairah bunker lines clearing faster. Saudi and UAE spare capacity draws continue absorbing Iranian crude displacement for Asia-bound buyers.

crossRef.label: Brent slips ~2% ahead of Islamabad Round 2
Lloyd's quotes broaden; Hormuz stays Restricted

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Gulf Intercept Tempo Steady; IRGC FIAC Probes JMIC Convoy Escort
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: UAE and Saudi MoDs reported 12 combined BM engagements and 35 UAV intercepts in the overnight window — slightly below Day 52 levels. Qatar (2 UAVs) and Bahrain (1 BM, 3 UAVs) report residual activity with no damage or casualties. USS Mason warned off an IRGC FIAC element that closed to 800 yards of a convoy in the northern Strait approach; craft disengaged. CENTCOM flagged the incident as provocation pattern consistent with Day 51-52 reporting. No Epic Fury kinetic strikes executed during ceasefire Day 14; ISR packages remain at elevated tempo.

crossRef.label: IRGC FIAC warned off JMIC convoy escort

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IAF Initiates 'Operation Epic Fury' Following Strategic Cabinet Review
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The Israeli Air Force has launched 'Operation Epic Fury' air sorties, marking a significant operational shift following the conclusion of Operation Roaring Lion Stage 4. The escalation follows an intensive strategic cabinet review of the Iran Dossier and prior suppression strikes targeting the Beqaa Valley.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: White House Expands Naval Interdiction Authority in CENTCOM AOR
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A new executive order vastly expands US naval authority to inspect third-country vessels reasonably suspected of sanctions evasion or weapons smuggling. The directive provides NAVCENT commanders with expedited interdiction protocols to counter blockade efforts.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Hormuz Escort Backlog Eases After IRGC FIAC Encounters
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The naval escort backlog in the region is easing as JMIC convoy intake resumes at a higher capacity of +7 vessels. Operations have normalized following a day of friction where the USS Mason fired warning flares to deter an approaching IRGC FIAC element and a separate vessel sustained minor drone debris damage.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Commercial Transits Halt Reaches Day 11 Amidst Escort Bottlenecks and Vessel Strike
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The Strait of Hormuz has recorded zero commercial transits for the 11th consecutive day as naval escort convoy systems experience severe bottlenecks. The backlog was exacerbated when a commercial vessel waiting for an escort was struck and damaged by a loitering munition.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Israeli Operations Shift as 'Epic Fury' Advances and 'Roaring Lion' Enters Monitoring Phase
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Following intense sustained strikes in western Iran, Israeli military efforts under Operation Epic Fury have moved into an advancement phase, while Operation Roaring Lion has transitioned to a monitoring posture. These operational shifts appear coordinated with the broader US ceasefire monitoring posture in the region.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: US-Led "Project Freedom" Escort Mission Commences; Coalition Warship Struck Near Jask
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The multinational "Project Freedom" naval escort mission officially launched on May 4, attempting to break the prolonged shipping paralysis in the Strait of Hormuz. In immediate retaliation, a coalition warship was struck by an inbound projectile near the Iranian port of Jask, triggering an emergency session in the Israeli cabinet and elevating CENTCOM force-protection postures to Charlie across Iraq.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Diplomatic Impasse Hardens as Trump Rejects Iranian 14-Point Proposal Amid WPR Pressure
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Former President Trump publicly dismissed Tehran's 14-point counterproposal as "unacceptable," signaling a collapse in back-channel negotiations and cementing a hardline US posture. Concurrently, domestic political friction over the prolonged Gulf deployment is mounting, as the bipartisan War Powers Resolution co-sponsor count reaches 43.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Prolonged Hormuz Blockade Triggers Sustained LNG Force Majeure as CENTCOM Turns Away 48 Vessels
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains paralyzed at roughly 5% of pre-war levels, following CENTCOM's report of 48 Iranian vessels turned around over a 20-day blockade. The sustained closure has forced Qatar to extend force-majeure on May-loading Asian LNG cargoes, compounding global energy market disruptions as alternative Cape reroutes reach capacity saturation.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IDF Elevates Readiness to RBR Posture Amid Gulf Escalation and Ongoing Lebanon Strikes
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: In direct response to the strike on a coalition vessel near Jask, the Israeli cabinet held an emergency session and authorized an elevated RBR posture for the IDF. This shift coincides with a rolling 24-hour campaign that struck approximately 50 Hezbollah-linked sites in southern Lebanon, underscoring widening regional instability.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Iranian Anti-Ship Missiles Strike US Naval Vessel Near Jask Island
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: IRGC shore batteries fired two anti-ship cruise missiles that struck a US Navy vessel near Jask Island, marking a severe escalation following the 16-day ceasefire. The IDF has activated its RBR posture in response as regional forces brace for wider conflict.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Iranian OWA-UAV Penetrates UAE Air Defenses, Striking Infrastructure
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: An Iranian one-way attack drone penetrated the outer air-defense ring of the UAE, resulting in the first confirmed infrastructure hit since hostilities resumed. The strike threatens to disrupt localized energy operations and has prompted emergency security reviews across the GCC.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Project Freedom Secures Hormuz Transit Amid Direct US-Iran Combat Losses
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The first US-flagged merchant pair successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz under US naval escort, destroying an Iranian small-boat swarm during the operation. CENTCOM confirmed the engagement resulted in the first direct US-Iran combat losses since the pre-ceasefire kinetic phase.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Trump Pauses Project Freedom Escorts Citing Diplomatic Progress
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: President Trump abruptly paused Operation Project Freedom escorts, citing 'Great Progress' toward a broader agreement, shortly after the State Department rejected Iranian preconditions. The maritime blockade remains intact, with Defense Secretary Hegseth noting 'the ceasefire is not over.'

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Off-Axis Iranian Munition Impacts Salalah, Wounding Civilians
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A near-miss UAV or missile event in Salalah wounded two foreign nationals with debris in a confirmed ceasefire violation. Oman's Ministry of Foreign Affairs categorized the incident as a likely off-axis Iranian munition and has launched a formal investigation.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Project Freedom Escorts Paused; 20,000 Seafarers Stranded Near Hormuz
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: President Trump has paused the Project Freedom naval escort framework after two successful US-flagged transits, citing diplomatic progress toward an agreement with Iran. Despite DefSec Hegseth asserting the conflict is not over, the blockade persists, leaving the IMO to report approximately 20,000 seafarers stranded on 2,000 vessels near the Strait of Hormuz.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IDF Escalates with Western Iran Airstrikes; Arrow 3 Intercepts Retaliatory Missiles
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Israel has transitioned from a heightened readiness posture to executing targeted airstrikes against strategic facilities in Western Iran. Retaliatory Iranian missile launches were intercepted by Israel's Arrow 3 system, coinciding with the Pentagon confirming expanded intelligence sharing for Operation Epic Fury.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Oman Territorial Violation Wounds Two Foreign Nationals in Salalah
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A near-miss UAV or missile strike impacted Salalah, Oman, wounding two foreign nationals with debris. Omani authorities have stated the weapon was likely an off-axis Iranian munition, representing a concerning territorial violation amid regional ceasefire fluctuations.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Emergency Defense Funding Passed as WPR Deadline Extensions Loom
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Congress has passed an emergency supplemental funding package specifically to resupply David's Sling and Arrow interceptors following the resumption of active hostilities. Concurrently, Senate procedural holds have extended the War Powers Resolution 60-day deadline by five days amid debates over US operational limits.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Russian Satellite Data Implicated in Precision Strikes on GCC Bases
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Declassified US intelligence indicates Russian commercial and military satellites are providing real-time targeting telemetry to Iranian missile forces. This data-sharing arrangement has drastically reduced the circular error probable (CEP) of Iranian ballistic missiles since the resumption of active hostilities, bypassing standard GPS jamming defenses.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: VLCC Tanker Sunk During US-Escorted Hormuz Breakout Attempt
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A Liberian-flagged Very Large Crude Carrier (VLCC) was critically damaged and sunk by newly deployed Iranian smart mines while attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz under US 5th Fleet escort. The incident has indefinitely halted coalition plans to forcibly reopen the maritime chokepoint and sent crude futures spiking.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: US Navy E-2D Hawkeye Downed by Upgraded Iranian SAM Network
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: An E-2D Advanced Hawkeye providing critical airborne command and control for coalition forces was shot down over the central Persian Gulf by an Iranian Bavar-373 air defense system. The loss of the C2 asset severely degrades coalition early warning capabilities against low-altitude drone swarms and represents a massive capability loss.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Russia and China Veto UNSC Resolution Authorizing Hormuz Intervention
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A UN Security Council resolution drafted by the UK and US to authorize a Chapter VII multinational maritime intervention to clear the Strait of Hormuz was vetoed by Russia and China. The diplomatic deadlock forces the GCC and US to consider non-mandated unilateral military action to break the ongoing blockade.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Declassified Intel Reveals Russian Space-Based Targeting Support to IRGC
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: US and allied intelligence have declassified findings indicating Russian GLONASS and electro-optical satellite constellations are providing real-time targeting telemetry to Iranian missile forces. This data sharing has demonstrably improved the Circular Error Probable (CEP) of recent Iranian ballistic missile strikes against GCC infrastructure.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Coalition Mine-Clearing Operations Commence in Strait of Hormuz Under Heavy Escort
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: US 5th Fleet and Royal Navy assets have initiated coordinated mine-countermeasures (MCM) operations in the western approaches of the Strait of Hormuz. The flotilla, heavily escorted by guided-missile destroyers, aims to establish a narrow transit corridor for stranded LNG carriers, though Iranian fast-attack craft remain a persistent threat.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: USAF E-3 Sentry AWACS Damaged by Long-Range SAM Over Gulf of Oman
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A USAF E-3 Sentry AWACS operating in international airspace over the Gulf of Oman sustained heavy fragmentation damage from an Iranian Sayyad-4B surface-to-air missile, forcing an emergency landing at Al Dhafra Air Base. While the crew survived, the temporary loss of this critical C2 node severely degrades coalition air tracking in the eastern Gulf sector.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: UNSC Fails to Pass Intervention Resolution Following Dual Vetoes
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A US-drafted UN Security Council resolution seeking authorization for expanded multi-national maritime enforcement in the Persian Gulf was vetoed by both Russia and China. The diplomatic deadlock forces the GCC and Western allies to rely entirely on bilateral defense pacts outside the formal UN framework.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: US E-3 Sentry AWACS Severely Damaged in Unprecedented Long-Range Engagement
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: An E-3 Sentry Airborne Warning and Control System (AWACS) aircraft sustained heavy damage from a suspected Iranian long-range air-to-air missile over the central Persian Gulf, forcing an emergency landing at Al Udeid Air Base. The incident marks the first successful targeting of a Tier-1 US C2 asset in the conflict, temporarily degrading coalition airspace management capabilities in the central sector.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Declassified Intel Suggests Russian Satellite Support for Iranian Targeting
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Recently declassified coalition intelligence assessments indicate that Iranian forces are actively utilizing commercial and state-sponsored Russian satellite imagery to coordinate precision strikes against moving naval assets. This foreign intelligence sharing arrangement significantly enhances Iran's anti-access/area denial (A2/AD) capabilities and explains the recent increase in accurate long-range anti-ship missile fire.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: UNSC Intervention Resolution Vetoed by Russia and China, Stalling Unified Action
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A US-backed UN Security Council resolution aimed at authorizing expanded multinational naval escorts and preemptive strikes in the Strait of Hormuz was vetoed by Russia and China. The veto underscores a growing diplomatic fracture, with Beijing citing the need for regional de-escalation while quietly maintaining alternative land-based and grey-fleet oil supply agreements with Tehran.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Coalition Commences Heavily Escorted Mine-Clearing Operations in Hormuz Approaches
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Multinational naval forces have initiated coordinated mine-clearing operations at the southern approaches to the Strait of Hormuz, supported by intensive air and surface cover. Progress remains extremely slow due to the confirmed presence of advanced bottom-tethered influence mines and continuous harassment threats from Iranian fast attack craft swarms.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Suspected Russian Satellite Intel Aiding Iranian Targeting of GCC Patriot Batteries
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: US DIA reports indicate a significant shift in Iranian drone flight paths, actively exploiting recent radar blind spots in Saudi and Emirati air defense networks. Intelligence assessments suggest commercial or state-level satellite imagery from Russian entities is being utilized to update Iranian targeting data following the recent ceasefire breakdown.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Coordinated Swarm Attack Partially Disables Ruwais Refinery Operations
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A wave of over 20 Shahed-136 drones breached UAE lower-tier defenses, striking the Ruwais industrial complex. While the UAE MoD confirmed the interception of 14 drones, 6 impacted secondary crude processing units, forcing a temporary 30% reduction in refining output and spiking regional gasoline futures.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Russia and China Veto UNSC Resolution Condemning Renewed Gulf Hostilities
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: A US-drafted UN Security Council resolution demanding an immediate halt to Iranian ballistic missile launches and strikes on GCC infrastructure was vetoed by Russia and China. The veto signals hardening diplomatic gridlock and severely limits international legal cover for expanded coalition kinetic responses against Iranian mainland targets.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Major Shipping Firms Suspend Hormuz Transit Amid Renewed Mine Threats
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Following intelligence leaks regarding new Iranian IRGC-N mine-laying exercises near the Strait of Hormuz, three of the top five global container lines have indefinitely suspended transit through the corridor. Consequently, war risk premiums for the region have surged an additional 45% within a 48-hour window.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Space Force Reports First Kinetic Targeting of U.S. Orbital Assets Amid THAAD Deployment
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: U.S. Space Force officials confirmed that American space capabilities have been subjected to unprecedented kinetic and cyber targeting by adversarial forces. In response to these multi-domain threats and ongoing ballistic attacks, the DoD has deployed a THAAD system and two additional Patriot batteries to reinforce the Middle East theater.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Maritime Strikes Cause U.S. Naval Casualties and Solidify Widespread Cape Rerouting
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Consecutive days of drone and cruise missile attacks against commercial shipping culminated in a loitering munition strike 50nm off Muscat. The intense operational tempo has resulted in U.S. and coalition casualties during intercept and damage control operations, exacerbating supply chain disruptions as vessels permanently reroute around the Cape of Good Hope.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: Draft 60-Day Peace MoU Negotiated as Senate Approves $12B Epic Fury Funding
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: Diplomatic back-channels have successfully drafted a Memorandum of Understanding proposing a 60-day cessation of hostilities to stabilize regional shipping and energy markets. However, the U.S. Senate simultaneously approved $12 billion in emergency supplemental funding for Operation Epic Fury, signaling Washington's readiness for a protracted conflict if talks collapse.

WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.headline: IAF Executes Deep Penetration Strikes to Neutralize Hezbollah Subterranean Infrastructure
WAR_ROOM_BRIEFS.body: The Israeli Air Force conducted deep penetration strikes targeting Hezbollah forward operating bases and neutralizing critical underground facilities following persistent UAV swarm incursions. These pre-emptive operations are designed to degrade adversarial command and control infrastructure before subterranean munitions can be deployed in the wider conflict.

IRAN_RECONCILIATION.comment: Data shows significant gaps between IRGC claims and verified MoD intercepts. UAE and Iraq account for the vast majority of verified drone intercepts.

fdi.title: FDI & Sovereign Wealth Fund Reallocation
fdi.execSummary: Three of the four major GCC economies are conducting active SWF reviews, covering future investment commitments, force majeure clauses, and potential asset divestiture. Gulf SWFs collectively manage ~$5 trillion. No confirmed capital repatriation as of March 20, but Dubai Real Estate Index fell 20% in five sessions and developer bond markets are effectively closed.
sections: Gulf as Safe Hub — Damage Assessment
keyFindings: Goldman Sachs models show Qatar and Kuwait could contract 14% and UAE 5% if the Strait remains closed through April — the worst economic disruption to Gulf states since the 1990 Iraqi invasion.
The 'Gulf as safe hub' narrative has been shattered — DIFC evacuations by Citi, PwC, Deloitte, and Google, with Dubai losing $2.8B in direct business activity.
Rating agencies affirmed Gulf sovereign ratings at stable outlook, reflecting $842B in aggregate central bank reserves and policy flexibility.

detailedSections.heading: SWF Strategic Review
detailedSections.content: Three of four major GCC economies (Saudi, UAE, Kuwait) reviewing SWF investments. Combined Gulf SWF AUM ~$5 trillion. Review covers slowing future commitments, reassessing sponsorships, evaluating force majeure clauses, and potential asset divestiture.

detailedSections.heading: GDP Growth Revisions
detailedSections.content: Oxford Economics cut GCC 2026 growth by 1.8ppts to 2.6%. Goldman worst case: Qatar and Kuwait -14%, UAE -5%, Saudi -3% if Strait closed through April. Capital Economics: 10-15% GDP decline if conflict lasts 3+ months.

detailedSections.heading: FDI Freeze and Tech Investment Risk
detailedSections.content: GCC M&A came to 'abrupt halt' after Feb 28. Microsoft ($15.2B), AWS ($5.3B), Google/PIF ($10B) Gulf investments now in question. AWS data centers in UAE/Bahrain struck — first military attacks on hyperscale cloud.

detailedSections.heading: Dubai Real Estate Shock
detailedSections.content: Dubai Real Estate Index fell 20% in first 5 sessions, erasing all 2026 YTD gains. Aldar and Emaar dropped 5% on first day. Bid-ask spreads widened to ~2 points (vs 0.5 normal). Off-plan transactions (65% of deals) most vulnerable.

detailedSections.heading: Rating Agency Resilience
detailedSections.content: S&P affirmed UAE at AA (Stable) citing 184% of GDP in net financial assets. Saudi affirmed at A+ (Stable). CDS widening modest: Abu Dhabi +13bps, Saudi +12bps. 84% of Fitch-rated GCC sukuk remain investment grade.

fx.title: Currency, Capital Flows & Safe Havens
fx.execSummary: All six GCC currency pegs are holding, backed by $842B in aggregate central bank foreign reserves (+10.5% YoY). UAE Central Bank launched a five-pillar resilience package backed by AED 1 trillion (~$270B). Gold spiked ~$300 on war outbreak to ~$5,423 then retreated to ~$4,680; the Swiss franc has displaced the yen as the clearest safe-haven currency.
keyFindings: Dubai physical gold traded at a $30/oz discount to London — an unprecedented inversion where war logistics turned the city from a bullion premium hub into a supply trap.
The CHF displaced JPY as primary safe-haven because Japan's crude and LNG imports via Hormuz structurally weaken the yen in this specific conflict.

Dubai wealth managers report 10–20 family office relocation inquiries per firm, but actual capital flight remains modest — only 4 of 1,000 UAE family office members formally inquired about relocation.

detailedSections.heading: GCC Currency Pegs Holding
detailedSections.content: All six GCC dollar pegs remain stable. SAMA reserves at $475B (6-year high, +10% YoY). GCC aggregate net foreign assets $842B. No NDF devaluation risk priced. Bahrain most vulnerable: reserves barely cover one month of imports, debt approaching 135% of GDP.

detailedSections.heading: Banking Sector and CBUAE Package
detailedSections.content: S&P modeled $307B deposit flight risk if conflict escalates. No significant departures observed. CBUAE launched 5-pillar resilience package: 30% reserve access, AED/USD liquidity facilities, LCR/NSFR relief, buffer suspension, and Dh50B zero-interest collateralized loans.

detailedSections.heading: Gold Price Dynamics
detailedSections.content: Gold spiked ~$300 on war outbreak to ~$5,423 then fell to ~$4,680. Dubai gold traded at $30/oz DISCOUNT to London — flight cancellations created local oversupply. Gold ETFs saw record $19B inflows in January.

detailedSections.heading: Capital Flight Indicators
detailedSections.content: Private jet demand surged from ~15 to 98 flights/day from Gulf. Charter prices doubled. Singapore wealth attorneys report 6-7 of 20 Dubai clients calling, 3 planning transfers. Dubai family offices control $1.2T+ in assets.

detailedSections.heading: Safe Haven Reconfiguration
detailedSections.content: CHF displaced JPY as clearest safe haven — Japan vulnerable to Hormuz energy disruption. EUR/CHF fell to 0.9039 (lowest since 2015). DXY crossed 100 driven by inflation repricing, not traditional flight-to-safety.

food.title: Food Security Impact on Gulf States
food.execSummary: The war has triggered what analysts call the GCC's most serious food security test since the 2008 global food crisis. A region importing 80–90% of its calories, with over 70% normally transiting Hormuz, faces simultaneous closure of both primary maritime arteries — Hormuz and the Red Sea/Suez corridor. Perishable prices have risen sharply, desalination plants struck, and WFP warns the disruption could become the worst to humanitarian supply chains since COVID-19.
keyFindings: Bahrain suffered the first confirmed military strike on a Gulf desalination plant — 90%+ of GCC drinking water comes from just 56 plants, creating extreme concentration risk.
Iran banned all food/agricultural exports, eliminating the UAE's top supplier of fresh produce and triggering 100–233% price spikes in perishables within days.
