Qatar's 18-month reserve (vs. UAE's 4–6 months) reflects post-2017 blockade investments in the Hamad Port Food Security Terminal — prior crisis infrastructure is the decisive variable.

detailedSections.heading: GCC Import Dependency
detailedSections.content: GCC imports 80-90% of food; 70%+ normally transits Hormuz. UAE imports 90% of what it eats. Iran banned all food exports — was UAE top fresh produce supplier. Hormuz traffic fell 97%. Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain effectively landlocked.

detailedSections.heading: Strategic Food Reserves
detailedSections.content: UAE: 4-6 months essential food with 300,000 MT Fujairah silos. Qatar: 18 months food, 4 months water (post-2017 blockade investment). Saudi: 6+ months wheat. No emergency drawdowns reported.

detailedSections.heading: Food Price Spikes
detailedSections.content: UAE onion prices +233%. Tomatoes +83-100%. Fruits/vegetables broadly +20-30%. Global urea jumped ~40% to $700+/ton. WFP: if conflict persists to mid-2026, ~45 million additional people face acute food insecurity.

detailedSections.heading: Water Security Crisis
detailedSections.content: GCC operates 400+ desalination plants, 90% from just 56 plants. Bahrain suffered first confirmed desalination strike. UAE emergency water reserves: only 2 days normal; 16-45 days under rationing.

detailedSections.heading: Supply Chain Rerouting
detailedSections.content: Air cargo dropped 79% in Gulf, 22% globally. CMA CGM reopened multimodal routes through Oman and Jeddah. War risk surcharges: $3,000-4,000/container. WFP: 'supply chains on brink of most severe disruption since COVID.'

SHIPPING_RISK.execSummary: Shipping risk premiums have temporarily stabilized following the announcement of Operation Sentinel Vigilance on Apr 03, though insurers remain cautious.

market_impact_wk6.execSummary: Economic recovery efforts face delays as early April strikes impact regional supply chains.
market_impact_wk6.appendDetailedSection: Infrastructure repairs are ongoing following the Apr 04 attacks, leading to temporary constraints on local exports.

DAY_37_UPDATE.appendDetailedSection: Supply lines adjusting to phase 3 operations.

HORMUZ_SHIPPING.execSummary: On Apr 05, maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz dropped by 40% following new exclusion zone declarations.
HORMUZ_SHIPPING.appendDetailedSection: Insurance premiums for Gulf shipping reached record highs on Apr 06 as the exclusion zone took full effect.

ECON-01.execSummary: Energy markets experienced increased volatility on Apr 6 following the introduction of Khaibar-5 munitions in the Gulf.

HORMUZ_TRANSIT.execSummary: Strait of Hormuz conditionally reopened under Iranian management with proposed transit fees following the Apr 8 ceasefire.
HORMUZ_TRANSIT.appendDetailedSection: As of Apr 8, commercial shipping is permitted but subject to Iranian military coordination and potential tolls to fund post-war reconstruction.

maritime_shipping.execSummary: Insurance premiums for shipping in the Strait of Hormuz hit record highs on Apr 9 following new naval blockades.
maritime_shipping.appendDetailedSection: Regional maritime agencies reported on Apr 9 that additional war risk premiums have surged due to escalated naval presence.

brent_crude_day41.execSummary: Brent crude stabilizes at $94.22 following the ceasefire announcement on Apr 8. Phase 3 market impacts observed.

GULF_ENERGY_EXPORTS.execSummary: As of Apr 10, Gulf energy exports face new logistical hurdles due to increased insurance premiums, despite naval escorts.

strait_hormuz_closure.execSummary: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed. Iran demanded tolls in yuan, which the U.S. warned would trigger strikes on Iranian power plants.
strait_hormuz_closure.appendDetailedSection: The East-West Crude Oil Pipeline in Saudi Arabia was damaged in recent attacks, further constraining alternative export routes. A single Gabon-flagged tanker transited on Apr 09.

ENERGY_INFRASTRUCTURE.execSummary: Saudi Arabia lost approximately 600,000 bpd of production capacity after the SATORP refinery was damaged on Apr 7-8 and shut down by TotalEnergies on Apr 10. The East-West Pipeline strike further caused a 700,000 bpd throughput drop.

ENERGY_MARKETS.execSummary: Brent crude is trading near $96.76 with WTI at $99.03. The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to commercial traffic, stranding over 600 ships.

GLOBAL_IMPACT.execSummary: The IMF warned of a looming global recession due to Strait of Hormuz disruptions.
GLOBAL_IMPACT.appendDetailedSection: Resolution Foundation data from Apr 15 shows the average British household is already $500 worse off due to war-driven energy spikes. The U.S. Treasury sanctioned 29 targets for involvement in Iranian oil smuggling on Apr 15.

SHIPPING_LANE_STATUS.execSummary: As of Apr 13, maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains heavily restricted, with insurance premiums surging following the deployment of Zulfiqar-7 missiles.
SHIPPING_LANE_STATUS.appendDetailedSection: New OSINT reports indicate an elevated threat to tankers in the eastern Gulf. Oman has increased naval patrols to secure adjacent routes.

strait_of_hormuz_blockade.execSummary: As of Apr 15, the conflict has entered a phase of economic warfare. Iran continues to close the Strait of Hormuz, while the U.S. officially initiated a naval blockade on Apr 14 targeting ships entering or departing Iranian ports.
strait_of_hormuz_blockade.appendDetailedSection: The U.S. naval blockade aims to test Iran's economic endurance but has raised concerns among GCC states, with Saudi Arabia urging an end to the blockade on Apr 14 to prevent further regional escalation.

energy_infrastructure.execSummary: Energy sector resilience tested on Apr 22 following heightened drone alerts near major LNG export terminals.
energy_infrastructure.appendDetailedSection: Heightened threat levels on Apr 22 resulted in minor loading delays at key export facilities, alongside a sharp increase in maritime insurance premiums.

hormuz_closure_apr18.execSummary: On Apr 18, Iran announced the re-closure of the Strait of Hormuz following the implementation of a U.S. naval blockade.
hormuz_closure_apr18.appendDetailedSection: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz on Apr 18 has caused global oil prices to surge, peaking at over $120 per barrel in mid-April.

GCC_TRADE_IMPACT.execSummary: As of Apr 18, maritime traffic rerouting continues to affect supply chains.
GCC_TRADE_IMPACT.appendDetailedSection: Recent data from Apr 17 indicates a stabilization in shipping insurance premiums.

MARITIME_INSURANCE.execSummary: War-risk insurance premiums for vessels transiting the Persian Gulf have spiked by an additional 15%.

econ_summary_d52.execSummary: Regional market volatility continues on Apr 20 following renewed infrastructure targeting. Energy sectors show strain.
econ_summary_d52.appendDetailedSection: New risk premiums applied to maritime shipping in the Strait of Hormuz after IRGC fast-boat harassment on Apr 20.

energy_markets.execSummary: Regional energy markets stabilized following coordinated GCC maritime patrols.
energy_markets.appendDetailedSection: New shipping lanes have been secured, mitigating previous supply chain disruptions.

infrastructure_damage.appendDetailedSection: Recent reports highlight the targeting of the Borouge petrochemical plant in the UAE, the Bapco refinery in Bahrain, and Kuwait International Airport's fuel tanks.

Energy & Maritime Security.execSummary: Market volatility continues as new drone variants threaten deep-water loading terminals and shipping lanes.
Energy & Maritime Security.appendDetailedSection: Recent deployments of extended-range UAVs have raised concerns over the security of major GCC export terminals, leading to a spike in maritime insurance rates and rerouting of non-essential commercial shipping.

hormuz_seizures.execSummary: On Apr 22, IRGC seized two container ships in the Strait of Hormuz. A Pentagon assessment leaked on Apr 23 suggests clearing Iranian mines from the strait could take up to six months.
hormuz_seizures.appendDetailedSection: The seizure of the Epaminondas and MSC Francesca marks a severe escalation in the Strait of Hormuz, exacerbating the global fuel crisis and supply chain disruptions.

shipping_lanes_may.execSummary: Commercial shipping routes remain open but with heightened security measures in place following new patrol initiatives.

Phase3_Energy_Impact.execSummary: By late May, oil markets experienced a significant price spike following the renewed targeting of GCC energy infrastructure in Phase 3.
Phase3_Energy_Impact.appendDetailedSection: Insurance premiums for tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz hit record highs following the May 18 and May 26 events, compounding the economic strain observed since the end of the ceasefire.

shipping_premiums.execSummary: Maritime insurance premiums spiked 45% following the May 20 drone incidents near Fujairah, prompting emergency rerouting protocols.
shipping_premiums.appendDetailedSection: New data from Ambrey Analytics indicates a severe shift in risk assessment for the newly designated SHEZ. Spot freight rates for tankers transiting the Gulf of Oman hit late-March peak levels on May 22.

shipping_impacts.execSummary: Insurance premiums for vessels passing the Strait of Hormuz spiked by 40% in late May following renewed anti-ship missile threats in Phase 3.
shipping_impacts.appendDetailedSection: Lloyd's List and S&P Global reported significant rerouting of tanker traffic on May 25, citing credible threats of swarm drone attacks near Omani territorial waters as the ceasefire dissolved.

energy_sector_impact.execSummary: Since May 28, attacks on offshore platforms have escalated, prompting new naval patrols and driving a brief spike in regional energy markets.
energy_sector_impact.appendDetailedSection: On May 29, renewed focus on UAE and Qatari offshore gas infrastructure led to a temporary 4% spike in LNG spot prices. Supply chains remain resilient due to rapid maritime rerouting protocols.

tour.title: Tourism & Hospitality Economic Impact
tour.execSummary: The war has inflicted the sharpest single-event shock to Gulf tourism since COVID-19. Within 48 hours, airspace closures grounded flights across the UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, and Kuwait, Dubai occupancy plummeted from ~86% to an estimated 20%, and forward bookings collapsed. WTTC estimates the conflict costs at least $600 million per day in lost international visitor spending.
keyFindings: Dubai hotel occupancy collapsed from 86% to single digits within three weeks — faster than COVID, the worst peacetime-to-crisis drop in the city's history.
The conflict created a dual-chokepoint aviation shutdown unprecedented in Gulf history, with foreign airlines banned from DXB indefinitely and cancellation rates above 65%.
Recovery will be faster than COVID (infrastructure intact) but slower than a simple flight restart — lasting sentiment damage to the 'Gulf as safe destination' narrative is the key variable.

detailedSections.heading: Hotel Occupancy Collapse
detailedSections.content: Dubai occupancy plummeted from 86% in January to below 20% by mid-March. Fortune Group closed 70% of rooms. ADR dropped ~50% week-on-week. Luxury hotels reported single-digit forecasts. Wego reported rates dropping up to 40%.

detailedSections.heading: Flight Disruption Scale
detailedSections.content: Over 30,000 flights cancelled; 5 million passengers affected. Peak cancellation rate exceeded 65%. Qatar Airways fell from 563 to 66 flights/day (−88%). DXB banned all non-UAE carriers indefinitely from March 17.

detailedSections.heading: WTTC $600M/Day Estimate
detailedSections.content: WTTC: conflict costs $600M/day in lost visitor spending. Tourism Economics: 23-38M fewer visitors and $34-56B lost. Pre-conflict forecast was +13% growth; swing is 24-40 ppts below baseline.

detailedSections.heading: Cruise and Conference Cancellations
detailedSections.content: MSC Euribia stranded. Aroya cancelled all 2026 Gulf sailings. TOKEN2049 postponed to April 2027. IMO repatriating 15,000+ stranded cruise passengers. 80,000+ Dubai bookings cancelled in first week.

detailedSections.heading: Recovery vs COVID Comparison
detailedSections.content: Unlike COVID (67% decline, 12-18 month recovery), this is acute aviation/safety shock. Infrastructure intact, demand suppressed not eliminated. Hotels pivoting to staycation model. WTTC: security incidents recover 'as quickly as two months.'

semi.title: Semiconductor & Global Supply Chain Impact
semi.execSummary: The war has triggered a compound supply-chain crisis extending far beyond oil markets. Vessel traffic through Hormuz collapsed 95%+ from ~138 ships/day to single-digit daily transits. The disruption simultaneously chokes LNG supplies critical to Taiwan's and South Korea's semiconductor energy grids, removes up to one-third of global helium supply, curtails aluminum and fertilizer production, and threatens hyperscaler data center plans across the Gulf.
keyFindings: Taiwan holds only 11 days of natural gas reserves with 37–38% of LNG from Qatar via Hormuz — creating a ticking clock for TSMC production if the blockade extends into summer.
Qatar's Ras Laffan shutdown removed ~33% of global helium supply with no alternative export route; fabs hold ~1 week of helium on-floor, meaning the gap hits fabrication directly by early April.

The war triggered the first-ever military strikes on hyperscale cloud data centers (AWS in UAE and Bahrain), potentially redirecting $30B+ in Gulf AI/cloud investment toward southeastern Europe and India.

detailedSections.heading: Hormuz Traffic Collapse
detailedSections.content: Vessel traffic collapsed 95%+ from 138 ships/day to single digits. Only 89 vessels crossed March 1-15, including 16 oil tankers. 3,200 ships caught in paralysis. All major container lines suspended and rerouted via Cape of Good Hope.

detailedSections.heading: Semiconductor Energy Crisis
detailedSections.content: Taiwan imports 95% of energy; 37-38% of LNG from Qatar via Hormuz. Only 11 days gas reserves. TSMC consumes ~9-10% of Taiwan electricity. LNG spot exceeded $15/MMBtu. Morgan Stanley warns $1.5-3B deferred semiconductor revenues.

detailedSections.heading: Helium Supply Shock
detailedSections.content: Qatar produces 30-38% of global helium, all via Hormuz. Ras Laffan shutdown since March 2. Spot prices surged 70-100%. Fab-floor helium inventory ~one week; Asian fabs hit directly by early April.

detailedSections.heading: SOX Index and Chip Stocks
detailedSections.content: SOX fell 13.3% peak-to-trough. Samsung and SK Hynix lost $200B+ combined. TSMC shares fell ~13%. South Korea KOSPI dropped 18% in four days — worst since 2008.

detailedSections.heading: Tower Semiconductor Disruption
detailedSections.content: Israel state of emergency disrupted Tower Semiconductor shipments. Orders rerouted to TSMC affiliate VIS and PSMC. Affected: PMICs, CIS sensors, RF, MEMS, analog. Mature-node pricing rising.

market_volatility_apr12.execSummary: Energy markets experienced minor fluctuations following reports of intercepted drones near offshore infrastructure, though swift MoD statements stabilized trading.

market_volatility_may26.execSummary: Brent crude volatility spiked 8% in mid-May due to renewed swarm tactics in the Gulf. Insurance premiums for vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz have reached wartime highs.
market_volatility_may26.appendDetailedSection: Regional equities saw continued capital flight to safe havens as Phase 3 combat operations extended into Day 90 without a diplomatic off-ramp. Operation Sea Shield has stabilized some shipping lanes but costs remain inflated.

hormuz_insurance_premiums.execSummary: War risk premiums for vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz have seen a localized spike following the maritime intercepts on May 27.

kpis.transit_volume: Stable but delayed by convoy grouping

maritime_insurance.execSummary: Following the May 04 resumption of hostilities, war risk premiums for Gulf shipping have surged exponentially. The establishment of the Strait Interdiction Zone has deterred massive transit volumes, squeezing global energy lifelines.
kpis: War Risk Premium: 2.75% of hull value
maritime_insurance.appendDetailedSection: Lloyd's Market Association has widened the Listed Areas in the Gulf, prompting several major carriers to invoke force majeure. Intelligence from UKMTO and Ambrey indicates targeted harassment of UAE and Saudi flagged vessels.

a.title: Iran's Remaining Offensive Capacity
a.execSummary: Iran entered the war with ~2,500 ballistic missiles and ~400–480 launchers. By Day 34, CENTCOM has struck 10,000+ targets; IDF 60-70% of pre-war targets destroyed. Iran sustains 5-20 BMs daily plus drone swarms. Hormuz remains effectively closed. IRGCN asymmetric capability (170 fast craft, ~6,000 mines) is 80-90% intact. Drone production (~10,000/month) enabled by Chinese weapon transfers via Russia/Turkmenistan/Pakistan. Beidou nav receivers confirmed for Shahed drones. IRGC shifted to economic targeting (tech companies).
keyFindings: Iran's conventional navy is 'combat ineffective' with 60+ ships sunk, but IRGCN asymmetric capability (170 fast craft, ~6,000 mines, drone boats) is 80–90% intact — making Hormuz disruption sustainable for months.
The Day 17–19 uptick in missile fire with cluster munitions contradicts simple depletion narratives — Iran is managing inventory deliberately, not running out.
Khamenei's assassination triggered IRGC seizure of state control via Mojtaba Khamenei; Iran explicitly rejected ceasefire talks, signaling intent for prolonged attrition.

detailedSections.heading: Ballistic Missile Inventory
detailedSections.content: Pre-war: ~2,500 missiles, ~400-480 launchers. By Day 15: ~150 active launchers, 70%+ destroyed. ~1,412 BMs fired through Day 19. Iran reluctant to bring launchers out of tunnels due to airstrike risk.

detailedSections.heading: Drone Capacity Underestimated
detailedSections.content: ~3,277 drones launched by Day 19. Production capacity ~10,000/month. War on the Rocks: 83% decline measures tempo, not capacity. Dispersed manufacturing generates few signatures. Iran may be stockpiling for coordinated attacks.

detailedSections.heading: Naval Asymmetric Threat
detailedSections.content: 60+ ships sunk; conventional navy 'combat ineffective.' But IRGCN retains 80-90% of fast attack craft (~170 vessels), ~6,000 mines, drone boats. Iran began mining Strait in Week 2. Could disrupt Hormuz with drones alone 'for months.'

detailedSections.heading: Depletion Curve Adaptation
detailedSections.content: Missile fire declined ~90% from Day 1 peak of 428. But Day 17-21 uptick with cluster munitions achieved highest hit rate. Iran managing inventory deliberately — fewer missiles, higher yield. Consistent with attrition strategy.

detailedSections.heading: Command Decapitation and IRGC Control
detailedSections.content: Khamenei killed Feb 28. Mojtaba elected March 9 under IRGC pressure. 500+ C2 sites struck. Personnel operating from tents. But Russia/China ISR partially compensating for degraded communications.

b.title: US Offensive Sustainability — Operation Epic Fury
b.execSummary: By Day 34, CENTCOM confirms 10,000+ US targets struck (IDF thousands more). 850+ Tomahawks fired (surpassing Iraq 2003). 57,000+ troops in theater. IDF hit Tehran Revolutionary Court, 10 MoI sites, Quds Force HQ. Strikes in 26 of 31 provinces. Trump signals exit in 2-3 weeks but IDF plans 3+ more weeks. UK hosts Hormuz diplomatic conference. April 6 deadline 4 days away. Cost: $500M/day with $200B supplemental requested.
keyFindings: The Navy fired ~400 Tomahawks in 72 hours (~10% of ready inventory), replacement 'could take years' — but rapid transition to JDAMs by Day 5 mitigated the standoff munitions constraint.
The USS Gerald R. Ford is at ~9 months at sea (nearing breaking point) with no replacement carrier in theater — the structural carrier rotation gap is the Navy's most acute sustainability risk.
Iran's strategy of targeting tankers is deliberate and effective: one KC-135 shot down, 5 more damaged on the ground, removing 6 from service in the campaign's most critical logistics bottleneck.

detailedSections.heading: Force Disposition
detailedSections.content: 2 carrier strike groups (Lincoln, Ford) plus 30+ warships. ~200 fighters across 8 bases. Ford at ~9 months at sea — nearing breaking point. Bush completing workups but not in theater. F-22s deployed to Israel for first time.

detailedSections.heading: Munitions Transition
detailedSections.content: ~400 Tomahawks in first 72 hours (~10% of inventory). By Day 14, >90% were low-cost JDAMs. Hegseth claimed 'nearly unlimited' stockpile — assessed credible given 30,000+ annual kit purchases. Boeing holds $7.5B contract.

detailedSections.heading: Cost Trajectory
detailedSections.content: $11.3B in first 6 days. ~$500M/day ongoing. Pentagon requesting $200B supplemental. Penn Wharton projects $40-95B for 2-month conflict. Israel spending ~$410M/day; IAF dropped 12,000 bombs in 18 days.

detailedSections.heading: Critical Constraints
detailedSections.content: KC-135 shot down over Iraq (6 crew KIA), 5 damaged at Prince Sultan. THAAD gap through April 2027. Ford needs replacement. 60-day War Powers deadline ~April 29. Only 25% public support for strikes.

detailedSections.heading: Campaign Phase Progression
detailedSections.content: Phase 1 (D1-4): Air defense, C2, nuclear sites. Phase 2 (D5-12): Industrial base, drone factories, naval. Phase 3 (D12+): Kharg Island, Hormuz sites, energy. 7,000+ targets struck; 6,000+ sorties.

c.title: Defender Interceptor Depletion
c.execSummary: The interceptor depletion problem is arguably the single most consequential variable in the war. Iran's offensive missiles cost $100K–$11.5M; interceptors cost $3.7M–$28M. Gulf states burned ~800 PAC-3 interceptors in three days — more than Ukraine used in four years. By March 14, Israel informed the US it was 'critically low' on ballistic interceptors, with no new THAAD deliveries until April 2027.
keyFindings: Core asymmetry is existential: Iran expends $20K–$500K weapons to force $3.7M–$28M interceptor expenditure — cost-exchange ratios of 10:1 to 370:1 in Iran's favor, making exhaustion the deliberate strategy.
The THAAD delivery gap through April 2027 is immovable — 100 interceptors in procurement backlog, production line diverted to foreign orders, factory expansion won't produce until 2028–2030.
Crisis deferred (not resolved) by destroying 70% of launchers; if mobile launchers are reconstituted, defender stocks would be critically insufficient.

detailedSections.heading: US THAAD Inventory Crisis
detailedSections.content: Pre-war: 534 interceptors; 100-150+ used in June 2025. No new deliveries until April 2027. THAAD radar destroyed in Jordan ($484M loss). South Korea battery being redeployed to Middle East.

detailedSections.heading: Gulf States Burning Through Interceptors
detailedSections.content: ~800 PAC-3 interceptors fired in first 72 hours — more than Ukraine used in 4 years. Gulf states now 'selective about which projectiles to target.' Former US official: 'We have shot several years of production.'

detailedSections.heading: Cost Asymmetry
detailedSections.content: Iran Shahed drone costs $20-50K; US Patriot interceptor $3.7M (ratio up to 370:1). SM-3 Block IIA costs $28M vs Iranian Emad at ~$250K. Iran's strategy is exhaustion, not destruction.

detailedSections.heading: Depletion Curve Scenarios
detailedSections.content: Grieco (Stimson): at Twelve-Day War rate, half of entire US interceptor stockpile depleted in 4-5 weeks. Crisis averted because Iranian launch rates collapsed 90% due to TEL attrition. But structural gap measured in years.

detailedSections.heading: Production Lag
detailedSections.content: THAAD: 96/year, expanding to 400/year over 7-year ramp (first impact ~2028-2030). Patriot PAC-3: 620/year, targeting 2,000 by 2030-32. Lead time 1.5-2.5 years per order. Lockheed investing $2B+ across 20+ facilities.

d.title: Iran Strategic Calculus & Endgame Scenarios
d.execSummary: By Day 34, Trump signals US exit in 2-3 weeks even if Hormuz remains closed. UK hosts international Hormuz conference — Iran FM: 'trust at zero.' April 6 deadline 4 days away. Brent ~$116/bbl (slight ease on ceasefire rumors). IEA warns April supply losses will double March. China confirmed supplying weapons to Iran via Russia/Turkmenistan/Pakistan. CENTCOM: 10,000+ targets struck. IDF plans 3 more weeks — potential US-Israel divergence. War Powers deadline April 28.
keyFindings: US objectives have shifted from nuclear to missiles to regime change and back, with 'unconditional surrender' serving as rhetorical maximum — Hegseth's March 19 removal of any timeline suggests the administration is no longer managing expectations around a near-term exit.

Iran's nuclear calculus has fundamentally changed: with ~75% of known nuclear infrastructure struck, the question shifts from 'can Iran build a bomb' to 'can a successor regime reconstruct under occupation-level surveillance.'

The War Powers Resolution 60-day clock (April 28) is the hardest political constraint — if Congress does not authorize force, Trump faces a constitutional confrontation that could limit escalation options regardless of military success.

detailedSections.heading: Ceasefire Dynamics
detailedSections.content: Trump demanded 'unconditional surrender' March 6. Rejected Egypt-Oman mediation. Iran's Araghchi: 'We never asked for a ceasefire.' Iran demands end on all fronts including Lebanon. Hegseth removed all timelines March 19.

detailedSections.heading: Iran Strategic Calculus
detailedSections.content: CSIS: Iran's strategy is 'Don't Calibrate — Escalate.' Core mechanisms: interceptor exhaustion, Gulf economic coercion, Hormuz leverage (~20% of global oil), US domestic pressure (56% opposition). Mojtaba Khamenei: 'Hormuz leverage must continue.'

detailedSections.heading: Nuclear Status Post-Strikes
detailedSections.content: Trump announced strikes on Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan. IAEA confirmed Natanz damage. ~200kg of 60% uranium remains intact underground — enough for ~5 warheads if enriched to 90%. Breakout timeline moved from zero-day to 8-15 year reconstitution.

detailedSections.heading: Five Endgame Scenarios
detailedSections.content: A: Quick ceasefire (15-25%). B: Grinding degradation (40-50%, most likely). C: Exponential escalation with Houthi activation (20-25%). D: Regime change (38% by year-end per Polymarket). E: Frozen stalemate.

detailedSections.heading: Gulf States Strategic Dilemma
detailedSections.content: GCC condemned Iran but refused to enter war directly. Carnegie: attacks pushing Gulf toward 'total dependence on US security guarantees.' US bases now seen as 'liability rather than asset.' Gulf states urging US not to stop short.

Maritime Security.execSummary: Increased drone activity intercepted off the eastern coast on Apr 8, leading to temporary rerouting of commercial vessels.

tactical_shift_phase3.execSummary: Transition to maritime exclusion and deep-strike counter-battery fire observed starting Apr 15.
tactical_shift_phase3.appendDetailedSection: Operations on Apr 16 marked a shift towards enforcing the Strait of Hormuz Exclusion Zone and neutralizing launch sites identified during the Apr 15 barrages.

diplomacy_update.execSummary: Islamabad Peace Talks began on Apr 10, mediated by Pakistan. U.S. delegation led by VP JD Vance. A fragile two-week ceasefire, known as the 'Two Generals Framework', is in effect.
diplomacy_update.appendDetailedSection: Despite the truce, violations occurred. Israel conducted over 100 airstrikes in Lebanon on Apr 08 and Apr 09. Kuwait reported drone attacks on Apr 09. Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement on the 40th day of the conflict, asserting Iran's rights.

naval_posture.execSummary: Naval and air forces shifted to forward screen on Apr 09.

DIPLOMACY.execSummary: Face-to-face peace negotiations commenced in Islamabad on Apr 11 between U.S. and Iranian delegations, mediated by Pakistan.

tactical_shift_apr.execSummary: Adversary forces have shifted to employing jet-powered loitering munitions in Phase 3 operations.
tactical_shift_apr.appendDetailedSection: The introduction of the Shahed-238B on Apr 17 indicates a tactical adjustment aimed at exploiting gaps in point-defense systems.

defense_update.execSummary: As of Apr 14, GCC nations are actively enhancing joint defense protocols under Operation Swift Shield to counter persistent aerial threats.

adv_day46.execSummary: Increased ballistic missile activity observed on Apr 14 across the Gulf, with new missile variants introduced.

islamabad_peace_talks.execSummary: A fragile ceasefire brokered by Pakistan on Apr 7 holds, with active large-scale bombings subsiding. A second round of peace talks is scheduled in Islamabad between U.S. and Iranian teams on Apr 15.

ADV-APR15.execSummary: Adversary forces introduced upgraded rocket variants on Apr 14.

defense_network_apr16.execSummary: On Apr 16, an integrated early warning framework became fully operational to counter low-altitude incursions.

defense_posture.execSummary: Regional defense posture enhanced on Apr 16 with stratospheric early warning integrations.
defense_posture.appendDetailedSection: As of Apr 16, GCC joint command has established a new datalink protocol integrating SAR assets.

STRIKE_SUMMARY.execSummary: Phase 3 missile exchanges have intensified, with Iran deploying the Fattah-3 variant on Apr 16. GCC integrated air defenses maintained a high interception rate.

adv_munitions_apr18.execSummary: New loitering munition variants deployed as of Apr 18.
adv_munitions_apr18.appendDetailedSection: Debris analysis from the Apr 17 engagements confirms the introduction of the Shahed-136B variant.

naval_blockade_apr18.execSummary: U.S. naval blockade implemented against Iranian ports on Apr 18. Iran retaliated by re-closing the Strait of Hormuz.
naval_blockade_apr18.appendDetailedSection: Following the failure of peace negotiations in Islamabad on Apr 13, a naval blockade came into effect on Apr 18. Iran responded immediately by closing the Strait of Hormuz, escalating regional maritime risks.

Day51_Strategic_Update.execSummary: Introduction of the Khaibar-X missile system on Apr 18 signals a shift in deep-strike capabilities.
Day51_Strategic_Update.appendDetailedSection: The Khaibar-X variant was first tracked entering UAE airspace on Apr 18 before being intercepted.

mil_intel.execSummary: As of Apr 19, allied forces note the introduction of extended-range munitions by Iranian forces, prompting shifts in GCC air defense posture.
mil_intel.appendDetailedSection: New telemetry data from the Apr 17 attacks indicates the use of the Zolfaghar-E variant.

TACTICS_UPDATE.execSummary: Adversary forces have increased the use of synchronized low-altitude UAV swarms intended to saturate coastal radar networks.
TACTICS_UPDATE.appendDetailedSection: Recent data from Apr 19 indicates a shift toward nocturnal, multi-vector drone approaches designed to minimize early warning windows.

ceasefire_status.execSummary: As of Apr 21, high-level negotiations are ongoing in Islamabad to prevent the collapse of the two-week ceasefire set to expire on Apr 22. Iran has demanded $270 billion in reparations.
ceasefire_status.appendDetailedSection: Negotiations face significant hurdles due to Iran's $270 billion reparation demand from Gulf nations (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, Jordan).

naval_operations.execSummary: Increased naval coordination among GCC states observed on Apr 21.
naval_operations.appendDetailedSection: New joint patrols in the Strait of Hormuz have commenced to secure shipping lanes.

tactical_shift_late_april.execSummary: Shift observed toward combined drone and hypersonic attacks on coastal infrastructure.

maritime_domain.execSummary: Naval operations intensified around the Strait of Hormuz on Apr 21, with GCC forces establishing exclusion zones.
maritime_domain.appendDetailedSection: New exclusion zones declared on Apr 21 have severely restricted shipping lanes, prompting alternative routing via overland pipelines where feasible.

ceasefire_stalemate.execSummary: On Apr 21, the U.S. announced an extension of the two-week truce for talks in Islamabad, but Iran refuses to negotiate while the U.S. naval blockade ('Operation Economic Fury') continues.

phase3_overview.execSummary: Hostilities resumed on May 4 (Day 66), ending the temporary ceasefire. Oman reported a unique ceasefire violation on May 6.
phase3_overview.appendDetailedSection: Day 68 saw a localized incident in Oman, which was previously a non-participant. This expands the theater of operations slightly, though the main focus remains on UAE, Qatar, and Bahrain.

Oman_D68.execSummary: A ceasefire violation on May 6 shifted the conflict phase back to ACTIVE.
Oman_D68.appendDetailedSection: Oman recorded an anomalous interception event on Day 68, formally breaking the regional pause.

jadc_integration_may26.execSummary: The operationalization of the Joint Air Defense Command (JADC) has reduced airspace deconfliction errors across GCC states by an estimated 40%.
jadc_integration_may26.appendDetailedSection: Saudi and Emirati radar network synchronization has effectively closed the low-altitude gaps previously exploited by Shahed-136 variants during Phase 1.

Phase3_Air_Defense.execSummary: The introduction of hypersonic glide vehicles and shadow swarm tactics by Iran has stressed existing regional interceptors, slightly lowering overall interception rates in May.
Phase3_Air_Defense.appendDetailedSection: Since May 15, the GCC integrated air defense network has adjusted tracking algorithms to better detect Fattah-2 HGV profiles and EW-shielded drone swarms.

adversary_strategy.execSummary: Shift towards targeting strategic maritime chokepoints and enhanced air defense postures observed since mid-May.
adversary_strategy.appendDetailedSection: Adversary forces have increased SAM network density along the coast, indicating an anti-access/area denial (A2/AD) strategy prioritization.

phase3_maritime_shift.execSummary: Since May 23, kinetic activity has shifted noticeably toward asymmetric maritime threats. USVs and low-altitude drone swarms are increasingly testing UAE and Omani territorial waters in the Gulf of Oman.
phase3_maritime_shift.appendDetailedSection: The introduction of the Shahed-136B variant has stretched the engagement envelopes of coastal defense batteries. Coalition naval assets have established a permanent patrol screen under Operation Nimble Guard to mitigate threats to commercial shipping.

air_defense_shift.execSummary: Transition to integrated tracking architectures improved regional intercept rates since D70.
air_defense_shift.appendDetailedSection: Recent analysis during Phase 3 indicates the operationalization of the Gulf Integrated Air Defense Network (GIADN) has led to a noticeable increase in successful early-warning interceptions between D70 and D90, allowing cross-border cueing of Patriot and THAAD batteries.

TACTICAL_SHIFTS.appendDetailedSection: Since May 18, Phase 3 operations have demonstrated a shift towards decentralized, multi-vector saturation attacks. The formal activation of the JADS-G framework has slightly improved GCC interception efficiency, though Iranian forces have introduced Zulfiqar-3 missiles to deliberately stress terminal defense systems across the Arabian Peninsula.

strategic_overview.execSummary: Phase 3 operations are marked by sophisticated combined-arms strikes intended to bypass integrated GCC air and missile defense networks.
strategic_overview.appendDetailedSection: On May 28, joint GCC defense grids successfully demonstrated integrated capabilities, pooling telemetry data to intercept hypersonic threats, though at a significantly higher cost per interception.

Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Ruwais, Fujairah, Al Minhad
Riyadh, PSAB, Ras Tanura, Yanbu, Shaybah
Kuwait City, Ali Al Salem, Camp Arifjan
Strategic sites — cruise missiles, large rockets
Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Al Dhafra AB, Barakah NPP
Riyadh, Dhahran, Jubail, Yanbu, Ras Tanura

UAE-1.rawClaim: MoD cumulative issued Mar 1: B=132 intercepted/137 detected, U=195/209. Deaths: 1 Pakistani (debris nr Zayed Airport).
UAE-1.derivation: Day 1 = opening baseline; no prior day to subtract. Detected/intercepted split from MoD territory/sea breakdown.

UAE-2.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=165,C=2,U=541. Daily delta: B=33,C=2,U=346.
UAE-2.derivation: ΔB=165-132=33, ΔC=2-0=2, ΔU=541-195=346. Deaths cumulative 3 (Pakistani, Nepali, Bangladeshi).

UAE-3.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=174,C=8,U=689. 9 drones fell on territory.
UAE-3.derivation: ΔB=9, ΔC=6, ΔU=148 from cumulative delta.

UAE-4.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=186,C=8,U=812. FIRST BM to land on UAE territory.

UAE-5.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=189,C=8,U=941. Lowest daily BM (3).

UAE-7.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=205,C=8,U=1184. All 9 BM destroyed.

UAE-8.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=221,C=8,U=1305. BM surge — 16 det highest since Day 2.

UAE-9.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=238,C=8,U=1422. Highest daily BM (17 det).

UAE-10.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=253,C=8,U=1440. SHARP drone drop (18 from 117).
UAE-10.derivation: ΔB=9 (int), ΔU=35 det / 26 int. Leakers: 9 drones penetrated (25.7% fail rate). DISPUTE: Wikipedia attributes 141 injuries to this day — actually Mar 14 per MoD text. KT reports 117 injuries vs MoD 122.

UAE-11.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=262,C=8,U=1475. WORST DRONE PENETRATION: 9 of 35 got through.
UAE-11.derivation: ΔB=9, ΔU=35 det / 26 int. Deaths reach 6.

UAE-12.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=268,C=15,U=1514. CRUISE MISSILE ESCALATION — 7 new CM.
UAE-12.derivation: ΔC=7 (first since Day 3). Signals new weapon deployment.

UAE-15.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=294,C=15,U=1600. Fujairah bunkering hub fire.

UAE-16.rawClaim: MoD: cum B=298,C=15,U=1606. LOWEST DAILY VOLUME (10 total).
UAE-16.derivation: ΔB=4, ΔU=6. Capability degradation signal confirmed.

Qatar-1.rawClaim: MoD/MoI joint presser (issued Mar 1, covers Feb 28): 65 BM + 11 drones + 2 aircraft intercepted. 2 BM struck Al Udeid.
Qatar-1.derivation: Feb 28 vs Mar 1 timing ambiguity: strikes began late Feb 28, presser issued Mar 1 covering first 24h. Treated as Day 1.

Qatar-3.rawClaim: 7 BM + 5 drones + 2 Su-24 bombers ALL intercepted. Qatar F-15QAs shot down 2 Iranian Su-24MK.
Qatar-3.derivation: First QEAF air-to-air kill. QatarEnergy declared Force Majeure.

Qatar-10.rawClaim: CUM AS OF MAR 10: 151 missiles, 69 drones, 2 aircraft targeted. 143M/53D/2A intercepted.
Qatar-10.derivation: Most comprehensive Qatar total. No updated cumulative through Mar 16 — true total substantially higher.

Bahrain-1.rawClaim: BDF: ~45 missiles, ~9 drones intercepted. US 5th Fleet HQ Juffair DIRECTLY STRUCK.
Bahrain-1.derivation: DISPUTE: AP/Stars&Stripes (Mar 5) reported 75M intercepted (65 destroyed), 124D (88 downed, 36 LANDED). BDF uses 'intercepted and destroyed' — true penetration rate higher than BDF implies.

Bahrain-9.rawClaim: SITRA ATTACK: 32 civilians injured incl. 17yo girl, 2-month-old infant. BAPCO Force Majeure.

Saudi-3.rawClaim: Ras Tanura Refinery struck by drone → fire at 7:04am. 550k bpd shut.
Saudi-3.derivation: No Saudi cumulative issued — incident-based. Fragmented bulletins with overlap risk.

Kuwait-2.rawClaim: 6 US SOLDIERS KILLED at Port Shuaiba (drone struck TOC). Cum ~97 BM + 283 drones intercepted.
Kuwait-2.derivation: Day 2 Kuwait bulletin covers ~36h. Breaking Defense '97BM/283D' consistent with later FDD tallies.

Kuwait-9.rawClaim: Drone struck 2 KAFCO fuel tanks inside Kuwait International Airport; large fire, no casualties. Source: Arab Times citing DGCA via KUNA.
Kuwait-9.derivation: Date confirmed as Mar 8 (Day 9). Treated as U-High: Arab Times cites official aviation authority DGCA.

Kuwait-10.rawClaim: Two drones caused explosions in fuel tanks near Kuwait International Airport. Another drone struck Public Institution for Social Security building — material damage.
Kuwait-10.derivation: Single source (Arab Times). Treated as U-Med. Consistent with broader Kuwait attack pattern.

Oman-2.rawClaim: Port of Duqm: drone hit mobile worker housing unit injuring 1 expatriate; 2nd drone intercepted (debris near fuel tanks). Near Khasab: tanker Skylight 4 crew injured, 20 evacuated.
Oman-2.derivation: Muscat Daily local reporting. Two separate incidents. Treated as U-High for Duqm (worker name/employer cited); U-Med for Khasab (MSC relay).

Oman-3.rawClaim: Drone-boat strike on tanker MKD VYOM off Muscat killed 1 crew member; 21 crew evacuated. Duqm: fuel tank struck, no casualties. Salalah: drone fell near port, no losses.
Oman-3.derivation: MKD VYOM fatality: Muscat Daily confirmed, cross-referenced with MSC/maritime reporting. Duqm fuel tank: Muscat Daily. Salalah fall: Times of Oman.

Oman-12.rawClaim: Several drones were downed while others struck fuel tanks at Salalah Port. No human casualties recorded. Several tanks damaged. Source: ONA via Times of Oman.
Oman-12.derivation: ONA official relay confirms damage but not specific count. AP satellite imagery corroborates long-burning fire at Salalah. Treated as High.

Iraq-8.rawClaim: Rockets hit Green Zone, including US Embassy area Baghdad. First Green Zone hit since war began. Several drones intercepted near Baghdad airport.
Iraq-8.derivation: Al Jazeera single-source. Security official quote. Treated as U-Med — no official Iraqi statement found for this specific incident.

Iraq-10.rawClaim: Drone attack targeting vicinity of Camp Victoria inside Baghdad International Airport intercepted. No casualties or damage immediately reported.
Iraq-10.derivation: Shafaq News, Kurdish-Iraqi outlet. U-High: named location, specific incident, no contradicting reports.

Iraq-11.rawClaim: One of six drones struck Baghdad Diplomatic Support Center near Baghdad airport, near a guard tower. Material damage, no casualties.
Iraq-11.derivation: Iraqi News/Reuters relay. Specific target named. Treated as U-High: two independent outlets confirm incident.

Iraq-19.rawClaim: Renewed drone and rocket attacks on US Embassy Baghdad and diplomatic facility near Baghdad airport. No casualties or damage immediately reported. Sources: Reuters.

UAE-22.rawClaim: On Apr 21, UAE MoD reported successful interception of three incoming ballistic missiles over Abu Dhabi.
UAE-22.derivation: Direct official statement cross-referenced with regional OSINT.

KHARG-22.rawClaim: US considering blockade/occupation of Kharg Island to force Strait of Hormuz reopening
KHARG-22.derivation: Trump administration exploring military seizure of Iran's main oil export terminal

DIMONA-22.rawClaim: Iranian missiles struck Israeli nuclear research center vicinity in Dimona/Arad, injuring 100+ people
DIMONA-22.derivation: First targeting of Israeli nuclear facilities during Iran war

corroborate: <cite index="9-4,9-5">Iran vows retaliation for power plant strikes</cite>
IRN-22.rawClaim: Trump threatens to 'hit and obliterate' Iran's power plants if Tehran doesn't open Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours
IRN-22.derivation: Multiple sources confirm Trump's ultimatum on Strait of Hormuz reopening

IRN-23.rawClaim: Iran's National Defence Council says any attempt to attack Iranian coast or islands will cause 'all communication lines in the Persian Gulf to be mined'
IRN-23.derivation: Direct statement from Iran's National Defence Council as Trump's deadline expires

IRN-24.rawClaim: Iran demonstrates ICBM capability with Diego Garcia strike attempt

USA-22.rawClaim: US temporarily lifts sanctions on Iranian oil already loaded on ships, expected to add 140 million barrels to global market
USA-22.derivation: Treasury Secretary Bessent announcement of temporary sanctions relief

QATAR-22.rawClaim: Iranian strikes damaged 17% of Qatar's LNG export capacity, forcing 3-5 year repair timeline
QATAR-22.derivation: Direct statements from QatarEnergy CEO Saad al-Kaabi to Reuters confirming damage to LNG Trains 4 and 6

LUCAS-23.rawClaim: LUCAS drones made combat debut in Operation Epic Fury as reverse-engineered Shahed-136 variants
LUCAS-23.derivation: Official CENTCOM announcement and video release confirming first combat use

PRSM-23.rawClaim: Precision Strike Missile made combat debut against Iran with 500km+ range capability
PRSM-23.derivation: Official CENTCOM confirmation and footage showing PrSM launch from HIMARS

HORMUZ-25.rawClaim: UK Maritime Trade Operations reports 21 confirmed attacks on commercial vessels since March 1, with threat level remaining 'critical'
HORMUZ-25.derivation: Official UKMTO assessment of maritime security situation

SANCTIONS-24.rawClaim: Trump administration temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil already loaded on ships until April 19, 2026, adding ~140 million barrels to market
SANCTIONS-24.derivation: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announcement and multiple confirmatory sources

IRAN-LEADERSHIP-25.rawClaim: Mojtaba Khamenei elected March 8, 2026 as new Iranian Supreme Leader, replacing his father Ali Khamenei
IRAN-LEADERSHIP-25.derivation: Iranian constitutional process following leadership succession

primary: Executive Order 110 signed by President Marcos
corroborate: Energy Secretary warns of 45-day fuel supply
First country to declare energy emergency due to Iran war
PHL-24.rawClaim: Philippines becomes first country to declare national energy emergency due to Iran war, citing imminent danger to energy supply
PHL-24.derivation: Official government announcements and energy ministry statements

USA-25.rawClaim: On March 24, the Trump administration presented a 15-point peace proposal to Tehran via Pakistan. President Trump extended the pause on striking Iranian energy infrastructure to April 6, 2026, from an initial March 27 deadline.
USA-25.derivation: Reported by the Institute for the Study of War and major news outlets.

IRN-25.rawClaim: On March 24, a projectile landed at the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant. Russia’s Rosatom evacuated 163 technicians, though no damage was reported.
IRN-25.derivation: Report from the Institute for the Study of War.

IRN-28.rawClaim: On March 27, the IDF and U.S. forces conducted wide-scale strikes in the heart of Tehran, targeting military industries and missile launch sites. Significant strikes also hit the Arak heavy water reactor and the Ardakan uranium processing facility in Yazd Province.
IRN-28.derivation: Official IDF statements and reports from The Guardian, corroborated by the Institute for the Study of War.

IRN-21.rawClaim: On March 18, Israeli strikes at Bandar Anzali Port (Gilan Province) reportedly 'shut down' a critical supply line between Iran and Russia.
IRN-21.derivation: Report from the Institute for the Study of War.

IRN-26.rawClaim: Between March 25–26, the combined US-Israel force conducted its northeastern-most strikes to date around Mashhad. On March 26, the IDF confirmed the assassination of IRGC Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri in a targeted strike in Bandar Abbas.
IRN-26.derivation: Report from the Institute for the Study of War, referencing IDF confirmation.

UAE-27.rawClaim: The UAE Ministry of Defense reported intercepting 15 missiles and 11 drones on March 26 alone.
UAE-27.derivation: Reported by CBS News, with broader context from Al Jazeera and The Guardian.

IRN-27_diplomacy.rawClaim: Iran has reportedly described the U.S. proposal as 'one-sided' and issued a 5-point counter-proposal that includes demands for war reparations.
IRN-27_diplomacy.derivation: Reported by financial news outlets covering diplomatic developments.

USA-28_diplomacy.rawClaim: On March 27, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio, meeting with G7 ministers in France, stated that the U.S. expects the operation to conclude in 'weeks, not months.'
USA-28_diplomacy.derivation: News reports quoting the U.S. Secretary of State.

GLOBAL-28_humanitarian.rawClaim: As of March 27, the International Federation of Red Cross (IFRC) reports over 1,900 killed and 20,000 injured in Iran. U.S. Central Command reported 13 U.S. troops killed and over 300 wounded since the start of the war. Approximately 3 million Iranians have been displaced, and in Lebanon, UNICEF reports over 370,000 children displaced.
GLOBAL-28_humanitarian.derivation: Reports from UN agencies (IFRC/UNICEF) and U.S. Central Command, covered by The Guardian.

GLOBAL-28_economy.rawClaim: The war has caused significant volatility in oil prices and the worst one-day drop in the U.S. stock market since the conflict began. The IMF and OECD have warned of rising global inflation directly tied to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
GLOBAL-28_economy.derivation: Reported by Reuters and Bloomberg on economic impacts.

UAE-31.rawClaim: On Mar 30, the UAE MoD reported the interception of three ballistic missiles over Abu Dhabi industrial zones.
UAE-31.derivation: Cross-referenced with independent radar tracking data.

QAT-29.rawClaim: On Mar 29, air raid sirens were activated in Doha following a detected drone flight path, though no impact occurred.
QAT-29.derivation: Confirmed by regional news networks and local social media alerts.

UAE-35.rawClaim: On Apr 17, UAE MoD reported successful interception of two incoming Shahed-136B loitering munitions.
UAE-35.derivation: Direct confirmation from MoD press statement, corroborated by The National.

UAE-33.rawClaim: On Apr 1, UAE air defenses successfully intercepted two high-altitude Shahed-149 drones approaching Fujairah.
UAE-33.derivation: Direct count compiled from the UAE MoD morning briefing on Apr 1.

UAE-34.rawClaim: On Apr 2, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of a maritime drone near the port of Fujairah.
UAE-34.derivation: MoD press release verified by local media coverage.

QATAR-04.rawClaim: On Apr 1, a cruise missile struck an oil tanker leased to QatarEnergy in Qatari waters.
QATAR-04.derivation: Cross-referenced maritime OSINT with official corporate energy statements regarding Strait of Hormuz disruptions.
QATAR-04.affects: Qatar maritime and economic damage assessments.

primary: Saudi Ministry of Defense Daily Briefing
KSA-12.rawClaim: On Apr 2, Saudi air defenses intercepted four incoming drones targeting the Eastern Province.
KSA-12.derivation: Extracted from the Apr 2 daily press briefing by Saudi defense officials and local regional reporting.

UAE-25.rawClaim: On Apr 03, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of multiple incoming aerial targets over the western region.
UAE-25.derivation: Official MoD statement and domestic news corroboration.

UAE-21.rawClaim: On Apr 19, UAE MoD reported the interception of three hostile drones targeting coastal infrastructure.
UAE-21.derivation: Direct official statement matched with local reporting.

KWT-04.rawClaim: On Apr 02, Kuwait Air Force intercepted three hostile UAVs approaching from the east.

UAE-45.rawClaim: On Apr 13, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of 2 ballistic missiles targeting Al Dhafra Air Base.
UAE-45.derivation: Direct statement from UAE MoD briefing on Day 45.

QAT-28.rawClaim: On Apr 19, Qatar MoD confirmed continuous air patrols following detected telemetry anomalies off the eastern coast.
QAT-28.derivation: Official press release verified via QNA distribution.

QAT-36.rawClaim: On Apr 04, regional observers noted increased aerial patrols over Doha following renewed threats.

UAE-37.rawClaim: On Apr 3, the UAE integrated new radar systems.

UAE-36.rawClaim: On Apr 04, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of 3 medium-range ballistic missiles targeting coastal infrastructure.
UAE-36.derivation: Matched official WAM and MoD press releases with Reuters secondary confirmation.

UAE-38.rawClaim: On Apr 6, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of 3 Khaibar-5 munitions targeting coastal energy facilities.
UAE-38.derivation: Cross-referenced official MoD statement with AFP and Reuters reporting.
UAE-38.affects: Total intercepted munitions count for UAE

KSA-40.rawClaim: On Apr 8, Pakistani and regional sources reported the reopening of the King Fahd Causeway following the Islamabad Accords ceasefire.
KSA-40.derivation: Diplomatic statements following the announcement of the 14-day truce.

UAE-40.rawClaim: On Apr 8, UAE MoD reported the interception of three maritime drones off the coast of Fujairah.

UAE-41.rawClaim: On Apr 9, UAE MoD reported the interception of three Fattah-2 missiles targeting Al Dhafra Air Base.
UAE-41.derivation: Direct statement from official MoD channels corroborated by local news.

UAE-42.rawClaim: On Apr 10, UAE MoD reported successful deployment of the Extended Coastal Screen.
UAE-42.derivation: Extracted from official defense ministry daily briefing.

KUWAIT-42.rawClaim: On Apr 10, Kuwaiti armed forces reported detecting and engaging seven Iranian drones in their airspace.
KUWAIT-42.derivation: ISW report of Kuwaiti Ministry of Defence intercepts.

QATAR-42.rawClaim: On Apr 09, Qatari officials reported intercepting 7 ballistic missiles and multiple drones.

SAUDI-42.rawClaim: On Apr 09, Saudi official sources confirmed that the East-West Crude Oil Pipeline was damaged in recent attacks.

SAU-43.rawClaim: On Apr 10, TotalEnergies confirmed the shutdown of the SATORP refinery in Jubail following damage during attacks on the night of Apr 7-8. A drone strike on the East-West Pipeline also reduced throughput.
SAU-43.derivation: Direct statements from TotalEnergies reported by financial and energy news outlets.

KWT-43.rawClaim: On Apr 10, Kuwait MoD reported engaging 7 hostile drones targeting vital facilities and National Guard sites between Apr 9 and Apr 10. Several National Guard members were injured.
KWT-43.derivation: Official statement by Kuwait MoD and MoFA.

UAE-44.rawClaim: On Apr 12, UAE MoD reported successfully neutralizing a drone swarm consisting of 12 Shahed-136 variants off the coast of Ras Al Khaimah.
UAE-44.derivation: Direct statement from UAE MoD integrated with independent tracking from Gulf News.

UAE-105.rawClaim: On Apr 12, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of three hostile aerial targets targeting unpopulated desert areas.
UAE-105.derivation: Direct translation of the official defense briefing.

KWT-44.rawClaim: On Apr 12, Kuwait MoD announced the interception of 3 UAVs over Boubyan Island.

QAT-45.rawClaim: On Apr 13, Qatar's interior ministry announced the discovery of washed-up debris from an intercepted ballistic missile on the eastern seaboard.
QAT-45.derivation: Official QNA press release detailing the recovery of missile fragments.

UAE-46.rawClaim: On Apr 13, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of 4 incoming drones over Abu Dhabi airspace, neutralizing the threat with no casualties.
UAE-46.derivation: Direct statement from UAE MoD, corroborated by local media reporting minimal disruption.

IRAN-UNSC-APR14.rawClaim: On Apr 14, Iran formally demanded that Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, and Jordan pay full compensation for damages.
IRAN-UNSC-APR14.derivation: Diplomatic letters submitted to UN Security Council

UAE-TOTALS-APR9.rawClaim: On Apr 9, the UAE Ministry of Defence reported intercepting a total of 537 ballistic missiles, 26 cruise missiles, and 2,256 drones since the conflict began.
UAE-TOTALS-APR9.derivation: Cumulative interception statistics published by UAE

BLOCKADE-01.rawClaim: On Apr 15, CENTCOM reported zero ships breached the naval blockade and 10 vessels were intercepted.
BLOCKADE-01.derivation: Direct statement from CENTCOM social media, corroborated by International News.

GCC-102.rawClaim: On Apr 16, UAE and allied forces launched a coordinated defense protocol across regional airspace.
GCC-102.derivation: MoD official statement with analytical corroboration from regional trackers.

UAE-48.rawClaim: On Apr 16, UAE MoD reported successful interception of a drone swarm targeting energy facilities.
UAE-48.derivation: Confirmed by MoD press release on Apr 16 and corroborated by Al Monitor.

UAE-102.rawClaim: On Apr 16, UAE MoD reported the interception of two ballistic missiles targeting Al Dhafra Air Base without casualties.
UAE-102.derivation: Extracted from MoD daily briefing and corroborated by Reuters on-the-ground reporting.
UAE-102.affects: Increases UAE Day 48 interception count by 2.

UAE-114.rawClaim: On Apr 16, UAE MoD reported successful interception of two Fattah-3 missiles targeting Al Dhafra, with minor debris falling on uninhabited areas.
UAE-114.derivation: Cross-referenced UAE MoD daily briefing on Apr 16 with Reuters situational update.
UAE-114.affects: Air defense intercept rates; Phase 3 infrastructure damage assessments.

QAT-52.rawClaim: On Apr 17, QNA reported maritime traffic resuming normal operations after a 12-hour precautionary halt due to naval skirmishes.
QAT-52.derivation: Official QNA statement corroborated by Al Jazeera port coverage.

IRN-50.rawClaim: On Apr 18, Iran announced the re-closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to a U.S. naval blockade.
IRN-50.derivation: Direct statements aggregated by OSINT and regional press.

UAE-50.rawClaim: On Apr 18, UAE MoD reported successful drone interception over the western seaboard.

UAE-92.rawClaim: On Apr 18, EPC reported that the Khaibar-X missile impacted an uninhabited desert region.
UAE-92.derivation: Satellite analysis and local field reports.

KWT-12.rawClaim: On Apr 19, Kuwait MoD confirmed minimal damage from a drone strike near Ali Al Salem Air Base.
KWT-12.derivation: Aggregated from MoD statements and local OSINT damage assessments.

UAE-52.rawClaim: On Apr 20, UAE announced the dismantling of a secret terrorist organization linked to Iran's IRGC planning sabotage operations.
UAE-52.derivation: Direct government announcement reported by regional media.

INTL-53.rawClaim: On Apr 21, Iran demanded $270 billion in reparations from Gulf nations, accusing them of complicity in US-Israeli strikes.
INTL-53.derivation: Diplomatic demands made public during the Islamabad talks.

UAE-104.rawClaim: On Apr 21, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of three ballistic missiles targeting Al Dhafra airbase.
UAE-104.derivation: Direct statement from official MoD briefing on Day 53.

QAT-088.rawClaim: On Apr 22, Qatari aviation authorities announced new civilian flight corridors due to unidentified drone activity in the northern Gulf.
QAT-088.derivation: Synthesized from QNA updates and regional aviation reporting on Day 54.

UAE-54.rawClaim: On Apr 22, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of four hostile UAVs targeting western maritime logistics hubs.
UAE-54.derivation: Direct official statement confirmed by local news coverage.

UAE-Day55.rawClaim: On Apr 23, total aerial attacks on UAE exceeded 2,800 missiles and drones since the war began.
UAE-Day55.derivation: Aggregate count from Long War Journal.

KSA-Day55.rawClaim: On Apr 23, total aerial attacks on Saudi Arabia reached approximately 680, with alternative export routes utilized via Port of Yanbu.
KSA-Day55.derivation: Aggregate count from Long War Journal.

QAT-Day55.rawClaim: On Apr 23, total incoming systems targeting Qatar recorded at 267, primarily at Al Udeid Air Base.
QAT-Day55.derivation: Aggregate count from Long War Journal.

HOR-Day54.rawClaim: On Apr 22, IRGC seized two container ships, Epaminondas and MSC Francesca, in the Strait of Hormuz.

Hormuz-Mines-Day55.rawClaim: On Apr 23, a leaked Pentagon assessment suggests clearing Iranian mines from the Strait of Hormuz could take up to six months.

UAE-66.rawClaim: On May 03, UAE MoD announced enhanced naval patrols in territorial waters.
UAE-66.derivation: Direct from official MoD announcement corroborated by regional news.

OMAN-68.rawClaim: On May 6, Omani defense forces reported an isolated airspace violation.
OMAN-68.derivation: Direct claim from Oman News Agency.

Oman-68.rawClaim: On May 6, Oman Daily Observer reported a localized breach of the ceasefire involving a drone interception.

KSA-75.rawClaim: On May 13, Saudi MoD reported interception of 5 UAVs over Dhahran. Tasnim claimed 8 drones hit critical infrastructure.
KSA-75.derivation: Aggregated cross-reference from SPA official release and Tasnim propaganda. Confirmed intercept count relies on SPA.

UAE-82.rawClaim: On May 20, UAE MoD reported 3 aerial targets intercepted over the Gulf of Oman near Fujairah.
UAE-82.derivation: MoD press release verified by Ambrey maritime security alert and corroborated by shipping deviations.

UAE-80.rawClaim: On May 18, UAE MoD reported intercepting 4 incoming ballistic missiles over Abu Dhabi, with debris falling in uninhabited desert.
UAE-80.derivation: Cross-referenced MoD statements with regional eyewitness accounts and US CENTCOM radar activity logs.

SAU-88.rawClaim: On May 26, Saudi MoD announced the successful interception of 3 hostile drones targeting eastern province desalination infrastructure.
SAU-88.derivation: Validated SPA official release against open-source satellite imagery showing no impact craters at the stated facility.

QATAR-85.rawClaim: On May 23, Qatari joint forces intercepted a drone swarm approaching Ras Laffan industrial city.
QATAR-85.derivation: Cross-referenced QNA official release with Al Jazeera timeline update.

UAE-89.rawClaim: On May 27, UAE MoD reported the interception of 2 unmanned surface vessels (USVs) and 3 loitering munitions targeting the approaches to Fujairah.
UAE-89.derivation: Direct statement released via MoD communications detailing Phase 3 maritime defense actions.

KSA-90.rawClaim: On May 28, Saudi MoD announced the successful destruction of 4 ballistic missiles over the Eastern Province, preventing damage to Jubail industrial infrastructure.
KSA-90.derivation: Aggregated from official SPA press releases and verified by regional defense intelligence feeds.

UAE-75.rawClaim: On May 13, UAE MoD reported 4 drones intercepted. IRNA claimed 6 drones hit military targets in Abu Dhabi. CENTCOM verified 4 intercepts but noted 1 additional drone crashed in an unpopulated desert area.
UAE-75.derivation: Logged 4 intercepts based on verified primary UAE MoD statements corroborated by CENTCOM. Disregarded IRNA claims of impact due to lack of verifiable OSINT damage.

QAT-81.rawClaim: On May 19, Qatar MoD reported no major incidents. UKMTO issued a warning of a drone detonating near a Qatari LNG carrier in international waters outside Doha's EEZ.
QAT-81.derivation: Treated as a maritime incident rather than a direct strike on Qatari soil. Strike count remains 0 for territorial QAT, but maritime risk noted.

UAE-85.rawClaim: On May 23, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of a mixed swarm of UAVs and SRBMs targeting western defense installations.
UAE-85.derivation: Aggregated from official daily statements and corroborated by US NAVCENT updates.

QAT-75.rawClaim: On May 13, Qatar MoD noted 2 UAVs intercepted offshore before reaching the mainland.
QAT-75.derivation: Confirmed by joint Qatari-US NAVCENT patrol statements.

KWT-79.rawClaim: On May 17, Kuwait MoD announced the successful interception of 1 drone targeting northern border infrastructure.
KWT-79.derivation: KUNA agency flash confirmed by Kuwait MoD; disputed by IRGC channels claiming a successful hit on infrastructure.

QAT-88.rawClaim: On May 26, the Qatari Ministry of Defense declared the downing of two loitering munitions off the eastern coastline.
QAT-88.derivation: Extracted from QNA briefing, aligned with independent reporting from Al Jazeera.

UAE-90.rawClaim: On May 28, UAE MoD reported the successful interception of a Zulfiqar-3 barrage aimed at Al Dhafra Air Base.
UAE-90.derivation: Official primary source confirmed by local media and independent radar tracking OSINT.

KWT-87.rawClaim: On May 25, Kuwait MoD reported 2 drones intercepted over Bubiyan Island following the breakdown of the ceasefire.
KWT-87.derivation: Official statement corroborated by regional news networks and joint GCC intel.

UAE-91.rawClaim: On May 29, UAE MoD reported successful interception of 3 Qader-type cruise missiles targeting offshore rigs.
UAE-91.derivation: Direct statement extracted and translated from MoD daily Phase 3 brief.
